<content>
1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Incremental Index
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: Deferred lexical-only indexing
5: // Mtime-based incremental indexing for fast re-indexing
6: // Node stdlib
7: import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';
8: import * as fs from 'fs';
9: import * as path from 'path';
10: 
11: // External packages
12: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
13: 
14: // Internal modules
15: import { getCanonicalPathKey } from '../utils/canonical-path.js';
16: import { extractSpecFolder, extractDocumentType } from '../parsing/memory-parser.js';
17: import { canonicalFingerprint } from './canonical-fingerprint.js';
18: import { createMemoStore } from './memo.js';
19: 
20: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
21:    1. CONSTANTS
22: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
23: 
24: const MTIME_FAST_PATH_MS = 1000; // Skip if mtime within 1 second
25: const DEFAULT_ORPHAN_SWEEP_LIMIT = 200;
26: 
27: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28:    2. INTERFACES
29: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
30: 
31: interface FileMetadata {
32:   path: string;
33:   mtime: number;
34:   size: number;
35:   exists: boolean;
36: }
37: 
38: interface StoredMetadata {
39:   file_path: string;
40:   canonical_file_path?: string | null;
41:   file_mtime_ms: number | null;
42:   content_hash: string | null;
43:   embedding_status: string;
44: }
45: 
46: type IndexDecision = 'skip' | 'reindex' | 'new' | 'deleted' | 'modified' | 'unknown';
47: 
48: interface CategorizedFiles {
49:   toIndex: string[];
50:   toUpdate: string[];
51:   toSkip: string[];
52:   toDelete: string[];
53: }
54: 
55: interface IndexedPathRow {
56:   file_path: string;
57:   canonical_file_path?: string | null;
58: }
59: 
60: interface IndexedRecordRow extends IndexedPathRow {
61:   id: number;
62: }
63: 
64: interface OrphanSweepOptions {
65:   limit?: number;
66:   cursor?: number;
67: }
68: 
69: interface OrphanSweepResult {
70:   swept: number;
71:   nextCursor: number | null;
72:   scannedRows: number;
73:   orphanRecordIds: number[];
74: }
75: 
76: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
77:    3. MODULE STATE
78: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
79: 
80: let db: Database.Database | null = null;
81: let canonicalPathColumnAvailable: boolean | null = null;
82: let documentTypeColumnAvailable: boolean | null = null;
83: 
84: function init(database: Database.Database): void {
85:   db = database;
86:   canonicalPathColumnAvailable = null;
87:   documentTypeColumnAvailable = null;
88: }
89: 
90: function hasCanonicalPathColumn(): boolean {
91:   if (!db) return false;
92: 
93:   if (canonicalPathColumnAvailable !== null) {
94:     return canonicalPathColumnAvailable;
95:   }
96: 
97:   try {
98:     const columns = (db.prepare('PRAGMA table_info(memory_index)').all() as Array<{ name: string }>);
99:     canonicalPathColumnAvailable = columns.some((column) => column.name === 'canonical_file_path');
100:   } catch {
101:     canonicalPathColumnAvailable = false;
102:   }
103: 
104:   return canonicalPathColumnAvailable;
105: }
106: 
107: function hasDocumentTypeColumn(): boolean {
108:   if (!db) return false;
109: 
110:   if (documentTypeColumnAvailable !== null) {
111:     return documentTypeColumnAvailable;
112:   }
113: 
114:   try {
115:     const columns = (db.prepare('PRAGMA table_info(memory_index)').all() as Array<{ name: string }>);
116:     documentTypeColumnAvailable = columns.some((column) => column.name === 'document_type');
117:   } catch {
118:     documentTypeColumnAvailable = false;
119:   }
120: 
121:   return documentTypeColumnAvailable;
122: }
123: 
124: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
125:    4. FILE METADATA
126: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
127: 
128: function getFileMetadata(filePath: string): FileMetadata {
129:   try {
130:     const stats = fs.statSync(filePath);
131:     return {
132:       path: filePath,
133:       mtime: stats.mtimeMs,
134:       size: stats.size,
135:       exists: true,
136:     };
137:   } catch {
138:     return {
139:       path: filePath,
140:       mtime: 0,
141:       size: 0,
142:       exists: false,
143:     };
144:   }
145: }
146: 
147: function getStoredMetadata(filePath: string): StoredMetadata | null {
148:   if (!db) return null;
149: 
150:   try {
151:     const canonicalPath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
152: 
153:     const row = hasCanonicalPathColumn()
154:       ? (db.prepare(`
155:           SELECT file_path, canonical_file_path, file_mtime_ms, content_hash, embedding_status
156:           FROM memory_index
157:           WHERE canonical_file_path = ? OR file_path = ?
158:           ORDER BY CASE WHEN canonical_file_path = ? THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, id DESC
159:           LIMIT 1
160:         `) as Database.Statement).get(canonicalPath, filePath, canonicalPath) as StoredMetadata | undefined
161:       : (db.prepare(`
162:           SELECT file_path, file_mtime_ms, content_hash, embedding_status
163:           FROM memory_index
164:           WHERE file_path = ?
165:         `) as Database.Statement).get(filePath) as StoredMetadata | undefined;
166: 
167:     return row || null;
168:   } catch (error: unknown) {
169:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
170:     console.warn(`[incremental-index] getStoredMetadata error: ${msg}`);
171:     return null;
172:   }
173: }
174: 
175: function computeFileContentHash(filePath: string): string | null {
176:   try {
177:     const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath);
178:     return createHash('sha256').update(content).digest('hex');
179:   } catch {
180:     return null;
181:   }
182: }
183: 
184: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
185:    5. DECISION LOGIC
186: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
187: 
188: /**
189:  * 6-path decision logic for whether a file needs re-indexing.
190:  */
191: function shouldReindex(filePath: string): IndexDecision {
192:   const fileInfo = getFileMetadata(filePath);
193: 
194:   // Path 1: File doesn't exist on disk
195:   if (!fileInfo.exists) {
196:     const stored = getStoredMetadata(filePath);
197:     if (stored) return 'deleted';
198:     return 'skip';
199:   }
200: 
201:   // Path 2: No stored metadata (new file)
202:   const stored = getStoredMetadata(filePath);
203:   if (!stored) return 'new';
204: 
205:   // Path 3: No stored mtime (legacy entry, needs re-indexing)
206:   if (stored.file_mtime_ms === null) return 'reindex';
207: 
208:   // Path 4: Mtime unchanged (fast path - skip)
209:   if (Math.abs(fileInfo.mtime - stored.file_mtime_ms) < MTIME_FAST_PATH_MS) {
210:     // Still check if embedding status is pending
211:     if (stored.embedding_status === 'pending' || stored.embedding_status === 'failed') {
212:       return 'reindex';
213:     }
214: 
215:     if (stored.content_hash) {
216:       const currentContentHash = computeFileContentHash(filePath);
217:       if (currentContentHash && currentContentHash !== stored.content_hash) {
218:         return 'modified';
219:       }
220:     }
221: 
222:     return 'skip';
223:   }
224: 
225:   // Path 5: Mtime changed (file modified)
226:   return 'modified';
227: }
228: 
229: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
230:    6. MTIME MANAGEMENT
231: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
232: 
233: function updateFileMtime(filePath: string, mtimeMs: number): boolean {
234:   if (!db) return false;
235: 
236:   try {
237:     const canonicalPath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
238: 
239:     const result = hasCanonicalPathColumn()
240:       ? (db.prepare(`
241:           UPDATE memory_index
242:           SET file_mtime_ms = ?
243:           WHERE canonical_file_path = ? OR file_path = ?
244:         `) as Database.Statement).run(mtimeMs, canonicalPath, filePath)
245:       : (db.prepare(`
246:           UPDATE memory_index
247:           SET file_mtime_ms = ?
248:           WHERE file_path = ?
249:         `) as Database.Statement).run(mtimeMs, filePath);
250: 
251:     return (result as { changes: number }).changes > 0;
252:   } catch (error: unknown) {
253:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
254:     console.warn(`[incremental-index] updateFileMtime error: ${msg}`);
255:     return false;
256:   }
257: }
258: 
259: function setIndexedMtime(filePath: string): boolean {
260:   const fileInfo = getFileMetadata(filePath);
261:   if (!fileInfo.exists) return false;
262:   return updateFileMtime(filePath, fileInfo.mtime);
263: }
264: 
265: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
266:    7. BATCH OPERATIONS
267: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
268: 
269: function categorizeFilesForIndexing(filePaths: string[]): CategorizedFiles {
270:   const result: CategorizedFiles = {
271:     toIndex: [],
272:     toUpdate: [],
273:     toSkip: [],
274:     toDelete: [],
275:   };
276: 
277:   for (const filePath of filePaths) {
278:     const decision = shouldReindex(filePath);
279: 
280:     switch (decision) {
281:       case 'new':
282:         result.toIndex.push(filePath);
283:         break;
284:       case 'modified':
285:       case 'reindex':
286:         result.toUpdate.push(filePath);
287:         break;
288:       case 'deleted':
289:         result.toDelete.push(filePath);
290:         break;
291:       case 'skip':
292:       default:
293:         result.toSkip.push(filePath);
294:         break;
295:     }
296:   }
297: 
298:   // Include stale indexed paths that are no longer discovered on disk.
299:   // Without this pass, removed files never enter toDelete during normal scans
300:   // Because discovery only returns files that currently exist.
301:   const staleIndexedPaths = listStaleIndexedPaths(filePaths);
302:   if (staleIndexedPaths.length > 0) {
303:     const seenDeleteKeys = new Set<string>(result.toDelete.map((filePath) => getCanonicalPathKey(filePath)));
304:     for (const stalePath of staleIndexedPaths) {
305:       const staleKey = getCanonicalPathKey(stalePath);
306:       if (!seenDeleteKeys.has(staleKey)) {
307:         result.toDelete.push(stalePath);
308:         seenDeleteKeys.add(staleKey);
309:       }
310:     }
311:   }
312: 
313:   return result;
314: }
315: 
316: function listStaleIndexedPaths(scannedFilePaths: string[]): string[] {
317:   if (!db) return [];
318: 
319:   const scannedCanonicalPaths = new Set(scannedFilePaths.map((filePath) => getCanonicalPathKey(filePath)));
320:   const stalePaths = new Set<string>();
321: 
322:   try {
323:     const rows = hasCanonicalPathColumn()
324:       ? (db.prepare(`
325:           SELECT DISTINCT file_path, canonical_file_path
326:           FROM memory_index
327:           WHERE file_path IS NOT NULL AND file_path != ''
328:         `) as Database.Statement).all() as IndexedPathRow[]
329:       : (db.prepare(`
330:           SELECT DISTINCT file_path
331:           FROM memory_index
332:           WHERE file_path IS NOT NULL AND file_path != ''
333:         `) as Database.Statement).all() as IndexedPathRow[];
334: 
335:     for (const row of rows) {
336:       if (!row || typeof row.file_path !== 'string' || row.file_path.length === 0) {
337:         continue;
338:       }
339: 
340:       const rowCanonicalPath = typeof row.canonical_file_path === 'string' && row.canonical_file_path.length > 0
341:         ? row.canonical_file_path
342:         : getCanonicalPathKey(row.file_path);
343: 
344:       if (scannedCanonicalPaths.has(rowCanonicalPath)) {
345:         continue;
346:       }
347: 
348:       const filePathExists = getFileMetadata(row.file_path).exists;
349:       const canonicalPathExists = typeof row.canonical_file_path === 'string' && row.canonical_file_path.length > 0
350:         ? getFileMetadata(row.canonical_file_path).exists
351:         : false;
352: 
353:       if (!filePathExists && !canonicalPathExists) {
354:         stalePaths.add(row.file_path);
355:       }
356:     }
357:   } catch (error: unknown) {
358:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
359:     console.warn(`[incremental-index] listStaleIndexedPaths error: ${msg}`);
360:   }
361: 
362:   return Array.from(stalePaths);
363: }
364: 
365: function listIndexedRecordIdsForDeletedPaths(filePaths: string[]): number[] {
366:   if (!db || filePaths.length === 0) return [];
367: 
368:   const ids = new Set<number>();
369:   const seenLookupKeys = new Set<string>();
370: 
371:   try {
372:     for (const filePath of filePaths) {
373:       if (typeof filePath !== 'string' || filePath.length === 0) {
374:         continue;
375:       }
376: 
377:       const canonicalPath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
378:       const lookupKey = `${canonicalPath}::${filePath}`;
379:       if (seenLookupKeys.has(lookupKey)) {
380:         continue;
381:       }
382:       seenLookupKeys.add(lookupKey);
383: 
384:       const rows = hasCanonicalPathColumn()
385:         ? (db.prepare(`
386:             SELECT id, file_path, canonical_file_path
387:             FROM memory_index
388:             WHERE canonical_file_path = ? OR file_path = ?
389:           `) as Database.Statement).all(canonicalPath, filePath) as IndexedRecordRow[]
390:         : (db.prepare(`
391:             SELECT id, file_path
392:             FROM memory_index
393:             WHERE file_path = ?
394:           `) as Database.Statement).all(filePath) as IndexedRecordRow[];
395: 
396:       for (const row of rows) {
397:         if (!row || typeof row.id !== 'number') {
398:           continue;
399:         }
400: 
401:         const rowFileExists = typeof row.file_path === 'string' && row.file_path.length > 0
402:           ? getFileMetadata(row.file_path).exists
403:           : false;
404:         const rowCanonicalExists = typeof row.canonical_file_path === 'string' && row.canonical_file_path.length > 0
405:           ? getFileMetadata(row.canonical_file_path).exists
406:           : false;
407: 
408:         if (rowFileExists || rowCanonicalExists) {
409:           continue;
410:         }
411: 
412:         ids.add(row.id);
413:       }
414:     }
415:   } catch (error: unknown) {
416:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
417:     console.warn(`[incremental-index] listIndexedRecordIdsForDeletedPaths error: ${msg}`);
418:     return [];
419:   }
420: 
421:   return Array.from(ids).sort((a, b) => a - b);
422: }
423: 
424: function normalizeOrphanSweepLimit(limit?: number): number {
425:   if (typeof limit !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(limit) || limit <= 0) {
426:     return DEFAULT_ORPHAN_SWEEP_LIMIT;
427:   }
428: 
429:   return Math.max(1, Math.floor(limit));
430: }
431: 
432: function indexedRecordIsAbsent(row: IndexedRecordRow): boolean {
433:   const rowFileExists = typeof row.file_path === 'string' && row.file_path.length > 0
434:     ? getFileMetadata(row.file_path).exists
435:     : false;
436:   const rowCanonicalExists = typeof row.canonical_file_path === 'string' && row.canonical_file_path.length > 0
437:     ? getFileMetadata(row.canonical_file_path).exists
438:     : false;
439: 
440:   return !rowFileExists && !rowCanonicalExists;
441: }
442: 
443: function sweepOrphanIndexRows(options: OrphanSweepOptions = {}): OrphanSweepResult {
444:   if (!db) {
445:     return { swept: 0, nextCursor: null, scannedRows: 0, orphanRecordIds: [] };
446:   }
447: 
448:   const limit = normalizeOrphanSweepLimit(options.limit);
449:   const cursor = typeof options.cursor === 'number' && Number.isFinite(options.cursor) && options.cursor > 0
450:     ? Math.floor(options.cursor)
451:     : 0;
452: 
453:   try {
454:     const rows = hasCanonicalPathColumn()
455:       ? (db.prepare(`
456:           SELECT id, file_path, canonical_file_path
457:           FROM memory_index
458:           WHERE id > ?
459:             AND file_path IS NOT NULL
460:             AND file_path != ''
461:           ORDER BY id ASC
462:           LIMIT ?
463:         `) as Database.Statement).all(cursor, limit) as IndexedRecordRow[]
464:       : (db.prepare(`
465:           SELECT id, file_path
466:           FROM memory_index
467:           WHERE id > ?
468:             AND file_path IS NOT NULL
469:             AND file_path != ''
470:           ORDER BY id ASC
471:           LIMIT ?
472:         `) as Database.Statement).all(cursor, limit) as IndexedRecordRow[];
473: 
474:     const orphanRecordIds: number[] = [];
475:     for (const row of rows) {
476:       if (!row || typeof row.id !== 'number') {
477:         continue;
478:       }
479:       if (indexedRecordIsAbsent(row)) {
480:         orphanRecordIds.push(row.id);
481:       }
482:     }
483: 
484:     return {
485:       swept: orphanRecordIds.length,
486:       nextCursor: rows.length === limit ? rows[rows.length - 1]?.id ?? null : null,
487:       scannedRows: rows.length,
488:       orphanRecordIds,
489:     };
490:   } catch (error: unknown) {
491:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
492:     console.warn(`[incremental-index] sweepOrphanIndexRows error: ${msg}`);
493:     return { swept: 0, nextCursor: null, scannedRows: 0, orphanRecordIds: [] };
494:   }
495: }
496: 
497: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
498:    7b. MOVE RECONCILIATION
499: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
500: 
501: interface MoveReconcileCandidate {
502:   oldPath: string;
503:   newPath: string;
504:   rowId: number;
505:   docType: string;
506: }
507: 
508: interface ReconcileMovesResult {
509:   reconciled: MoveReconcileCandidate[];
510:   filteredToDelete: string[];
511:   filteredToIndex: string[];
512: }
513: 
514: interface IncrementalChunkFingerprint {
515:   chunkId: string;
516:   chunkFingerprint: string;
517: }
518: 
519: interface MemoizedPlanningInput {
520:   componentPath: string;
521:   canonicalInput: unknown;
522:   codeHash: string;
523:   chunks?: IncrementalChunkFingerprint[];
524: }
525: 
526: interface MemoizedPlanningOptions {
527:   invalidateDependents?: boolean;
528: }
529: 
530: interface MemoizedPlanningResult {
531:   memoHits: number;
532:   memoMisses: number;
533:   chunkHits: number;
534:   chunkMisses: number;
535:   dependencyInvalidated: number;
536:   changedComponentPaths: string[];
537:   unchangedComponentPaths: string[];
538:   invalidatedComponentPaths: string[];
539: }
540: 
541: function reconcileMoves(
542:   toDelete: string[],
543:   toIndex: string[],
544: ): ReconcileMovesResult {
545:   if (!db || toDelete.length === 0 || toIndex.length === 0) {
546:     return { reconciled: [], filteredToDelete: toDelete, filteredToIndex: toIndex };
547:   }
548: 
549:   // Build a map: packet_id → [{newPath, docType, newGrandparent}] from the toIndex files.
550:   // Reads graph-metadata.json from the NEW location only — the old one may be gone after a move.
551:   const newByPacketId = new Map<string, Array<{ newPath: string; docType: string; newGrandparent: string }>>();
552:   for (const newPath of toIndex) {
553:     try {
554:       const newSpecFolder = path.dirname(newPath);
555:       const graphMetaPath = path.join(newSpecFolder, 'graph-metadata.json');
556:       if (!fs.existsSync(graphMetaPath)) continue;
557:       const raw = fs.readFileSync(graphMetaPath, 'utf-8');
558:       const meta = JSON.parse(raw) as { packet_id?: string };
559:       const packetId = meta?.packet_id;
560:       if (typeof packetId !== 'string' || packetId.length === 0) continue;
561:       const docType = path.basename(newPath, path.extname(newPath));
562:       const newGrandparent = path.dirname(newSpecFolder);
563:       if (!newByPacketId.has(packetId)) newByPacketId.set(packetId, []);
564:       newByPacketId.get(packetId)!.push({ newPath, docType, newGrandparent });
565:     } catch {
566:       // skip unreadable or non-JSON graph-metadata
567:     }
568:   }
569: 
570:   if (newByPacketId.size === 0) {
571:     return { reconciled: [], filteredToDelete: toDelete, filteredToIndex: toIndex };
572:   }
573: 
574:   const reconciledOldPaths = new Set<string>();
575:   const reconciledNewPaths = new Set<string>();
576:   const reconciled: MoveReconcileCandidate[] = [];
577: 
578:   // Iterate from the NEW side: for each new path whose folder still carries a packet_id,
579:   // find the corresponding old path in toDelete by matching grandparent directory + basename
580:   // (a sibling folder rename within the same parent). The packet_id only gates which new
581:   // paths are eligible — the actual old→new pairing is grandparent + basename plus the
582:   // DB uniqueness guards below; it does NOT match the old row by its packet_id (the old
583:   // graph-metadata.json may be gone, and memory_index has no packet_id column). To avoid
584:   // repointing across mismatched document kinds, the stored document_type is cross-checked
585:   // against the doc type derived from the new path before the in-place update.
586:   const docTypeAvailable = hasDocumentTypeColumn();
587:   const selectSql = docTypeAvailable
588:     ? `SELECT id, document_type FROM memory_index WHERE (file_path = ? OR canonical_file_path = ?) AND embedding_status != 'failed' LIMIT 2`
589:     : `SELECT id FROM memory_index WHERE (file_path = ? OR canonical_file_path = ?) AND embedding_status != 'failed' LIMIT 2`;
590:   for (const [, newCandidates] of newByPacketId) {
591:     for (const { newPath, docType, newGrandparent } of newCandidates) {
592:       if (reconciledNewPaths.has(newPath)) continue;
593: 
594:       const newBasename = path.basename(newPath);
595: 
596:       // Find toDelete paths that are sibling renames: same grandparent dir, same basename.
597:       // A sibling rename means only the immediate spec folder name changed (e.g. 012-old → 012-new).
598:       const siblingDeleted = toDelete.filter(oldPath =>
599:         !reconciledOldPaths.has(oldPath) &&
600:         path.basename(oldPath) === newBasename &&
601:         path.dirname(path.dirname(oldPath)) === newGrandparent
602:       );
603: 
604:       if (siblingDeleted.length !== 1) continue; // uniqueness required
605: 
606:       const oldPath = siblingDeleted[0];
607:       const canonicalOld = getCanonicalPathKey(oldPath);
608: 
609:       // Verify in DB: exactly one live row for this old path (uniqueness guard).
610:       const rows = db!.prepare(selectSql).all(oldPath, canonicalOld) as Array<{ id: number; document_type?: string | null }>;
611:       if (rows.length !== 1) continue;
612: 
613:       // Cross-verify document kind: the stored document_type must agree with the doc type
614:       // derived from the new path. Skip rows whose stored type diverged from the new file
615:       // (e.g. a generic 'memory' row that happens to share a basename), preventing a
616:       // mismatched repoint. Tolerant when the column is absent (legacy/test schemas).
617:       if (docTypeAvailable) {
618:         const storedDocType = rows[0].document_type ?? null;
619:         if (storedDocType !== null && storedDocType !== extractDocumentType(newPath)) continue;
620:       }
621: 
622:       const rowId = rows[0].id;
623:       const newCanonical = getCanonicalPathKey(newPath);
624:       const newSpecFolder = extractSpecFolder(newPath);
625:       const newMtime = (() => {
626:         try { return fs.statSync(newPath).mtimeMs; } catch { return null; }
627:       })();
628: 
629:       // Update the row's path in place (preserves embedding, id, history). A reconciled
630:       // move is a folder rename, so spec_folder is repointed with the same canonicalization
631:       // indexMemoryFile/migration 23 use — otherwise WHERE spec_folder=? grouping goes stale.
632:       const updated = db!.prepare(`
633:         UPDATE memory_index
634:         SET file_path = ?,
635:             canonical_file_path = ?,
636:             spec_folder = ?,
637:             file_mtime_ms = ?,
638:             updated_at = datetime('now')
639:         WHERE id = ?
640:           AND embedding_status != 'failed'
641:       `).run(newPath, newCanonical, newSpecFolder, newMtime, rowId);
642: 
643:       if ((updated as { changes: number }).changes !== 1) continue;
644: 
645:       reconciledOldPaths.add(oldPath);
646:       reconciledNewPaths.add(newPath);
647:       reconciled.push({ oldPath, newPath, rowId, docType });
648:       console.error(`[incremental-index] Move reconciled: ${newBasename} ${oldPath} → ${newPath} (row ${rowId}, embedding preserved)`);
649:     }
650:   }
651: 
652:   return {
653:     reconciled,
654:     filteredToDelete: toDelete.filter(p => !reconciledOldPaths.has(p)),
655:     filteredToIndex: toIndex.filter(p => !reconciledNewPaths.has(p)),
656:   };
657: }

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: File Watcher

5: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
6:    1. IMPORTS
7: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
8: 
9: import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
10: import path from 'node:path';
11: import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';
12: import { BetterSqliteContentionPolicy } from '../storage/ports/contention-policy.js';
13: 
14: // Feature catalog: Real-time filesystem watching with chokidar
15: // Feature catalog: Watcher delete/rename cleanup
16: 
17: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
18:    2. TYPES
19: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
20: 
21: /**
22:  * Describes the WatcherConfig shape.
23:  */
24: export interface WatcherConfig {
25:   paths: string[];
26:   reindexFn: (filePath: string) => Promise<unknown>;
27:   removeFn?: (filePath: string) => Promise<unknown>;
28:   debounceMs?: number;
29:   watchFactory?: (paths: string[], options: Record<string, unknown>) => FSWatcher;
30: }
31: 
32: /**
33:  * Describes the FSWatcher shape.
34:  */
35: export interface FSWatcher {
36:   on: (event: string, listener: (...args: unknown[]) => void) => FSWatcher;
37:   close: () => Promise<void>;
38: }
39: 
40: interface ChokidarModule {
41:   default: {
42:     watch: (paths: string[], options: Record<string, unknown>) => FSWatcher;
43:   };
44: }
45: 
46: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
47:    3. CONSTANTS
48: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
49: 
50: const DEFAULT_DEBOUNCE_MS = 2000;
51: const RETRY_DELAYS_MS = [1000, 2000, 4000];
52: const MAX_BUSY_RETRIES = RETRY_DELAYS_MS.length;
53: const busyRetryPolicy = new BetterSqliteContentionPolicy({ retryable: isSqliteBusyError });
54: 
55: // Watcher metrics counters
56: let filesReindexed = 0;
57: let totalReindexTimeMs = 0;
58: let chokidarModule: ChokidarModule | null = null;
59: let chokidarModulePromise: Promise<ChokidarModule | null> | null = null;
60: let chokidarModuleLoadError: string | null = null;
61: 
62: /** Return accumulated watcher metrics for diagnostics. */
63: export function getWatcherMetrics(): { filesReindexed: number; avgReindexTimeMs: number } {
64:   return {
65:     filesReindexed,
66:     avgReindexTimeMs: filesReindexed > 0 ? Math.round(totalReindexTimeMs / filesReindexed) : 0,
67:   };
68: }
69: 
70: export function resetWatcherMetrics(): void {
71:   filesReindexed = 0;
72:   totalReindexTimeMs = 0;
73: }
74: 
75: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
76:    4. HELPERS
77: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
78: 
79: function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
80:   return new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
81: }
82: 
83: /**
84:  * Coordinates early wake-up of in-flight retry sleeps so a watcher shutdown
85:  * never has to wait for a pending backoff timer to expire. A retry sleep
86:  * registers a `wake` callback; `wakeAll()` settles every outstanding sleep
87:  * immediately, letting each one re-check its abort condition. This keeps
88:  * `close()` from blocking on a non-cancellable timer (and from deadlocking
89:  * under fake timers, where the backoff timeout would otherwise never fire).
90:  */
91: interface RetryWaker {
92:   register(wake: () => void): () => void;
93:   wakeAll(): void;
94: }
95: 
96: function createRetryWaker(): RetryWaker {
97:   const waiters = new Set<() => void>();
98:   let woken = false;
99:   return {
100:     register(wake: () => void): () => void {
101:       // Sticky after wakeAll(): a sleep entered after shutdown began is woken
102:       // at once, so a task that hits SQLITE_BUSY mid-close still exits promptly
103:       // instead of stalling close() on a fresh retry timer.
104:       if (woken) {
105:         wake();
106:         return () => undefined;
107:       }
108:       waiters.add(wake);
109:       return () => { waiters.delete(wake); };
110:     },
111:     wakeAll(): void {
112:       woken = true;
113:       const pending = Array.from(waiters);
114:       waiters.clear();
115:       for (const wake of pending) {
116:         wake();
117:       }
118:     },
119:   };
120: }
121: 
122: /**
123:  * Sleep that resolves either when `ms` elapses or when woken early via the
124:  * supplied waker. On early wake the backoff timer is cleared so no stray timer
125:  * survives shutdown.
126:  */
127: function abortableSleep(ms: number, waker?: RetryWaker): Promise<void> {
128:   if (!waker) {
129:     return sleep(ms);
130:   }
131:   return new Promise((resolve) => {
132:     let settled = false;
133:     let unregister = (): void => undefined;
134:     const finish = (): void => {
135:       if (settled) return;
136:       settled = true;
137:       clearTimeout(timer);
138:       unregister();
139:       resolve();
140:     };
141:     const timer = setTimeout(finish, ms);
142:     unregister = waker.register(finish);
143:   });
144: }
145: 
146: async function loadChokidarModule(): Promise<ChokidarModule | null> {
147:   if (chokidarModule !== null) {
148:     return chokidarModule;
149:   }
150:   if (chokidarModulePromise !== null) {
151:     return chokidarModulePromise;
152:   }
153: 
154:   const loadPromise = (async (): Promise<ChokidarModule | null> => {
155:     try {
156:       chokidarModule = await import('chokidar') as ChokidarModule;
157:       chokidarModuleLoadError = null;
158:       return chokidarModule;
159:     } catch (error: unknown) {
160:       chokidarModuleLoadError = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
161:       return null;
162:     }
163:   })();
164: 
165:   chokidarModulePromise = loadPromise;
166:   try {
167:     return await loadPromise;
168:   } finally {
169:     if (chokidarModulePromise === loadPromise) {
170:       chokidarModulePromise = null;
171:     }
172:   }
173: }
174: 
175: function getWatchFactory(): (paths: string[], options: Record<string, unknown>) => FSWatcher {
176:   if (chokidarModule?.default?.watch) {
177:     return chokidarModule.default.watch;
178:   }
179: 
180:   if (chokidarModulePromise === null) {
181:     void loadChokidarModule();
182:   }
183:   const loadErrorSuffix = chokidarModuleLoadError
184:     ? ` Last module resolution error: ${chokidarModuleLoadError}`
185:     : '';
186:   throw new Error(`chokidar module is still loading or unavailable; retry startFileWatcher once lazy import completes.${loadErrorSuffix}`);
187: }
188: 
189: function isDotfilePath(filePath: string): boolean {
190:   const parts = filePath
191:     .split(/[/\\]/)
192:     .filter((part) => part.length > 0);
193: 
194:   for (const part of parts) {
195:     if (!part.startsWith('.') || part.length <= 1) {
196:       continue;
197:     }
198: 
199:     // .opencode is a first-class workspace root for Spec Kit assets.
200:     // Treating it as a dotfile path would suppress watcher events for
201:     // .opencode/specs/** and break auto re-indexing in default layouts.
202:     if (part === '.opencode') {
203:       continue;
204:     }
205: 
206:     return true;
207:   }
208: 
209:   return false;
210: }
211: 
212: function isMarkdownPath(filePath: string): boolean {
213:   return filePath.toLowerCase().endsWith('.md');
214: }
215: 
216: function shouldIgnoreWatchTarget(targetPath: string): boolean {
217:   if (isDotfilePath(targetPath)) return true;
218:   const basename = path.basename(targetPath);
219:   return basename.startsWith('.');
220: }
221: 
222: function getWatchScopedPath(targetPath: string, watchRoots: string[]): string | null {
223:   const resolvedTarget = path.resolve(targetPath);
224: 
225:   for (const watchRoot of watchRoots) {
226:     const relativeTarget = path.relative(watchRoot, resolvedTarget);
227:     const isWithinRoot = relativeTarget === '' || (
228:       !relativeTarget.startsWith(`..${path.sep}`) &&
229:       relativeTarget !== '..' &&
230:       !path.isAbsolute(relativeTarget)
231:     );
232: 
233:     if (isWithinRoot) {
234:       return relativeTarget;
235:     }
236:   }
237: 
238:   return null;
239: }
240: 
241: function isSqliteBusyError(error: unknown): boolean {
242:   const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
243:   const code = (error as { code?: unknown })?.code;
244:   return code === 'SQLITE_BUSY' || /SQLITE_BUSY/i.test(message);
245: }
246: 
247: async function hashFileContent(filePath: string): Promise<string> {
248:   const content = await fs.readFile(filePath);
249:   return createHash('sha256').update(content).digest('hex');
250: }
251: 
252: interface BusyRetryOptions {
253:   shouldAbort?: () => boolean;
254:   waker?: RetryWaker;
255: }
256: 
257: async function withBusyRetry(
258:   operation: () => Promise<void>,
259:   options: BusyRetryOptions = {},
260: ): Promise<void> {
261:   const { shouldAbort, waker } = options;
262:   await busyRetryPolicy.withRetry(operation, {
263:     attempts: MAX_BUSY_RETRIES + 1,
264:     retryDelaysMs: RETRY_DELAYS_MS,
265:     shouldAbort,
266:     sleep: (ms) => abortableSleep(ms, waker),
267:   });
268: }
269: 
270: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
271:    5. WATCHER CORE
272: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
273: 
274: /**
275:  * Provides the startFileWatcher helper.
276:  */
277: export function startFileWatcher(config: WatcherConfig): FSWatcher {
278:   const watchFactory = config.watchFactory ?? getWatchFactory();
279: 
280:   const debounceMs = config.debounceMs ?? DEFAULT_DEBOUNCE_MS;
281:   const watchRoots = config.paths.map((watchPath) => path.resolve(watchPath));
282:   const debounceTimers = new Map<string, NodeJS.Timeout>();
283:   const contentHashes = new Map<string, string>();
284:   const canonicalPaths = new Map<string, string>();
285:   const inFlightReindex = new Set<Promise<void>>();
286:   let isClosing = false;
287:   // Wakes in-flight SQLITE_BUSY backoff sleeps on close() so shutdown never
288:   // blocks on a pending retry timer.
289:   const retryWaker = createRetryWaker();
290: 
291:   // C3 fix: Bounded concurrency — prevent unbounded parallel reindex operations
292:   const MAX_CONCURRENT_REINDEX = 2;
293:   // Cap the pending-slot queue so a runaway watcher event storm
294:   // can't grow the wait list without bound. 1000 pending entries is generous
295:   // enough for ordinary workloads (debounceTimers already coalesces by file
296:   // path upstream so each slot maps to one distinct file's reindex turn) but
297:   // keeps a runaway loop from leaking memory until OOM. When the queue is at
298:   // cap the OLDEST waiter is rejected — newer events represent fresher state,
299:   // and the dropped waiter's caller has its own catch path that tolerates
300:   // QUEUE_OVERFLOW. On close() every queued waiter is rejected with the same
301:   // sentinel so dependent code can exit early without hanging.
302:   const MAX_PENDING_REINDEX_SLOTS = 1000;
303:   const QUEUE_OVERFLOW_REASON = 'file-watcher reindex queue overflow';
304:   const QUEUE_CLOSED_REASON = 'file-watcher closed before queued reindex ran';
305:   interface QueuedSlot {
306:     readonly resolve: () => void;
307:     readonly reject: (reason: Error) => void;
308:   }
309:   let activeReindexCount = 0;
310:   const pendingReindexSlots: QueuedSlot[] = [];
311: 
312:   function acquireReindexSlot(): Promise<void> {
313:     if (isClosing) {
314:       return Promise.reject(new Error(QUEUE_CLOSED_REASON));
315:     }
316:     if (activeReindexCount < MAX_CONCURRENT_REINDEX) {
317:       activeReindexCount++;
318:       return Promise.resolve();
319:     }
320:     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
321:       // Drop the oldest waiter when the queue is at cap; the
322:       // displaced waiter's wrapping operation should treat the sentinel error
323:       // as a recoverable signal (skip this round, debounce will re-fire on the
324:       // next change).
325:       if (pendingReindexSlots.length >= MAX_PENDING_REINDEX_SLOTS) {
326:         const dropped = pendingReindexSlots.shift();
327:         if (dropped) {
328:           dropped.reject(new Error(QUEUE_OVERFLOW_REASON));
329:         }
330:       }
331:       pendingReindexSlots.push({
332:         resolve: () => { activeReindexCount++; resolve(); },
333:         reject,
334:       });
335:     });
336:   }
337: 
338:   function releaseReindexSlot(): void {
339:     activeReindexCount--;
340:     const next = pendingReindexSlots.shift();
341:     if (next) next.resolve();
342:   }
343: 
344:   // Drain the pending-slot queue on close() so the watcher does
345:   // not silently leak Promises. Every queued waiter rejects with the same
346:   // sentinel; the wrapping `operation()` catch in scheduleTask logs and exits
347:   // cleanly. The active count is intentionally NOT decremented here — those
348:   // slots are owned by in-flight reindex tasks and `releaseReindexSlot()` runs
349:   // in their finally blocks.
350:   function abortPendingReindexQueue(): void {
351:     while (pendingReindexSlots.length > 0) {
352:       const slot = pendingReindexSlots.shift();
353:       if (slot) {
354:         slot.reject(new Error(QUEUE_CLOSED_REASON));
355:       }
356:     }
357:   }
358: 
359:   // AbortController per file path for cancellation of stale reindex
360:   const activeAbortControllers = new Map<string, AbortController>();
361: 
362:   const trackInFlight = (task: Promise<void>): void => {
363:     inFlightReindex.add(task);
364:     void task.finally(() => {
365:       inFlightReindex.delete(task);
366:     });
367:   };
368: 
369:   const watcher = watchFactory(config.paths, {
370:     ignoreInitial: true,
371:     awaitWriteFinish: { stabilityThreshold: 1000 },
372:     ignored: (targetPath: string) => {
373:       return shouldIgnoreWatchTarget(getWatchScopedPath(targetPath, watchRoots) ?? targetPath);
374:     },
375:     followSymlinks: false,
376:   });
377: 
378:   const scheduleTask = (
379:     targetPath: unknown,
380:     operation: () => Promise<void>,
381:   ): void => {
382:     if (typeof targetPath !== 'string') {
383:       return;
384:     }
385:     if (isClosing) {
386:       return;
387:     }
388: 
389:     const filePath = targetPath;
390:     const watchScopedPath = getWatchScopedPath(filePath, watchRoots);
391: 
392:     if (!isMarkdownPath(filePath) || shouldIgnoreWatchTarget(watchScopedPath ?? filePath)) {
393:       return;
394:     }
395: 
396:     const existing = debounceTimers.get(filePath);
397:     if (existing) {
398:       clearTimeout(existing);
399:     }
400: 
401:     const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
402:       debounceTimers.delete(filePath);
403:       if (isClosing) {
404:         return;
405:       }
406: 
407:       const task = operation().catch((error: unknown) => {
408:         const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
409:         // Queue-overflow and queue-closed errors are expected
410:         // outcomes of the bounded-queue policy, not failures. Log them at a
411:         // lower severity so storms do not drown legitimate task warnings.
412:         if (message === QUEUE_OVERFLOW_REASON || message === QUEUE_CLOSED_REASON) {
413:           console.error(`[file-watcher] Skipped queued reindex for ${path.basename(filePath)}: ${message}`);
414:           return;
415:         }
416:         console.warn(`[file-watcher] Watch task failed for ${path.basename(filePath)}: ${message}`);
417:       });
418:       trackInFlight(task);
419:     }, debounceMs);
420: 
421:     debounceTimers.set(filePath, timeout);
422:   };
423: 
424:   const scheduleReindex = (targetPath: unknown, options: { force?: boolean } = {}): void => {
425:     const filePath = typeof targetPath === 'string' ? targetPath : null;
426:     const forceReindex = options.force === true;
427: 
428:     // Abort any in-flight reindex for this same file before scheduling new one
429:     if (filePath) {
430:       const prev = activeAbortControllers.get(filePath);
431:       if (prev) prev.abort();
432:     }
433: 
434:     scheduleTask(targetPath, async () => {
435:       if (!filePath) {
436:         return;
437:       }
438: 
439:       const controller = new AbortController();
440:       activeAbortControllers.set(filePath, controller);
441: 
442:       try {
443:         let resolvedPath: string;
444:         try {
445:           resolvedPath = await fs.realpath(filePath);
446:         } catch (realpathErr: unknown) {
447:           const code = realpathErr instanceof Error && 'code' in realpathErr && typeof realpathErr.code === 'string'
448:             ? realpathErr.code
449:             : undefined;
450:           if (code === 'ENOENT') return;
451:           throw realpathErr;
452:         }
453: 
454:         if (controller.signal.aborted) return;
455:         activeAbortControllers.set(resolvedPath, controller);
456:         canonicalPaths.set(filePath, resolvedPath);
457:         canonicalPaths.set(resolvedPath, resolvedPath);
458: 
459:         const containmentRoots = await Promise.all(config.paths.map(async (root) => {
460:           const normalizedRoot = path.resolve(root);
461:           try {
462:             return await fs.realpath(normalizedRoot);
463:           } catch (rootErr: unknown) {
464:             const code = rootErr instanceof Error && 'code' in rootErr && typeof rootErr.code === 'string'
465:               ? rootErr.code
466:               : undefined;
467:             if (code === 'ENOENT') return normalizedRoot;
468:             throw rootErr;
469:           }
470:         }));
471:         const isContained = containmentRoots.some((root) => {
472:           const rootPrefix = root.endsWith(path.sep) ? root : `${root}${path.sep}`;
473:           return resolvedPath === root || resolvedPath.startsWith(rootPrefix);
474:         });
475:         if (!isContained) {
476:           console.warn(`[file-watcher] Skipping reindex outside watch roots: ${path.basename(filePath)}`);
477:           return;
478:         }
479: 
480:         // Handle ENOENT gracefully when a file is rapidly
481:         // Created then deleted before the debounce timer fires.
482:         let nextHash: string;
483:         try {
484:           nextHash = await hashFileContent(resolvedPath);
485:         } catch (hashErr: unknown) {
486:           const code = hashErr instanceof Error && 'code' in hashErr && typeof hashErr.code === 'string'
487:             ? hashErr.code
488:             : undefined;
489:           if (code === 'ENOENT') return; // File was deleted — silently ignore
490:           throw hashErr;
491:         }
492: 
493:         if (controller.signal.aborted) return;
494: 
495:         if (!forceReindex) {
496:           const previousHash = contentHashes.get(resolvedPath);
497:           if (previousHash && previousHash === nextHash) {
498:             return;
499:           }
500:         }
501: 
502:         // C3: Acquire concurrency slot before CPU-intensive reindex
503:         await acquireReindexSlot();
504:         try {
505:           if (controller.signal.aborted) return;
506: 
507:           const reindexStart = Date.now();
508:           await withBusyRetry(async () => {
509:             await config.reindexFn(resolvedPath);
510:           }, { shouldAbort: () => isClosing, waker: retryWaker });
511:           if (isClosing) return;
512:           const reindexElapsed = Date.now() - reindexStart;
513:           filesReindexed++;
514:           totalReindexTimeMs += reindexElapsed;
515:           console.error(`[file-watcher] Reindexed ${path.basename(resolvedPath)} in ${reindexElapsed}ms (total: ${filesReindexed} files, avg: ${Math.round(totalReindexTimeMs / filesReindexed)}ms)`);
516: 
517:           contentHashes.set(resolvedPath, nextHash);
518:         } finally {
519:           releaseReindexSlot();
520:         }
521:       } finally {
522:         if (activeAbortControllers.get(filePath) === controller) {
523:           activeAbortControllers.delete(filePath);
524:         }
525:         const resolvedPath = canonicalPaths.get(filePath);
526:         if (resolvedPath && activeAbortControllers.get(resolvedPath) === controller) {
527:           activeAbortControllers.delete(resolvedPath);
528:         }
529:       }
530:     });
531:   };
532: 
533:   const scheduleRemove = (targetPath: unknown): void => {
534:     const filePath = typeof targetPath === 'string' ? targetPath : null;
535: 
536:     scheduleTask(targetPath, async () => {
537:       if (!filePath) {
538:         return;
539:       }
540: 
541:       const canonicalPath = canonicalPaths.get(filePath) ?? filePath;
542:       contentHashes.delete(canonicalPath);
543:       contentHashes.delete(filePath);
544:       canonicalPaths.delete(filePath);
545:       if (canonicalPath !== filePath) {
546:         canonicalPaths.delete(canonicalPath);
547:       }
548:       if (!config.removeFn) {
549:         return;
550:       }
551: 
552:       await withBusyRetry(async () => {
553:         await config.removeFn?.(filePath);
554:       }, { shouldAbort: () => isClosing, waker: retryWaker });
555:       if (isClosing) return;
556:       console.error(`[file-watcher] Removed indexed entries for ${path.basename(filePath)}`);
557:     });
558:   };
559: 
560:   watcher.on('add', (targetPath: unknown) => {
561:     scheduleReindex(targetPath, { force: true });
562:   });
563:   watcher.on('change', (targetPath: unknown) => {
564:     scheduleReindex(targetPath);
565:   });
566:   watcher.on('unlink', (targetPath: unknown) => {
567:     scheduleRemove(targetPath);
568:   });
569:   watcher.on('error', (error: unknown) => {
570:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
571:     console.warn(`[file-watcher] Watcher error: ${message}`);
572:   });
573: 
574:   const originalClose = watcher.close.bind(watcher);
575:   watcher.close = async () => {
576:     isClosing = true;
577:     for (const timeout of debounceTimers.values()) {
578:       clearTimeout(timeout);
579:     }
580:     debounceTimers.clear();
581: 
582:     // Drop every queued slot waiter before settling in-flight
583:     // work. The waiters reject with QUEUE_CLOSED_REASON; their wrapping
584:     // operation()s catch and log without re-entering the watcher.
585:     abortPendingReindexQueue();
586: 
587:     // Wake any in-flight SQLITE_BUSY backoff sleeps so a task mid-retry exits
588:     // immediately (it re-checks isClosing) instead of holding close() until a
589:     // non-cancellable retry timer expires.
590:     retryWaker.wakeAll();
591: 
592:     while (inFlightReindex.size > 0) {
593:       await Promise.allSettled(Array.from(inFlightReindex));
594:     }
595: 
596:     await originalClose();
597:   };
598: 
599:   return watcher;
600: }
601: 
602: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
603:    6. EXPORTS
604: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
605: 
606: /**
607:  * Defines the __testables constant.
608:  */
609: export const __testables = {
610:   getWatchScopedPath,
611:   isDotfilePath,
612:   isMarkdownPath,
613:   shouldIgnoreWatchTarget,
614:   isSqliteBusyError,
615:   resetWatcherMetrics,
616: };

(End of file - total 616 lines)

<content>
1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Maintenance Job Store
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Kind-agnostic persisted job lifecycle: jobId, state record, progress
5: // counters, error capture+truncation, cancel flag, and crash-recovery
6: // reset. Shared lifecycle/registry across long-running maintenance jobs.
7: 
8: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9:    1. IMPORTS
10: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
11: 
12: import { randomBytes } from 'node:crypto';
13: 
14: import { requireDb, toErrorMessage } from '../../utils/index.js';
15: import { withBusyRetry, withBusyRetrySync } from './sqlite-busy-retry.js';
16: 
17: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
18:    2. TYPES
19: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
20: 
21: export type MaintenanceJobKind = 'ingest' | 'index_scan';
22: 
23: export type JobLifecycleState = 'queued' | 'running' | 'complete' | 'failed' | 'cancelled';
24: 
25: export interface JobError {
26:   source: string;
27:   message: string;
28:   timestamp: string;
29: }
30: 
31: export interface MaintenanceJob {
32:   id: string;
33:   kind: MaintenanceJobKind;
34:   state: JobLifecycleState;
35:   phase: string | null;
36:   progressTotal: number;
37:   progressProcessed: number;
38:   cancelRequested: boolean;
39:   errors: JobError[];
40:   result: unknown | null;
41:   payload: unknown | null;
42:   createdAt: string;
43:   updatedAt: string;
44: }
45: 
46: interface MaintenanceJobRow {
47:   id: string;
48:   kind: string;
49:   state: string;
50:   phase: string | null;
51:   progress_total: number;
52:   progress_processed: number;
53:   cancel_requested: number;
54:   errors_json: string;
55:   result_json: string | null;
56:   payload_json: string | null;
57:   created_at: string;
58:   updated_at: string;
59: }
60: 
61: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
62:    3. CONSTANTS
63: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
64: 
65: export const MAX_STORED_ERRORS = 50;
66: 
67: const TERMINAL_STATES = new Set<JobLifecycleState>(['complete', 'failed', 'cancelled']);
68: 
69: // In-process mirror of cancel_requested. A running job polls this from a hot loop
70: // to decide whether to stop, avoiding a DB SELECT on the single shared connection
71: // every check. The cancel request runs in this same process, so the Set is
72: // authoritative for an in-flight run; the durable column still backs status
73: // responses and crash recovery. Entries are cleared when a job reaches a terminal
74: // state so the Set cannot grow without bound across many jobs.
75: const cancelledJobIds = new Set<string>();
76: 
77: const ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS: Record<JobLifecycleState, Set<JobLifecycleState>> = {
78:   queued: new Set<JobLifecycleState>(['running', 'cancelled', 'failed']),
79:   running: new Set<JobLifecycleState>(['complete', 'failed', 'cancelled']),
80:   complete: new Set<JobLifecycleState>([]),
81:   failed: new Set<JobLifecycleState>([]),
82:   cancelled: new Set<JobLifecycleState>([]),
83: };
84: 
85: // URL-safe 12-char identifier without a UUID dependency.
86: const NANOID_ALPHABET = '0123456789ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz';
87: 
88: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
89:    4. HELPERS
90: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
91: 
92: function nowIso(): string {
93:   return new Date().toISOString();
94: }
95: 
96: function isTransitionAllowed(from: JobLifecycleState, to: JobLifecycleState): boolean {
97:   return ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS[from]?.has(to) ?? false;
98: }
99: 
100: function parseJson<T>(value: string | null | undefined, fallback: T): T {
101:   if (value === null || value === undefined) return fallback;
102:   try {
103:     return JSON.parse(value) as T;
104:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
105:     return fallback;
106:   }
107: }
108: 
109: function parseJobErrors(value: string | null | undefined): JobError[] {
110:   const parsed = parseJson<unknown>(value, []);
111:   return Array.isArray(parsed) ? (parsed as JobError[]) : [];
112: }
113: 
114: function mapRowToJob(row: MaintenanceJobRow | undefined): MaintenanceJob | null {
115:   if (!row) return null;
116:   return {
117:     id: row.id,
118:     kind: row.kind as MaintenanceJobKind,
119:     state: row.state as JobLifecycleState,
120:     phase: row.phase,
121:     progressTotal: row.progress_total,
122:     progressProcessed: row.progress_processed,
123:     cancelRequested: row.cancel_requested === 1,
124:     errors: parseJobErrors(row.errors_json),
125:     result: row.result_json === null || row.result_json === undefined ? null : parseJson<unknown>(row.result_json, null),
126:     payload: row.payload_json === null || row.payload_json === undefined ? null : parseJson<unknown>(row.payload_json, null),
127:     createdAt: row.created_at,
128:     updatedAt: row.updated_at,
129:   };
130: }
131: 
132: const SELECT_COLUMNS =
133:   'id, kind, state, phase, progress_total, progress_processed, cancel_requested, errors_json, result_json, payload_json, created_at, updated_at';
134: 
135: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
136:    5. SCHEMA
137: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
138: 
139: export function ensureMaintenanceJobsTable(): void {
140:   const db = requireDb();
141:   db.exec(`
142:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS maintenance_jobs (
143:       id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
144:       kind TEXT NOT NULL,
145:       state TEXT NOT NULL,
146:       phase TEXT,
147:       progress_total INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
148:       progress_processed INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
149:       cancel_requested INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
150:       errors_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
151:       result_json TEXT,
152:       payload_json TEXT,
153:       created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
154:       updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
155:     )
156:   `);
157:   db.exec(`CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_maintenance_jobs_kind_state ON maintenance_jobs(kind, state)`);
158: }
159: 
160: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
161:    6. IDENTIFIERS
162: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
163: 
164: export function createJobId(): string {
165:   const bytes = randomBytes(12);
166:   let id = '';
167:   for (let i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
168:     id += NANOID_ALPHABET[bytes[i] % NANOID_ALPHABET.length];
169:   }
170:   return `job_${id}`;
171: }
172: 
173: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
174:    7. LIFECYCLE
175: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
176: 
177: export async function createMaintenanceJob(args: {
178:   id: string;
179:   kind: MaintenanceJobKind;
180:   payload?: unknown;
181: }): Promise<MaintenanceJob> {
182:   const db = requireDb();
183:   const timestamp = nowIso();
184:   const payloadJson = args.payload === undefined ? null : JSON.stringify(args.payload);
185: 
186:   await withBusyRetry(() =>
187:     db.prepare(`
188:       INSERT INTO maintenance_jobs (
189:         id, kind, state, phase, progress_total, progress_processed,
190:         cancel_requested, errors_json, result_json, payload_json, created_at, updated_at
191:       ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
192:     `).run(
193:       args.id,
194:       args.kind,
195:       'queued',
196:       null,
197:       0,
198:       0,
199:       0,
200:       '[]',
201:       null,
202:       payloadJson,
203:       timestamp,
204:       timestamp,
205:     )
206:   );
207: 
208:   return {
209:     id: args.id,
210:     kind: args.kind,
211:     state: 'queued',
212:     phase: null,
213:     progressTotal: 0,
214:     progressProcessed: 0,
215:     cancelRequested: false,
216:     errors: [],
217:     result: null,
218:     payload: args.payload ?? null,
219:     createdAt: timestamp,
220:     updatedAt: timestamp,
221:   };
222: }
223: 
224: export function getMaintenanceJob(jobId: string): MaintenanceJob | null {
225:   const db = requireDb();
226:   const row = db.prepare(`
227:     SELECT ${SELECT_COLUMNS}
228:     FROM maintenance_jobs
229:     WHERE id = ?
230:   `).get(jobId) as MaintenanceJobRow | undefined;
231:   return mapRowToJob(row);
232: }
233: 
234: export async function setJobState(jobId: string, nextState: JobLifecycleState): Promise<MaintenanceJob> {
235:   const db = requireDb();
236:   const current = getMaintenanceJob(jobId);
237:   if (!current) {
238:     throw new Error(`Maintenance job not found: ${jobId}`);
239:   }
240: 
241:   if (current.state === nextState) {
242:     return current;
243:   }
244: 
245:   if (!isTransitionAllowed(current.state, nextState)) {
246:     throw new Error(`Invalid maintenance job state transition: ${current.state} -> ${nextState}`);
247:   }
248: 
249:   const updatedAt = nowIso();
250:   const result = await withBusyRetry(() =>
251:     db.prepare(`
252:       UPDATE maintenance_jobs
253:       SET state = ?, updated_at = ?
254:       WHERE id = ? AND state = ?
255:     `).run(nextState, updatedAt, jobId, current.state)
256:   );
257: 
258:   if ((result as { changes: number }).changes === 0) {
259:     throw new Error(`State transition conflict: job ${jobId} state was changed by another process (expected '${current.state}')`);
260:   }
261: 
262:   // Every terminal transition must release the in-process cancel mirror, not just
263:   // completeJob/resetRunningJobsForKind. A cancel-then-fail-while-running race
264:   // routes through setJobState('failed'); without this the job-id would leak,
265:   // contradicting the cancelledJobIds bounded-growth guarantee.
266:   if (isTerminalJobState(nextState)) {
267:     cancelledJobIds.delete(jobId);
268:   }
269: 
270:   return { ...current, state: nextState, updatedAt };
271: }
272: 
273: export async function setJobPhase(jobId: string, phase: string): Promise<void> {
274:   const db = requireDb();
275:   await withBusyRetry(() =>
276:     db.prepare(`UPDATE maintenance_jobs SET phase = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?`)
277:       .run(phase, nowIso(), jobId)
278:   );
279: }
280: 
281: export async function setJobProgress(jobId: string, progress: { processed: number; total: number }): Promise<void> {
282:   const db = requireDb();
283:   const processed = Math.max(0, Math.trunc(progress.processed));
284:   const total = Math.max(0, Math.trunc(progress.total));
285:   await withBusyRetry(() =>
286:     db.prepare(`UPDATE maintenance_jobs SET progress_processed = ?, progress_total = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?`)
287:       .run(processed, total, nowIso(), jobId)
288:   );
289: }
290: 
291: export async function appendJobError(jobId: string, source: string, error: unknown): Promise<MaintenanceJob> {
292:   const db = requireDb();
293:   const current = getMaintenanceJob(jobId);
294:   if (!current) {
295:     throw new Error(`Maintenance job not found: ${jobId}`);
296:   }
297: 
298:   let errors: JobError[] = [
299:     ...current.errors,
300:     { source, message: toErrorMessage(error), timestamp: nowIso() },
301:   ];
302: 
303:   if (errors.length > MAX_STORED_ERRORS) {
304:     const tailLength = Math.max(0, MAX_STORED_ERRORS - 1);
305:     const trimmedCount = errors.length - tailLength;
306:     const truncationNotice: JobError = {
307:       source: '__job__',
308:       message: `error log truncated (${trimmedCount} older entries removed)`,
309:       timestamp: nowIso(),
310:     };
311:     const truncatedTail = tailLength > 0 ? errors.slice(-tailLength) : [];
312:     errors = [truncationNotice, ...truncatedTail];
313:   }
314: 
315:   const updatedAt = nowIso();
316:   await withBusyRetry(() =>
317:     db.prepare(`UPDATE maintenance_jobs SET errors_json = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?`)
318:       .run(JSON.stringify(errors), updatedAt, jobId)
319:   );
320: 
321:   return { ...current, errors, updatedAt };
322: }
323: 
324: export async function requestCancel(jobId: string): Promise<void> {
325:   // Set the in-process flag first so a running loop observes the cancel even if the
326:   // durable write contends on the shared connection.
327:   cancelledJobIds.add(jobId);
328:   const db = requireDb();
329:   await withBusyRetry(() =>
330:     db.prepare(`UPDATE maintenance_jobs SET cancel_requested = 1, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?`)
331:       .run(nowIso(), jobId)
332:   );
333: }
334: 
335: // Synchronous so the runner can poll it inline at batch/phase boundaries.
336: export function isCancelRequested(jobId: string): boolean {
337:   const db = requireDb();
338:   const row = db.prepare(`SELECT cancel_requested FROM maintenance_jobs WHERE id = ?`)
339:     .get(jobId) as { cancel_requested?: number } | undefined;
340:   return !!row && row.cancel_requested === 1;
341: }
342: 
343: // Allocation- and IO-free cancel check for hot loops. Reads only the in-process
344: // mirror, so it is safe to call thousands of times during a scan without touching
345: // the shared SQLite connection. Use isCancelRequested when durability across the
346: // in-memory boundary matters (status responses, post-restart).
347: export function isCancelRequestedFast(jobId: string): boolean {
348:   return cancelledJobIds.has(jobId);
349: }
350: 
351: export async function completeJob(
352:   jobId: string,
353:   options: { state: JobLifecycleState; result?: unknown },
354: ): Promise<MaintenanceJob> {
355:   const db = requireDb();
356:   const current = getMaintenanceJob(jobId);
357:   if (!current) {
358:     throw new Error(`Maintenance job not found: ${jobId}`);
359:   }
360: 
361:   if (current.state !== options.state && !isTransitionAllowed(current.state, options.state)) {
362:     throw new Error(`Invalid maintenance job state transition: ${current.state} -> ${options.state}`);
363:   }
364: 
365:   const updatedAt = nowIso();
366:   const resultJson = options.result === undefined ? null : JSON.stringify(options.result);
367:   const completion = await withBusyRetry(() =>
368:     db.prepare(`UPDATE maintenance_jobs SET state = ?, result_json = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ? AND state = ?`)
369:       .run(options.state, resultJson, updatedAt, jobId, current.state)
370:   );
371:   if (completion.changes === 0) {
372:     // The row moved out from under the read-then-check; refuse to clobber a state
373:     // another writer set rather than overwriting it from a stale read.
374:     throw new Error(`Maintenance job state changed concurrently: ${jobId}`);
375:   }
376: 
377:   cancelledJobIds.delete(jobId);
378:   return { ...current, state: options.state, result: options.result ?? null, updatedAt };
379: }
380: 
381: // Crash recovery: mark interrupted jobs of a kind terminal on boot. Index scans
382: // re-run fresh, so they are reset to 'failed' rather than re-enqueued.
383: export function resetRunningJobsForKind(
384:   kind: MaintenanceJobKind,
385:   // Crash recovery only moves interrupted jobs to a terminal state. Restricting the
386:   // target keeps a caller from persisting an illegal transition past the guard.
387:   options: { to: 'failed' | 'cancelled' },
388: ): string[] {
389:   const db = requireDb();
390:   const rows = db.prepare(`
391:     SELECT id FROM maintenance_jobs
392:     WHERE kind = ? AND state IN ('queued', 'running')
393:   `).all(kind) as Array<{ id: string }>;
394: 
395:   if (rows.length === 0) return [];
396: 
397:   withBusyRetrySync(() =>
398:     db.prepare(`
399:       UPDATE maintenance_jobs
400:       SET state = ?, updated_at = ?
401:       WHERE kind = ? AND state IN ('queued', 'running')
402:     `).run(options.to, nowIso(), kind)
403:   );
404: 
405:   for (const row of rows) {
406:     cancelledJobIds.delete(row.id);
407:   }
408:   return rows.map((r) => r.id);
409: }
410: 
411: export function isTerminalJobState(state: JobLifecycleState): boolean {
412:   return TERMINAL_STATES.has(state);
413: }

(End of file - total 413 lines)
</content>
