<content>
1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Transaction Manager
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: Transaction wrappers on mutation handlers
5: // Atomic file + index operations with pending file recovery
6: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
7: import * as fs from 'fs';
8: import * as path from 'path';
9: import { resolveDatabasePaths } from '../../core/config.js';
10: 
11: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
12:    1. CONSTANTS
13: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
14: 
15: const PENDING_SUFFIX = '_pending';
16: const TEMP_SUFFIX = '.tmp';
17: const PENDING_UNIQUE_SUFFIX_PATTERN = /^(?:[0-9a-f]{8}|[0-9a-f]{8}(?:-[0-9a-f]{4}){3}-[0-9a-f]{12})$/i;
18: 
19: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
20:    2. INTERFACES
21: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
22: 
23: interface TransactionMetrics {
24:   totalAtomicWrites: number;
25:   totalDeletes: number;
26:   totalRecoveries: number;
27:   totalErrors: number;
28:   lastOperationTime: string | null;
29:   [key: string]: unknown;
30: }
31: 
32: interface AtomicSaveResult {
33:   success: boolean;
34:   filePath: string;
35:   error?: string;
36:   /** Set when DB committed but rename failed, indicating partial state */
37:   dbCommitted?: boolean;
38: }
39: 
40: interface RecoveryResult {
41:   path: string;
42:   recovered: boolean;
43:   error?: string;
44: }
45: 
46: /**
47:  * Optional database probe used during pending-file recovery.
48:  * Return `true` when the original path already has a committed `memory_index` row.
49:  * Return `false` to leave the `_pending` file in place for manual review.
50:  * Throwing aborts recovery for that file and surfaces the error message in `RecoveryResult.error`.
51:  */
52: type IsCommittedCheck = (originalPath: string) => boolean;
53: 
54: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
55:    3. MODULE STATE
56: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
57: 
58: const metrics: TransactionMetrics = {
59:   totalAtomicWrites: 0,
60:   totalDeletes: 0,
61:   totalRecoveries: 0,
62:   totalErrors: 0,
63:   lastOperationTime: null,
64: };
65: 
66: const activeTransactions = new WeakMap<object, number>();
67: 
68: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69:    4. METRICS
70: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
71: 
72: function getMetrics(): TransactionMetrics {
73:   return { ...metrics };
74: }
75: 
76: function resetMetrics(): void {
77:   metrics.totalAtomicWrites = 0;
78:   metrics.totalDeletes = 0;
79:   metrics.totalRecoveries = 0;
80:   metrics.totalErrors = 0;
81:   metrics.lastOperationTime = null;
82: }
83: 
84: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
85:    5. PATH HELPERS
86: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
87: 
88: function getPendingPath(filePath: string): string {
89:   const dir = path.dirname(filePath);
90:   const ext = path.extname(filePath);
91:   const base = path.basename(filePath, ext);
92:   return path.join(dir, `${base}${PENDING_SUFFIX}${ext}`);
93: }
94: 
95: interface PendingPathInfo {
96:   originalPath: string;
97:   basePendingPath: string;
98:   uniqueSuffix: string | null;
99: }
100: 
101: function parsePendingPath(filePath: string): PendingPathInfo | null {
102:   const parseBasePendingPath = (candidatePath: string): PendingPathInfo | null => {
103:     const dir = path.dirname(candidatePath);
104:     const ext = path.extname(candidatePath);
105:     const base = path.basename(candidatePath, ext);
106:     if (!base.endsWith(PENDING_SUFFIX)) {
107:       return null;
108:     }
109: 
110:     const originalBase = base.slice(0, -PENDING_SUFFIX.length);
111:     return {
112:       originalPath: path.join(dir, `${originalBase}${ext}`),
113:       basePendingPath: candidatePath,
114:       uniqueSuffix: null,
115:     };
116:   };
117: 
118:   const suffixExt = path.extname(filePath);
119:   if (suffixExt) {
120:     const uniqueSuffix = suffixExt.slice(1);
121:     const basePendingPath = filePath.slice(0, -suffixExt.length);
122:     const suffixedMatch = parseBasePendingPath(basePendingPath);
123:     if (
124:       suffixedMatch &&
125:       path.extname(suffixedMatch.basePendingPath) &&
126:       PENDING_UNIQUE_SUFFIX_PATTERN.test(uniqueSuffix)
127:     ) {
128:       return {
129:         ...suffixedMatch,
130:         uniqueSuffix,
131:       };
132:     }
133:   }
134: 
135:   return parseBasePendingPath(filePath);
136: }
137: 
138: function isPendingFile(filePath: string): boolean {
139:   return parsePendingPath(filePath) !== null;
140: }
141: 
142: function getOriginalPath(pendingPath: string): string {
143:   return parsePendingPath(pendingPath)?.originalPath ?? pendingPath;
144: }
145: 
146: function runInTransaction<T>(database: Database.Database, callback: () => T): T {
147:   const runCallback = (): T => {
148:     const depth = activeTransactions.get(database) ?? 0;
149:     activeTransactions.set(database, depth + 1);
150:     try {
151:       return callback();
152:     } finally {
153:       const nextDepth = (activeTransactions.get(database) ?? 1) - 1;
154:       if (nextDepth > 0) {
155:         activeTransactions.set(database, nextDepth);
156:       } else {
157:         activeTransactions.delete(database);
158:       }
159:     }
160:   };
161: 
162:   if ((activeTransactions.get(database) ?? 0) > 0 || database.inTransaction) {
163:     return runCallback();
164:   }
165: 
166:   return database.transaction(() => runCallback())();
167: }
168: 
169: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
170:    6. ATOMIC FILE OPERATIONS
171: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
172: 
173: /**
174:  * Write a file atomically using write-to-temp-then-rename pattern.
175:  * This prevents partial writes if the process crashes mid-write.
176:  */
177: function atomicWriteFile(filePath: string, content: string, encoding: BufferEncoding = 'utf-8'): boolean {
178:   const tempPath = filePath + TEMP_SUFFIX;
179: 
180:   try {
181:     // Ensure directory exists
182:     const dir = path.dirname(filePath);
183:     if (!fs.existsSync(dir)) {
184:       fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });
185:     }
186: 
187:     // Write to temp file
188:     fs.writeFileSync(tempPath, content, encoding);
189: 
190:     // Atomic rename
191:     fs.renameSync(tempPath, filePath);
192: 
193:     metrics.totalAtomicWrites++;
194:     metrics.lastOperationTime = new Date().toISOString();
195:     return true;
196:   } catch (error: unknown) {
197:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
198:     console.warn(`[transaction-manager] atomicWriteFile error: ${msg}`);
199:     metrics.totalErrors++;
200: 
201:     // Clean up temp file
202:     try {
203:       if (fs.existsSync(tempPath)) fs.unlinkSync(tempPath);
204:     } catch { /* ignore cleanup errors */ }
205: 
206:     return false;
207:   }
208: }
209: 
210: /**
211:  * Delete a file if it exists.
212:  */
213: function deleteFileIfExists(filePath: string): boolean {
214:   try {
215:     if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
216:       fs.unlinkSync(filePath);
217:       metrics.totalDeletes++;
218:       metrics.lastOperationTime = new Date().toISOString();
219:       return true;
220:     }
221:     return false;
222:   } catch (error: unknown) {
223:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
224:     console.warn(`[transaction-manager] deleteFileIfExists error: ${msg}`);
225:     metrics.totalErrors++;
226:     return false;
227:   }
228: }
229: 
230: /**
231:  * Execute an atomic save with pending file pattern.
232:  * 1. Write to pending path
233:  * 2. Execute database operations
234:  * 3. Rename pending to final
235:  *
236:  * This function provides file-system-level atomicity (write-to-pending + rename),
237:  * NOT database transaction isolation. Callers needing transactional guarantees for
238:  * dbOperation() must wrap it in their own db.transaction() call. The rename step
239:  * ensures the file is either fully written or not present, but the DB operation
240:  * is not rolled back if the rename fails (extremely rare on local filesystems).
241:  */
242: function executeAtomicSave(
243:   filePath: string,
244:   content: string,
245:   dbOperation: () => void
246: ): AtomicSaveResult {
247:   const pendingPath = getPendingPath(filePath);
248: 
249:   try {
250:     // Step 1: Write to pending path
251:     const dir = path.dirname(pendingPath);
252:     if (!fs.existsSync(dir)) {
253:       fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });
254:     }
255:     fs.writeFileSync(pendingPath, content, 'utf-8');
256: 
257:     // Flag-based rollback coordination.
258:     // The dbOperation callback runs synchronously (better-sqlite3 transaction). If it
259:     // Succeeds but the rename (Step 3) fails, the DB change is already committed and
260:     // Cannot be rolled back. The dbCommitted flag tracks this state so the error path
261:     // Can report which step failed. No SAVEPOINT is used — better-sqlite3 transactions
262:     // Are all-or-nothing within the dbOperation() callback itself.
263:     let dbCommitted = false;
264: 
265:     // Step 2: Execute database operation
266:     try {
267:       dbOperation();
268:       dbCommitted = true;
269:     } catch (dbError: unknown) {
270:       // Database failed - clean up pending file
271:       try { fs.unlinkSync(pendingPath); } catch { /* ignore */ }
272:       const msg = dbError instanceof Error ? dbError.message : String(dbError);
273:       metrics.totalErrors++;
274:       return { success: false, filePath, error: `DB operation failed: ${msg}` };
275:     }
276: 
277:     // Step 3: Rename pending to final (atomic)
278:     // KNOWN LIMITATION: If renameSync fails after dbOperation() committed,
279:     // The DB contains the new state but the file is not at its final path. This is
280:     // A window of vulnerability that cannot be eliminated without two-phase commit.
281:     // The `dbCommitted` flag on the returned error result enables callers to detect
282:     // This state and trigger recovery (e.g., re-index from DB or replay the write).
283:     // Mitigation: `recoverAllPendingFiles()` can be called on startup to find
284:     // Orphaned pending files and rename them to their final paths.
285:     try {
286:       fs.renameSync(pendingPath, filePath);
287:     } catch (renameError: unknown) {
288:       // Rename failed after DB committed — DB has new state but file wasn't renamed.
289:       // Leave pending file for startup recovery and report failure with dbCommitted flag.
290:       const msg = renameError instanceof Error ? renameError.message : String(renameError);
291:       console.warn(`[transaction-manager] rename failed after DB commit; pending file kept for recovery: ${pendingPath} (${msg})`);
292:       metrics.totalErrors++;
293:       return { success: false, filePath, error: `Rename failed after DB commit: ${msg}`, dbCommitted };
294:     }
295: 
296:     metrics.totalAtomicWrites++;
297:     metrics.lastOperationTime = new Date().toISOString();
298:     return { success: true, filePath };
299:   } catch (error: unknown) {
300:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
301:     metrics.totalErrors++;
302: 
303:     // Clean up pending file
304:     try {
305:       if (fs.existsSync(pendingPath)) fs.unlinkSync(pendingPath);
306:     } catch { /* ignore */ }
307: 
308:     return { success: false, filePath, error: msg };
309:   }
310: }
311: 
312: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
313:    7. RECOVERY
314: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
315: 
316: /**
317:  * Find pending files in a directory (crash recovery).
318:  */
319: function listFilesRecursive(dirPath: string): string[] {
320:   const files: string[] = [];
321:   const stack: string[] = [dirPath];
322: 
323:   while (stack.length > 0) {
324:     const current = stack.pop();
325:     if (!current) continue;
326: 
327:     const entries = fs.readdirSync(current, { withFileTypes: true, encoding: 'utf-8' });
328:     for (const entry of entries) {
329:       const fullPath = path.join(current, entry.name);
330:       if (entry.isDirectory()) {
331:         stack.push(fullPath);
332:       } else if (entry.isFile()) {
333:         files.push(fullPath);
334:       }
335:     }
336:   }
337: 
338:   return files;
339: }
340: 
341: function findPendingFiles(dirPath: string): string[] {
342:   try {
343:     if (!fs.existsSync(dirPath)) return [];
344: 
345:     let files: string[];
346:     try {
347:       files = (fs.readdirSync(dirPath, { recursive: true, encoding: 'utf-8' }) as string[]).map((f) => path.join(dirPath, f));
348:     } catch {
349:       // Node 18 compatibility fallback when recursive readdir is unavailable.
350:       files = listFilesRecursive(dirPath);
351:     }
352: 
353:     return files.filter((filePath) => parsePendingPath(filePath) !== null && fs.statSync(filePath).isFile());
354:   } catch (error: unknown) {
355:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
356:     console.warn(`[transaction-manager] findPendingFiles error: ${msg}`);
357:     return [];
358:   }
359: }
360: 
361: /**
362:  * Recover a single pending file by renaming to its original path.
363:  */
364: function recoverPendingFile(
365:   pendingPath: string,
366:   isCommittedInDb?: IsCommittedCheck,
367:   databasePathOverride?: string
368: ): RecoveryResult {
369:   try {
370:     // Guard: verify pending file still exists before attempting recovery
371:     if (!fs.existsSync(pendingPath)) {
372:       return { path: pendingPath, recovered: false, error: 'Pending file no longer exists' };
373:     }
374:     const pendingInfo = parsePendingPath(pendingPath);
375:     if (!pendingInfo) {
376:       return { path: pendingPath, recovered: false, error: 'File does not match pending-file naming contract' };
377:     }
378: 
379:     const originalPath = pendingInfo.originalPath;
380:     const originalExists = fs.existsSync(originalPath);
381:     let committedInDb: boolean | null = null;
382:     if (isCommittedInDb) {
383:       const databasePath = databasePathOverride ?? resolveDatabasePaths().databasePath;
384:       if (!fs.existsSync(databasePath)) {
385:         console.warn(`[transaction-manager] Skipping pending recovery because DB file is missing: ${databasePath}`);
386:         return { path: pendingPath, recovered: false, error: 'Database file missing during recovery' };
387:       }
388:       committedInDb = isCommittedInDb(originalPath);
389:     }
390: 
391:     // If original exists and is newer, delete pending
392:     if (originalExists) {
393:       const pendingStats = fs.statSync(pendingPath);
394:       const originalStats = fs.statSync(originalPath);
395:       if (originalStats.mtimeMs > pendingStats.mtimeMs) {
396:         fs.unlinkSync(pendingPath);
397:         return { path: pendingPath, recovered: false, error: 'Original is newer' };
398:       }
399:     }
400: 
401:     // Stale detection: if DB check is provided and row was never committed,
402:     // Log and leave for manual review instead of renaming.
403:     if (committedInDb === false) {
404:       console.warn(`[transaction-manager] Stale pending file detected (no committed DB row): ${pendingPath}`);
405:       return { path: pendingPath, recovered: false, error: 'Stale pending file: DB row not committed' };
406:     }
407: 
408:     // Rename pending to original
409:     fs.renameSync(pendingPath, originalPath);
410:     metrics.totalRecoveries++;
411:     metrics.lastOperationTime = new Date().toISOString();
412: 
413:     return { path: pendingPath, recovered: true };
414:   } catch (error: unknown) {
415:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
416:     metrics.totalErrors++;
417:     return { path: pendingPath, recovered: false, error: msg };
418:   }
419: }
420: 
421: /**
422:  * Recover all pending files in a directory.
423:  */
424: function recoverAllPendingFiles(
425:   dirPath: string,
426:   isCommittedInDb?: IsCommittedCheck,
427:   databasePathOverride?: string
428: ): RecoveryResult[] {
429:   const pendingFiles = findPendingFiles(dirPath);
430:   return pendingFiles.map((f) => recoverPendingFile(f, isCommittedInDb, databasePathOverride));
431: }
432: 
433: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
434:    8. EXPORTS
435: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
436: 
437: export {
438:   PENDING_SUFFIX,
439:   TEMP_SUFFIX,
440: 
441:   // Metrics
442:   getMetrics,
443:   resetMetrics,
444: 
445:   // Path helpers
446:   getPendingPath,
447:   isPendingFile,
448:   getOriginalPath,
449:   runInTransaction,
450: 
451:   // Atomic operations
452:   atomicWriteFile,
453:   deleteFileIfExists,
454:   executeAtomicSave,
455: 
456:   // Recovery
457:   findPendingFiles,
458:   recoverPendingFile,
459:   recoverAllPendingFiles,
460: };
461: 
462: /**
463:  * Re-exports related public types.
464:  */
465: export type {
466:   TransactionMetrics,
467:   AtomicSaveResult,
468:   RecoveryResult,
469:   IsCommittedCheck,
470: };

(End of file - total 470 lines)
</content>

<content>
1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Job Queue
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // True sequential worker, meaningful state transitions,
5: // Continue-on-error for bulk ingestion, SQLITE_BUSY async retry on DB writes,
6: // Crash recovery with re-enqueue, and original-path progress tracking.
7: 
8: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9:    1. IMPORTS
10: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
11: 
12: import path from 'node:path';
13: import { requireDb, toErrorMessage } from '../../utils/index.js';
14: import type { GovernanceDecision } from '../governance/scope-governance.js';
15: import { withBusyRetry, withBusyRetrySync } from './sqlite-busy-retry.js';
16: 
17: // Feature catalog: Async ingestion job lifecycle
18: 
19: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
20:    2. TYPES
21: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
22: 
23: /**
24:  * Defines the IngestJobState type.
25:  */
26: export type IngestJobState =
27:   | 'queued'
28:   | 'parsing'
29:   | 'embedding'
30:   | 'indexing'
31:   | 'complete'
32:   | 'failed'
33:   | 'cancelled';
34: 
35: /**
36:  * Describes the IngestJobError shape.
37:  */
38: export interface IngestJobError {
39:   filePath: string;
40:   message: string;
41:   timestamp: string;
42: }
43: 
44: /**
45:  * Describes the IngestJob shape.
46:  */
47: export interface IngestJob {
48:   id: string;
49:   state: IngestJobState;
50:   specFolder: string | null;
51:   paths: string[];
52:   filesTotal: number;
53:   filesProcessed: number;
54:   errors: IngestJobError[];
55:   createdAt: string;
56:   updatedAt: string;
57:   // Validated governance decision captured at enqueue time so the worker
58:   // re-indexes each path under the same provenance/retention/scope the
59:   // synchronous save path applies. Null for ungoverned jobs (the default).
60:   governance: GovernanceDecision | null;
61: }
62: 
63: export interface IngestJobForecast {
64:   etaSeconds: number | null;
65:   etaConfidence: number | null;
66:   failureRisk: number | null;
67:   riskSignals: string[];
68:   caveat: string | null;
69: }
70: 
71: interface IngestJobRow {
72:   id: string;
73:   state: IngestJobState;
74:   spec_folder: string | null;
75:   paths_json: string;
76:   files_total: number;
77:   files_processed: number;
78:   errors_json: string;
79:   created_at: string;
80:   updated_at: string;
81:   governance_json?: string | null;
82: }
83: 
84: interface JobQueueConfig {
85:   processFile: (filePath: string, governance?: GovernanceDecision | null) => Promise<unknown>;
86: }
87: 
88: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
89:    3. CONSTANTS
90: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
91: 
92: const ACTIVE_STATES = new Set<IngestJobState>(['queued', 'parsing', 'embedding', 'indexing']);
93: const TERMINAL_STATES = new Set<IngestJobState>(['complete', 'failed', 'cancelled']);
94: 
95: const ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS: Record<IngestJobState, Set<IngestJobState>> = {
96:   queued: new Set<IngestJobState>(['parsing', 'cancelled', 'failed']),
97:   parsing: new Set<IngestJobState>(['embedding', 'cancelled', 'failed']),
98:   embedding: new Set<IngestJobState>(['indexing', 'cancelled', 'failed']),
99:   indexing: new Set<IngestJobState>(['complete', 'cancelled', 'failed']),
100:   complete: new Set<IngestJobState>([]),
101:   failed: new Set<IngestJobState>([]),
102:   cancelled: new Set<IngestJobState>([]),
103: };
104: 
105: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
106:    5. STATE MANAGEMENT
107: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
108: 
109: // True sequential queue — only one job processes at a time.
110: const pendingQueue: string[] = [];
111: let workerActive = false;
112: // Drain fence. Once set, the worker stops draining and refuses to touch the
113: // database. This prevents the worker from reopening a connection that shutdown
114: // has already checkpointed and closed, which would otherwise re-dirty the WAL
115: // and the unclean-shutdown marker at rest.
116: let shuttingDown = false;
117: let processFileFn: ((filePath: string, governance?: GovernanceDecision | null) => Promise<unknown>) | null = null;
118: const MAX_STORED_ERRORS = 50;
119: const MAX_PENDING_INGEST_JOBS = parsePositiveIntEnv('SPECKIT_INGEST_QUEUE_MAX_PENDING', 1_000);
120: 
121: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
122:    4. HELPERS
123: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
124: 
125: function toPublicPathLabel(filePath: string): string {
126:   return filePath === '__job__' ? filePath : path.basename(filePath || '');
127: }
128: 
129: function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
130:   return new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
131: }
132: 
133: function parsePositiveIntEnv(name: string, fallback: number): number {
134:   const raw = process.env[name];
135:   if (!raw) return fallback;
136: 
137:   const parsed = Number.parseInt(raw, 10);
138:   return Number.isFinite(parsed) && parsed > 0 ? parsed : fallback;
139: }
140: 
141: function parseJsonArray<T>(value: string | null | undefined, fallback: T[]): T[] {
142:   if (!value) return fallback;
143:   try {
144:     const parsed = JSON.parse(value) as unknown;
145:     return Array.isArray(parsed) ? (parsed as T[]) : fallback;
146:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
147:     return fallback;
148:   }
149: }
150: 
151: function parseGovernanceJson(value: string | null | undefined): GovernanceDecision | null {
152:   if (!value) return null;
153:   try {
154:     const parsed = JSON.parse(value) as unknown;
155:     if (parsed && typeof parsed === 'object' && 'normalized' in (parsed as Record<string, unknown>)) {
156:       return parsed as GovernanceDecision;
157:     }
158:     return null;
159:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
160:     return null;
161:   }
162: }
163: 
164: function mapRowToJob(row: IngestJobRow | undefined): IngestJob | null {
165:   if (!row) return null;
166:   return {
167:     id: row.id,
168:     state: row.state,
169:     specFolder: row.spec_folder,
170:     paths: parseJsonArray<string>(row.paths_json, []),
171:     filesTotal: row.files_total,
172:     filesProcessed: row.files_processed,
173:     errors: parseJsonArray<IngestJobError>(row.errors_json, []),
174:     createdAt: row.created_at,
175:     updatedAt: row.updated_at,
176:     governance: parseGovernanceJson(row.governance_json),
177:   };
178: }
179: 
180: function nowIso(): string {
181:   return new Date().toISOString();
182: }
183: 
184: function isTransitionAllowed(from: IngestJobState, to: IngestJobState): boolean {
185:   return ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS[from]?.has(to) ?? false;
186: }
187: 
188: // DB accessor for the worker path. While shutting down it refuses to acquire a
189: // connection so the worker can never travel the requireDb -> getDb -> reopen
190: // path after shutdown has closed the database. The throw is caught by the
191: // worker's surrounding error handling and the drain unwinds cleanly.
192: function acquireWorkerDb(): ReturnType<typeof requireDb> {
193:   if (shuttingDown) {
194:     throw new Error('Ingest worker is shutting down; database access refused');
195:   }
196:   return requireDb();
197: }
198: 
199: function ensureIngestJobsTable(): void {
200:   const db = requireDb();
201:   db.exec(`
202:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ingest_jobs (
203:       id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
204:       state TEXT,
205:       spec_folder TEXT,
206:       paths_json TEXT,
207:       files_total INTEGER,
208:       files_processed INTEGER,
209:       errors_json TEXT,
210:       created_at TEXT,
211:       updated_at TEXT,
212:       governance_json TEXT
213:     )
214:   `);
215:   // Backward-compatible migration: add the nullable governance column to tables
216:   // created before governed ingest existed. In-flight jobs simply carry a null
217:   // governance and remain ungoverned, exactly as before.
218:   const columns = db.prepare(`PRAGMA table_info(ingest_jobs)`).all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
219:   if (!columns.some((column) => column.name === 'governance_json')) {
220:     db.exec(`ALTER TABLE ingest_jobs ADD COLUMN governance_json TEXT`);
221:   }
222: }
223: 
224: // Restart interrupted jobs from the beginning on process startup.
225: // Indexing is idempotent, and replaying the original path list avoids
226: // Mismatches when path accessibility changes between runs.
227: function resetIncompleteJobsToQueued(): string[] {
228:   const db = requireDb();
229:   // Collect IDs of jobs that will be reset so we can re-enqueue them.
230:   const rows = db.prepare(`
231:     SELECT id FROM ingest_jobs
232:     WHERE state NOT IN ('complete', 'failed', 'cancelled')
233:   `).all() as Array<{ id: string }>;
234: 
235:   if (rows.length === 0) return [];
236: 
237:   withBusyRetrySync(() =>
238:     db.prepare(`
239:       UPDATE ingest_jobs
240:       SET
241:         state = 'queued',
242:         files_processed = 0,
243:         errors_json = '[]',
244:         updated_at = ?
245:       WHERE state NOT IN ('complete', 'failed', 'cancelled')
246:     `).run(nowIso())
247:   );
248: 
249:   return rows.map((r) => r.id);
250: }
251: 
252: async function createIngestJob(args: {
253:   id: string;
254:   paths: string[];
255:   specFolder?: string;
256:   governance?: GovernanceDecision | null;
257: }): Promise<IngestJob> {
258:   const db = requireDb();
259:   const timestamp = nowIso();
260:   const normalizedPaths = args.paths.map((entry) => String(entry).trim()).filter((entry) => entry.length > 0);
261: 
262:   if (normalizedPaths.length === 0) {
263:     throw new Error('paths must include at least one file path');
264:   }
265: 
266:   const governance = args.governance ?? null;
267:   const governanceJson = governance ? JSON.stringify(governance) : null;
268: 
269:   await withBusyRetry(() =>
270:     db.prepare(`
271:       INSERT INTO ingest_jobs (
272:         id, state, spec_folder, paths_json, files_total, files_processed, errors_json, created_at, updated_at, governance_json
273:       ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
274:     `).run(
275:       args.id,
276:       'queued',
277:       args.specFolder ?? null,
278:       JSON.stringify(normalizedPaths),
279:       normalizedPaths.length,
280:       0,
281:       JSON.stringify([]),
282:       timestamp,
283:       timestamp,
284:       governanceJson,
285:     )
286:   );
287: 
288:   return {
289:     id: args.id,
290:     state: 'queued',
291:     specFolder: args.specFolder ?? null,
292:     paths: normalizedPaths,
293:     filesTotal: normalizedPaths.length,
294:     filesProcessed: 0,
295:     errors: [],
296:     createdAt: timestamp,
297:     updatedAt: timestamp,
298:     governance,
299:   };
300: }
301: 
302: function getIngestJob(jobId: string): IngestJob | null {
303:   const db = acquireWorkerDb();
304:   const row = db.prepare(`
305:     SELECT id, state, spec_folder, paths_json, files_total, files_processed, errors_json, created_at, updated_at, governance_json
306:     FROM ingest_jobs
307:     WHERE id = ?
308:   `).get(jobId) as IngestJobRow | undefined;
309:   return mapRowToJob(row);
310: }
311: 
312: async function setIngestJobState(jobId: string, nextState: IngestJobState): Promise<IngestJob> {
313:   const db = acquireWorkerDb();
314:   const current = getIngestJob(jobId);
315:   if (!current) {
316:     throw new Error(`Ingest job not found: ${jobId}`);
317:   }
318: 
319:   if (current.state === nextState) {
320:     return current;
321:   }
322: 
323:   if (!isTransitionAllowed(current.state, nextState)) {
324:     throw new Error(`Invalid ingest job state transition: ${current.state} -> ${nextState}`);
325:   }
326: 
327:   const updatedAt = nowIso();
328:   const result = await withBusyRetry(() =>
329:     db.prepare(`
330:       UPDATE ingest_jobs
331:       SET state = ?, updated_at = ?
332:       WHERE id = ? AND state = ?
333:     `).run(nextState, updatedAt, jobId, current.state)
334:   );
335: 
336:   if ((result as { changes: number }).changes === 0) {
337:     throw new Error(`State transition conflict: job ${jobId} state was changed by another process (expected '${current.state}')`);
338:   }
339: 
340:   return {
341:     ...current,
342:     state: nextState,
343:     updatedAt,
344:   };
345: }
346: 
347: async function incrementProcessed(jobId: string): Promise<IngestJob> {
348:   const db = acquireWorkerDb();
349:   const updatedAt = nowIso();
350:   await withBusyRetry(() =>
351:     db.prepare(`
352:       UPDATE ingest_jobs
353:       SET files_processed = files_processed + 1, updated_at = ?
354:       WHERE id = ?
355:     `).run(updatedAt, jobId)
356:   );
357: 
358:   const updated = getIngestJob(jobId);
359:   if (!updated) {
360:     throw new Error(`Ingest job not found after progress update: ${jobId}`);
361:   }
362:   return updated;
363: }
364: 
365: async function appendIngestError(jobId: string, filePath: string, error: unknown): Promise<IngestJob> {
366:   const db = acquireWorkerDb();
367:   const current = getIngestJob(jobId);
368:   if (!current) {
369:     throw new Error(`Ingest job not found: ${jobId}`);
370:   }
371: 
372:   let errors = [
373:     ...current.errors,
374:     {
375:       filePath: toPublicPathLabel(filePath),
376:       message: toErrorMessage(error),
377:       timestamp: nowIso(),
378:     },
379:   ];
380: 
381:   if (errors.length > MAX_STORED_ERRORS) {
382:     const tailLength = Math.max(0, MAX_STORED_ERRORS - 1);
383:     const trimmedCount = errors.length - tailLength;
384:     const truncationNotice: IngestJobError = {
385:       filePath: '__job__',
386:       message: `error log truncated (${trimmedCount} older entries removed)`,
387:       timestamp: nowIso(),
388:     };
389:     const truncatedTail = tailLength > 0 ? errors.slice(-tailLength) : [];
390:     errors = [truncationNotice, ...truncatedTail];
391:   }
392: 
393:   const updatedAt = nowIso();
394:   await withBusyRetry(() =>
395:     db.prepare(`
396:       UPDATE ingest_jobs
397:       SET errors_json = ?, updated_at = ?
398:       WHERE id = ?
399:     `).run(JSON.stringify(errors), updatedAt, jobId)
400:   );
401: 
402:   return {
403:     ...current,
404:     errors,
405:     updatedAt,
406:   };
407: }
408: 
409: async function cancelIngestJob(jobId: string): Promise<IngestJob> {
410:   const MAX_CANCEL_RETRIES = 3;
411: 
412:   for (let attempt = 0; attempt < MAX_CANCEL_RETRIES; attempt += 1) {
413:     const current = getIngestJob(jobId);
414:     if (!current) {
415:       throw new Error(`Ingest job not found: ${jobId}`);
416:     }
417:     if (TERMINAL_STATES.has(current.state)) {
418:       return current;
419:     }
420: 
421:     try {
422:       return await setIngestJobState(jobId, 'cancelled');
423:     } catch (error: unknown) {
424:       const message = toErrorMessage(error);
425:       if (message.startsWith('State transition conflict:')) {
426:         continue;
427:       }
428: 
429:       if (message.startsWith('Invalid ingest job state transition:')) {
430:         const latest = getIngestJob(jobId);
431:         if (latest && TERMINAL_STATES.has(latest.state)) {
432:           return latest;
433:         }
434:         continue;
435:       }
436: 
437:       throw error;
438:     }
439:   }
440: 
441:   const latest = getIngestJob(jobId);
442:   if (!latest) {
443:     throw new Error(`Ingest job not found: ${jobId}`);
444:   }
445:   if (TERMINAL_STATES.has(latest.state)) {
446:     return latest;
447:   }
448: 
449:   return setIngestJobState(jobId, 'cancelled');
450: }
451: 
452: /**
453:  * Provides the getIngestProgressPercent helper.
454:  */
455: export function getIngestProgressPercent(job: Pick<IngestJob, 'filesProcessed' | 'filesTotal'>): number {
456:   if (job.filesTotal <= 0) return 0;
457:   const raw = Math.round((job.filesProcessed / job.filesTotal) * 100);
458:   return Math.max(0, Math.min(100, raw));
459: }
460: 
461: function parseIsoTimestamp(timestamp: string): number | null {
462:   if (typeof timestamp !== 'string' || timestamp.trim().length === 0) {
463:     return null;
464:   }
465: 
466:   const parsed = Date.parse(timestamp);
467:   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
468: }
469: 
470: function clampUnitInterval(value: number): number {
471:   if (!Number.isFinite(value)) return 0;
472:   return Math.max(0, Math.min(1, value));
473: }
474: 
475: export function getIngestForecast(
476:   job: Pick<IngestJob, 'state' | 'filesProcessed' | 'filesTotal' | 'errors' | 'createdAt' | 'updatedAt'>,
477: ): IngestJobForecast {
478:   const filesTotal = Math.max(0, job.filesTotal || 0);
479:   const filesProcessed = Math.max(0, job.filesProcessed || 0);
480:   const remaining = Math.max(0, filesTotal - filesProcessed);
481:   const errorCount = Array.isArray(job.errors) ? job.errors.length : 0;
482:   const errorRatio = filesTotal > 0 ? errorCount / filesTotal : 0;
483:   const progressRatio = filesTotal > 0 ? filesProcessed / filesTotal : 0;
484:   const riskSignals: string[] = [];
485: 
486:   if (job.state === 'failed') {
487:     return {
488:       etaSeconds: 0,
489:       etaConfidence: 1,
490:       failureRisk: 1,
491:       riskSignals: ['terminal_failed'],
492:       caveat: null,
493:     };
494:   }
495: 
496:   if (job.state === 'complete') {
497:     return {
498:       etaSeconds: 0,
499:       etaConfidence: 1,
500:       failureRisk: clampUnitInterval(errorRatio * 0.5),
501:       riskSignals: errorCount > 0 ? ['completed_with_file_errors'] : [],
502:       caveat: null,
503:     };
504:   }
505: 
506:   if (job.state === 'cancelled') {
507:     return {
508:       etaSeconds: 0,
509:       etaConfidence: 1,
510:       failureRisk: 0,
511:       riskSignals: ['terminal_cancelled'],
512:       caveat: 'Forecast is advisory only because the job is no longer progressing.',
513:     };
514:   }
515: 
516:   if (filesTotal <= 0) {
517:     return {
518:       etaSeconds: null,
519:       etaConfidence: null,
520:       failureRisk: null,
521:       riskSignals,
522:       caveat: 'Forecast unavailable because the queue has no file-count baseline yet.',
523:     };
524:   }
525: 
526:   if (errorCount > 0) {
527:     riskSignals.push('file_errors_seen');
528:   }
529:   if (job.state === 'queued') {
530:     riskSignals.push('queued_not_started');
531:   }
532: 
533:   const createdAtMs = parseIsoTimestamp(job.createdAt);
534:   const updatedAtMs = parseIsoTimestamp(job.updatedAt);
535:   const elapsedSeconds = createdAtMs !== null && updatedAtMs !== null && updatedAtMs >= createdAtMs
536:     ? Math.max(0, (updatedAtMs - createdAtMs) / 1000)
537:     : 0;
538: 
539:   let etaSeconds: number | null = null;
540:   let etaConfidence: number | null = null;
541:   let caveat: string | null = null;
542: 
543:   if (filesProcessed <= 0 || elapsedSeconds < 1) {
544:     caveat = 'Forecast is low-confidence until at least one file has been processed.';
545:   } else {
546:     const throughput = filesProcessed / elapsedSeconds;
547:     if (throughput > 0) {
548:       etaSeconds = Math.max(0, Math.round(remaining / throughput));
549:       etaConfidence = clampUnitInterval(0.35 + (progressRatio * 0.45) - (errorRatio * 0.25));
550:       if (etaConfidence < 0.4) {
551:         caveat = 'Forecast is low-confidence because queue history is still sparse or noisy.';
552:       }
553:     } else {
554:       caveat = 'Forecast unavailable because throughput could not be derived from queue history.';
555:     }
556:   }
557: 
558:   if (progressRatio <= 0 && job.state !== 'queued') {
559:     riskSignals.push('active_without_progress');
560:   }
561:   if (errorRatio >= 0.5 && errorCount > 0) {
562:     riskSignals.push('high_error_ratio');
563:   }
564: 
565:   const stateRisk = job.state === 'indexing' ? 0.15 : (job.state === 'embedding' ? 0.1 : 0.05);
566:   const failureRisk = clampUnitInterval((errorRatio * 0.7) + stateRisk + (progressRatio === 0 ? 0.05 : 0));
567: 
568:   return {
569:     etaSeconds,
570:     etaConfidence,
571:     failureRisk,
572:     riskSignals,
573:     caveat,
574:   };
575: }
576: 
577: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
578:    6. WORKER
579: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
580: 
581: // Real state machine — states now correspond to actual work phases.
582: // Progress is tracked against the original submitted path list so terminal
583: // Accounting stays stable even when some files are inaccessible.
584: async function processQueuedJob(jobId: string): Promise<void> {
585:   if (!processFileFn) {
586:     throw new Error('Ingest queue not initialized: processFile handler is missing');
587:   }
588: 
589:   let job = getIngestJob(jobId);
590:   if (!job || TERMINAL_STATES.has(job.state)) {
591:     return;
592:   }
593: 
594:   // Step 1: Parsing — validate all paths exist and are readable.
595:   job = await setIngestJobState(jobId, 'parsing');
596:   if (TERMINAL_STATES.has(job.state)) return;
597: 
598:   const { access } = await import('fs/promises');
599: 
600:   // Step 2: Embedding — placeholder for batch embedding pre-processing.
601:   // Transition is meaningful: it signals readiness for indexing after validation.
602:   const latest1 = getIngestJob(jobId);
603:   if (!latest1 || TERMINAL_STATES.has(latest1.state)) return;
604:   job = await setIngestJobState(jobId, 'embedding');
605:   if (TERMINAL_STATES.has(job.state)) return;
606: 
607:   // Step 3: Indexing — process only validated paths (not original paths).
608:   const latest2 = getIngestJob(jobId);
609:   if (!latest2 || TERMINAL_STATES.has(latest2.state)) return;
610:   job = await setIngestJobState(jobId, 'indexing');
611: 
612:   // Resume from filesProcessed against the original submitted path order.
613:   let currentIndex = job.filesProcessed;
614:   // Track actual failure count independently of the truncated errors array.
615:   // The errors array is capped at MAX_STORED_ERRORS, so using errors.length for
616:   // Terminal state decisions would misclassify all-fail jobs with >50 files as "complete".
617:   let failCount = 0;
618: 
619:   while (currentIndex < job.paths.length) {
620:     // Stop touching the DB the moment shutdown begins. The job stays in an
621:     // active state and boot crash-recovery re-enqueues it on the next start.
622:     if (shuttingDown) return;
623:     const current = getIngestJob(jobId);
624:     if (!current) return;
625:     if (current.state === 'cancelled') return;
626: 
627:     const nextPath = current.paths[currentIndex];
628:     if (!nextPath) {
629:       break;
630:     }
631: 
632:     // Continue on file error instead of aborting the entire job.
633:     try {
634:       await access(nextPath);
635:       await processFileFn(nextPath, current.governance);
636:     } catch (error: unknown) {
637:       const normalizedError = error instanceof Error
638:         && 'code' in error
639:         && typeof error.code === 'string'
640:         && error.code === 'ENOENT'
641:         ? new Error('File not accessible')
642:         : error;
643:       await appendIngestError(jobId, nextPath, normalizedError);
644:       failCount += 1;
645:       console.warn(`[job-queue] File error (continuing): ${toPublicPathLabel(nextPath)} — ${toErrorMessage(normalizedError)}`);
646:     }
647: 
648:     job = await incrementProcessed(jobId);
649:     currentIndex = job.filesProcessed;
650:   }
651: 
652:   // Determine terminal state: complete if no errors, failed if all files errored.
653:   const done = getIngestJob(jobId);
654:   if (!done) return;
655:   if (done.state === 'cancelled') return;
656: 
657:   if (failCount > 0 && failCount >= done.filesTotal) {
658:     // All files failed — mark as failed.
659:     await setIngestJobState(jobId, 'failed');
660:   } else {
661:     // Partial or full success — mark as complete (errors are preserved in record).
662:     await setIngestJobState(jobId, 'complete');
663:   }
664: }
665: 
666: // True sequential worker — processes one job at a time.
667: // Multiple enqueueIngestJob calls add to pendingQueue; a single worker drains it.
668: async function drainQueue(): Promise<void> {
669:   if (workerActive) return;
670:   workerActive = true;
671: 
672:   try {
673:     while (pendingQueue.length > 0) {
674:       // Halt draining once shutdown begins so no new job opens the DB.
675:       if (shuttingDown) break;
676:       // PendingQueue.length > 0 in the loop condition guarantees shift() returns a job id
677:       const jobId = pendingQueue.shift()!;
678:       try {
679:         await processQueuedJob(jobId);
680:       } catch (error: unknown) {
681:         try {
682:           await appendIngestError(jobId, '__job__', error);
683:           const current = getIngestJob(jobId);
684:           if (current && ACTIVE_STATES.has(current.state)) {
685:             await setIngestJobState(jobId, 'failed');
686:           }
687:         } catch (_error: unknown) {
688:           // Non-fatal: queue guard to avoid unhandled rejection loops.
689:         }
690:       }
691: 
692:       // Brief yield between jobs to avoid starving the event loop.
693:       await sleep(10);
694:     }
695:   } finally {
696:     workerActive = false;
697:   }
698: }
699: 
700: function enqueueIngestJob(jobId: string): void {
701:   // Never admit work or respawn the worker once shutdown has begun.
702:   if (shuttingDown) {
703:     return;
704:   }
705:   // Prevent duplicate enqueue of the same job.
706:   if (pendingQueue.includes(jobId)) {
707:     return;
708:   }
709: 
710:   pendingQueue.push(jobId);
711:   while (pendingQueue.length > MAX_PENDING_INGEST_JOBS) {
712:     const evictedJobId = pendingQueue.shift();
713:     if (!evictedJobId) break;
714:     void appendIngestError(
715:       evictedJobId,
716:       '__job__',
717:       new Error(`Pending ingest queue exceeded cap ${MAX_PENDING_INGEST_JOBS}; oldest job aborted`),
718:     )
719:       .then(() => cancelIngestJob(evictedJobId))
720:       .catch((error: unknown) => {
721:         console.warn(`[job-queue] Failed to cancel overflow job ${evictedJobId}: ${toErrorMessage(error)}`);
722:       });
723:   }
724: 
725:   // Start worker if not already running (fire-and-forget).
726:   if (!workerActive) {
727:     setImmediate(() => {
728:       void drainQueue().catch((error: unknown) => {
729:         console.error(`[job-queue] Worker error: ${toErrorMessage(error)}`);
730:       });
731:     });
732:   }
733: }
734: 
735: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
736:    7. INITIALIZATION
737: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
738: 
739: function initIngestJobQueue(config: JobQueueConfig): { resetCount: number } {
740:   processFileFn = config.processFile;
741:   ensureIngestJobsTable();
742: 
743:   // Crash recovery: reset incomplete jobs to 'queued' AND re-enqueue them
744:   // So they actually get processed by the worker.
745:   const resetJobIds = resetIncompleteJobsToQueued();
746:   for (const jobId of resetJobIds) {
747:     enqueueIngestJob(jobId);
748:   }
749: 
750:   return { resetCount: resetJobIds.length };
751: }
752: 
753: // Default ceiling for how long stopWorker waits for the active drain to settle.
754: // The worker yields between files and between jobs, so it observes the fence
755: // quickly; the bound only guards against a wedged in-flight processFile call.
756: const STOP_WORKER_DRAIN_DEADLINE_MS = 2_000;
757: 
758: // Fence the ingest worker for shutdown: refuse new work, drop the pending
759: // queue, and wait (bounded) for any in-flight drain to unwind before the
760: // database is closed. Must run before closeDb() so the worker cannot reopen a
761: // closed connection. Idempotent and safe to call when the worker is idle.
762: async function stopWorker(options: { drainDeadlineMs?: number } = {}): Promise<void> {
763:   shuttingDown = true;
764:   pendingQueue.length = 0;
765: 
766:   if (!workerActive) return;
767: 
768:   const deadline = Date.now()
769:     + (Number.isFinite(options.drainDeadlineMs) && (options.drainDeadlineMs as number) > 0
770:       ? (options.drainDeadlineMs as number)
771:       : STOP_WORKER_DRAIN_DEADLINE_MS);
772: 
773:   while (workerActive && Date.now() < deadline) {
774:     await sleep(10);
775:   }
776: }
777: 
778: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
779:    8. EXPORTS
780: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
781: 
782: export {
783:   initIngestJobQueue,
784:   stopWorker,
785:   createIngestJob,
786:   getIngestJob,
787:   cancelIngestJob,
788:   enqueueIngestJob,
789:   resetIncompleteJobsToQueued,
790:   MAX_PENDING_INGEST_JOBS,
791: };

(End of file - total 791 lines)
