1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Reconsolidation
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Reconsolidation-on-Save
5: //
6: // After embedding generation, check top-3 most similar memories
7: // In the spec folder:
8: // - similarity in [0.88, 1.0]: MERGE (duplicate — merge content, boost importance_weight)
9: // - similarity in [0.75, 0.88): CONFLICT (supersede prior memory via causal 'supersedes' edge)
10: // - similarity in [0, 0.75): COMPLEMENT (store new memory unchanged)
11: // Note: cosine similarity, normalized [0,1]. Thresholds defined by MERGE_THRESHOLD and CONFLICT_THRESHOLD constants.
12: //
13: // Behind SPECKIT_RECONSOLIDATION opt-in flag (default OFF)
14: // REQUIRES: checkpoint created before first enable
15: import { createHash } from 'crypto';
16: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
17: import { recordHistory } from './history.js';
18: import * as bm25Index from '../search/bm25-index.js';
19: import { clear_search_cache } from '../search/vector-index-aliases.js';
20: import { refresh_interference_scores_for_folder } from '../search/vector-index-store.js';
21: import { getCanonicalPathKey } from '../utils/canonical-path.js';
22: import { assertNever } from '../utils/exhaustiveness.js';
23: import { delete_memory_from_database } from '../search/vector-index-mutations.js';
24: import { recordLineageTransition, retirePredecessorForActiveReindex } from './lineage-state.js';
25: import {
26:   applyPostInsertMetadata,
27:   type PostInsertMetadataFields,
28: } from './post-insert-metadata.js';
29: 
30: // Feature catalog: Reconsolidation-on-save
31: 
32: 
33: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
34: // 1. TYPES
35: 
36: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
37: /** Action determined by similarity threshold comparison */
38: export type ReconsolidationAction = 'merge' | 'conflict' | 'complement';
39: 
40: /** An existing memory found during similarity search */
41: export interface SimilarMemory {
42:   id: number;
43:   file_path: string;
44:   title: string | null;
45:   content_text: string | null;
46:   similarity: number;
47:   spec_folder: string;
48:   importance_weight?: number;
49:   [key: string]: unknown;
50: }
51: 
52: /** A new memory to be reconsolidated */
53: export interface NewMemoryData {
54:   id?: number;
55:   title: string | null;
56:   content: string;
57:   specFolder: string;
58:   filePath: string;
59:   embedding: Float32Array | number[];
60:   triggerPhrases?: string[];
61:   importanceTier?: string;
62:   importanceWeight?: number;
63: }
64: 
65: /** Result of a merge operation */
66: export interface MergeResult {
67:   action: 'merge';
68:   existingMemoryId: number;
69:   newMemoryId: number;
70:   importanceWeight: number;
71:   mergedContentLength: number;
72:   similarity: number;
73:   warnings?: string[];
74: }
75: 
76: export type MergeAbortStatus = 'predecessor_changed' | 'predecessor_gone';
77: 
78: /** Result when a merge candidate becomes stale before commit */
79: export interface MergeAbortedResult {
80:   action: 'complement';
81:   status: MergeAbortStatus;
82:   existingMemoryId: number;
83:   newMemoryId: number;
84:   similarity: number;
85:   warnings?: string[];
86: }
87: 
88: /** Result of a conflict (supersede) operation */
89: export interface ConflictResult {
90:   action: 'conflict';
91:   existingMemoryId: number;
92:   newMemoryId: number;
93:   causalEdgeId: number | null;
94:   similarity: number;
95:   warnings?: string[];
96: }
97: 
98: export type ConflictAbortStatus = 'conflict_stale_predecessor' | 'scope_retagged';
99: 
100: export interface ConflictAbortedResult {
101:   action: 'conflict';
102:   status: ConflictAbortStatus;
103:   existingMemoryId: number;
104:   newMemoryId: number;
105:   causalEdgeId: null;
106:   similarity: number;
107:   warnings?: string[];
108: }
109: 
110: /** Result of a complement (new) operation */
111: export interface ComplementResult {
112:   action: 'complement';
113:   /**
114:    * Memory ID when already known by caller; 0 means "not persisted yet" and
115:    * caller should execute normal create flow.
116:    */
117:   newMemoryId: number;
118:   similarity: number | null;
119:   warnings?: string[];
120: }
121: 
122: /** Combined reconsolidation result */
123: export type ReconsolidationResult =
124:   | MergeResult
125:   | MergeAbortedResult
126:   | ConflictResult
127:   | ConflictAbortedResult
128:   | ComplementResult;
129: 
130: /** Callback for finding similar memories by embedding */
131: type FindSimilarFn = (
132:   embedding: Float32Array | number[],
133:   options: { limit: number; specFolder?: string }
134: ) => SimilarMemory[];
135: 
136: /** Callback for storing a new memory (complement path) */
137: type StoreMemoryFn = (memory: NewMemoryData) => number;
138: 
139: /** Callback for generating an embedding from content */
140: type GenerateEmbeddingFn = (content: string) => Promise<Float32Array | number[] | null>;
141: 
142: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
143: // 2. CONFIGURATION
144: 
145: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
146: /** Threshold above which memories are merged (near-duplicates) */
147: const MERGE_THRESHOLD = 0.88;
148: 
149: /** Threshold above which memories are in conflict (supersede) */
150: const CONFLICT_THRESHOLD = 0.75;
151: 
152: /** Maximum number of similar memories to check */
153: const SIMILAR_MEMORY_LIMIT = 3;
154: 
155: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
156: // 3. FEATURE FLAG
157: 
158: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
159: // Reconsolidation gate — canonical implementation in search-flags.ts.
160: // Default: ON (graduated). Set SPECKIT_RECONSOLIDATION=false to disable.
161: import { isReconsolidationEnabled } from '../search/search-flags.js';
162: export { isReconsolidationEnabled };
163: 
164: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
165: // 4. SIMILARITY SEARCH
166: 
167: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
168: /**
169:  * Find the top-N most similar memories in a spec folder.
170:  *
171:  * @param embedding - The embedding vector to compare against
172:  * @param specFolder - The spec folder to search within
173:  * @param findSimilar - Callback to find similar memories by embedding
174:  * @param limit - Maximum number of results (default: 3)
175:  * @returns Array of similar memories sorted by similarity DESC
176:  */
177: export function findSimilarMemories(
178:   embedding: Float32Array | number[],
179:   specFolder: string,
180:   findSimilar: FindSimilarFn,
181:   limit: number = SIMILAR_MEMORY_LIMIT
182: ): SimilarMemory[] {
183:   try {
184:     return findSimilar(embedding, { limit, specFolder });
185:   } catch (err: unknown) {
186:     const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
187:     console.warn('[reconsolidation] findSimilarMemories error:', message);
188:     return [];
189:   }
190: }
191: 
192: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
193: // 5. ACTION DETERMINATION
194: 
195: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
196: /**
197:  * Determine the reconsolidation action based on similarity score.
198:  *
199:  * @param similarity - Cosine similarity between new and existing memory
200:  * @returns The action to take: 'merge', 'conflict', or 'complement'
201:  */
202: export function determineAction(similarity: number): ReconsolidationAction {
203:   if (similarity >= MERGE_THRESHOLD) {
204:     return 'merge';
205:   }
206:   if (similarity >= CONFLICT_THRESHOLD) {
207:     return 'conflict';
208:   }
209:   return 'complement';
210: }
211: 
212: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
213: // 6. MERGE OPERATION
214: 
215: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
216: /**
217:  * Merge a new memory into an existing one (similarity >= 0.88).
218:  *
219:  * Combines content by appending new unique sections to the existing
220:  * memory, boosts the importance_weight, and updates the embedding
221:  * to reflect the merged content.
222:  *
223:  * @param existingMemory - The existing memory to merge into
224:  * @param newMemory - The new memory being saved
225:  * @param db - The database instance
226:  * @param generateEmbedding - Optional callback to regenerate embedding for merged content
227:  * @returns MergeResult when merged, or a complement-style abort result when the predecessor changed
228:  */
229: export async function executeMerge(
230:   existingMemory: SimilarMemory,
231:   newMemory: NewMemoryData,
232:   db: Database.Database,
233:   generateEmbedding?: GenerateEmbeddingFn | null
234: ): Promise<MergeResult | MergeAbortedResult> {
235:   const newContent = newMemory.content;
236:   let newId = 0;
237:   let bm25RepairWarning: string | null = null;
238: 
239:   try {
240:     const memoryIndexColumns = getTableColumns(db, 'memory_index');
241:     ensureBm25RepairFlagColumn(db, memoryIndexColumns);
242:     const existingRow = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').get(existingMemory.id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
243: 
244:     if (!existingRow || isArchivedRow(existingRow)) {
245:       return buildMergeAbortResult(existingMemory, newMemory, 'predecessor_gone');
246:     }
247: 
248:     const predecessorVersion = capturePredecessorVersion(existingRow);
249:     const existingContent = getOptionalString(existingRow, 'content_text') ?? '';
250:     const mergedContent = mergeContent(existingContent, newContent);
251:     const mergedHash = createHash('sha256').update(mergedContent, 'utf-8').digest('hex');
252:     const currentWeight = getOptionalNumber(existingRow, 'importance_weight') ?? existingMemory.importance_weight ?? 0.5;
253:     const boostedWeight = Math.min(1.0, currentWeight + 0.1);
254:     const now = new Date().toISOString();
255: 
256:     // Generate embedding BEFORE transaction (async I/O cannot run inside
257:     // Better-sqlite3's synchronous transaction callback).
258:     let newEmbedding: Float32Array | number[] | null = null;
259:     if (generateEmbedding) {
260:       try {
261:         newEmbedding = await generateEmbedding(mergedContent);
262:       } catch (embErr: unknown) {
263:         const msg = embErr instanceof Error ? embErr.message : String(embErr);
264:         console.warn('[reconsolidation] Failed to regenerate embedding for merge:', msg);
265:         // Non-fatal: merged content is stored even without updated embedding
266:       }
267:     }
268: 
269:     let bm25RepairNeeded = false;
270:     let mergedBm25DocumentText = '';
271: 
272:     // F04-001: Append-only merge — mark old as superseded, create new record
273:     const txResult = db.transaction((): { status: 'merged' } | { status: MergeAbortStatus } => {
274:       const currentRow = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').get(existingMemory.id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
275: 
276:       if (!currentRow || isArchivedRow(currentRow)) {
277:         return { status: 'predecessor_gone' };
278:       }
279: 
280:       if (hasPredecessorChanged(predecessorVersion, currentRow)) {
281:         return { status: 'predecessor_changed' };
282:       }
283: 
284:       const currentFilePath = getOptionalString(currentRow, 'file_path') ?? existingMemory.file_path;
285:       const reusedEmbeddingRow = db.prepare(`
286:         SELECT embedding
287:         FROM vec_memories
288:         WHERE rowid = ?
289:       `).get(existingMemory.id) as { embedding?: Buffer } | undefined;
290:       const mergedEmbeddingBuffer = newEmbedding
291:         ? embeddingToBuffer(newEmbedding)
292:         : (reusedEmbeddingRow?.embedding ?? null);
293:       const mergedEmbeddingStatus = mergedEmbeddingBuffer ? 'success' : getOptionalString(currentRow, 'embedding_status') ?? 'pending';
294:       const mergedCanonicalPath = getOptionalString(currentRow, 'canonical_file_path') ?? getCanonicalPathKey(currentFilePath);
295:       const mergedTriggerPhrases = buildMergedTriggerPhrases(currentRow, newMemory.triggerPhrases);
296:       const mergedTitle = newMemory.title ?? getOptionalString(currentRow, 'title') ?? existingMemory.title ?? '';
297:       const mergedImportanceTier = newMemory.importanceTier ?? getOptionalString(currentRow, 'importance_tier');
298: 
299:       mergedBm25DocumentText = bm25Index.buildBm25DocumentText({
300:         title: mergedTitle,
301:         content_text: mergedContent,
302:         trigger_phrases: mergedTriggerPhrases,
303:         file_path: currentFilePath,
304:       });
305: 
306:       const insertValues = buildMergedMemoryInsertValues(currentRow, {
307:         spec_folder: existingMemory.spec_folder,
308:         file_path: currentFilePath,
309:         canonical_file_path: mergedCanonicalPath,
310:         anchor_id: getOptionalString(currentRow, 'anchor_id'),
311:         title: mergedTitle,
312:         trigger_phrases: mergedTriggerPhrases,
313:         importance_weight: boostedWeight,
314:         importance_tier: mergedImportanceTier,
315:         content_text: mergedContent,
316:         content_hash: mergedHash,
317:         embedding_status: mergedEmbeddingStatus,
318:         embedding_model: getOptionalString(currentRow, 'embedding_model'),
319:         embedding_generated_at: mergedEmbeddingBuffer ? now : getOptionalString(currentRow, 'embedding_generated_at'),
320:         encoding_intent: getOptionalString(currentRow, 'encoding_intent'),
321:         document_type: getOptionalString(currentRow, 'document_type'),
322:         spec_level: getOptionalNumber(currentRow, 'spec_level'),
323:         bm25_repair_needed: 0,
324:         source_kind: 'system',
325:         provenance_source: 'reconsolidation',
326:         provenance_actor: 'memory-save',
327:         created_at: now,
328:         updated_at: now,
329:       }, memoryIndexColumns);
330:       // Retire the merged-from predecessor before inserting the merged row so the
331:       // active-row uniqueness guard holds at insert time; lineage and history persist.
332:       retirePredecessorForActiveReindex(db, existingMemory.id);
333: 
334:       const insertColumns = Object.keys(insertValues);
335:       const insertSql = `
336:         INSERT INTO memory_index (${insertColumns.join(', ')})
337:         VALUES (${insertColumns.map(() => '?').join(', ')})
338:       `;
339:       const insertResult = db.prepare(insertSql).run(...insertColumns.map((column) => insertValues[column]));
340: 
341:       newId = Number(insertResult.lastInsertRowid);
342: 
343:       if (mergedEmbeddingBuffer) {
344:         db.prepare(`
345:           INSERT INTO vec_memories (rowid, embedding)
346:           VALUES (?, ?)
347:         `).run(newId, mergedEmbeddingBuffer);
348:       }
349: 
350:       const postInsertMetadata = buildMergePostInsertMetadata(currentRow, {
351:         content_hash: mergedHash,
352:         importance_tier: mergedImportanceTier,
353:         embedding_status: mergedEmbeddingStatus,
354:         document_type: getOptionalString(currentRow, 'document_type'),
355:         spec_level: getOptionalNumber(currentRow, 'spec_level'),
356:         provenance_source: 'reconsolidation',
357:         provenance_actor: 'memory-save',
358:       }, memoryIndexColumns);
359: 
360:       if (Object.keys(postInsertMetadata).length > 0) {
361:         applyPostInsertMetadata(db, newId, postInsertMetadata);
362:       }
363: 
364:       // Create supersedes causal edge, carrying anchors when the table supports them.
365:       const edgeId = insertSupersedesEdge(db, newId, existingMemory.id);
366:       if (edgeId == null) {
367:         throw new Error(`Failed to insert supersedes edge (${newId} -> ${existingMemory.id})`);
368:       }
369: 
370:       recordLineageTransition(db, newId, {
371:         actor: 'mcp:reconsolidation',
372:         predecessorMemoryId: existingMemory.id,
373:         transitionEvent: 'SUPERSEDE',
374:       });
375: 
376:       if (bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
377:         try {
378:           const bm25 = bm25Index.getIndex();
379:           bm25.removeDocument(String(existingMemory.id));
380:           bm25.addDocument(String(newId), mergedBm25DocumentText);
381:         } catch (bm25Err: unknown) {
382:           const message = bm25Err instanceof Error ? bm25Err.message : String(bm25Err);
383:           console.warn('[reconsolidation] Failed to update BM25 index for merge:', message);
384:           bm25RepairNeeded = true;
385:         }
386:       }
387: 
388:       refresh_interference_scores_for_folder(db, existingMemory.spec_folder);
389:       clear_search_cache();
390: 
391:       try {
392:         recordHistory(newId, 'ADD', null, mergedTitle || existingMemory.file_path, 'mcp:reconsolidation', existingMemory.spec_folder);
393:         recordHistory(
394:           existingMemory.id,
395:           'UPDATE',
396:           existingMemory.title ?? existingMemory.file_path,
397:           mergedTitle || existingMemory.file_path,
398:           'mcp:reconsolidation',
399:           // Pass the folder explicitly so the history spec_folder cache is
400:           // refreshed rather than attributing the row to a stale cached folder.
401:           existingMemory.spec_folder,
402:         );
403:       } catch (_historyErr: unknown) {
404:         // Best-effort history tracking during reconsolidation merge
405:       }
406:       return { status: 'merged' };
407:     })();
408: 
409:     if (txResult.status !== 'merged') {
410:       return buildMergeAbortResult(existingMemory, newMemory, txResult.status);
411:     }
412: 
413:     if (bm25RepairNeeded) {
414:       const repairResult = repairBm25Document({
415:         previousMemoryId: existingMemory.id,
416:         memoryId: newId,
417:         documentText: mergedBm25DocumentText,
418:       });
419:       if (!repairResult.success) {
420:         setBm25RepairNeededFlag(db, memoryIndexColumns, newId, true);
421:         bm25RepairWarning =
422:           `BM25 repair failed after reconsolidation merge for memory ${newId}: ${repairResult.error}`;
423:       } else {
424:         setBm25RepairNeededFlag(db, memoryIndexColumns, newId, false);
425:       }
426:     }
427: 
428:     return {
429:       action: 'merge',
430:       existingMemoryId: existingMemory.id,
431:       newMemoryId: newId,
432:       importanceWeight: boostedWeight,
433:       mergedContentLength: mergedContent.length,
434:       similarity: existingMemory.similarity,
435:       warnings: bm25RepairWarning ? [bm25RepairWarning] : undefined,
436:     };
437:   } catch (err: unknown) {
438:     const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
439:     throw new Error(`Merge operation failed: ${message}`);
440:   }
441: }
442: 
443: /**
444:  * Merge two content strings by appending unique new lines.
445:  *
446:  * Splits both contents into lines, then appends lines from the new
447:  * content that are not present in the existing content.
448:  *
449:  * @param existing - The existing memory content
450:  * @param incoming - The new memory content
451:  * @returns The merged content string
452:  */
453: export function mergeContent(existing: string, incoming: string): string {
454:   if (!existing || existing.trim().length === 0) {
455:     return incoming;
456:   }
457:   if (!incoming || incoming.trim().length === 0) {
458:     return existing;
459:   }
460: 
461:   const existingLines = new Set(
462:     existing.split('\n').map(line => line.trim()).filter(line => line.length > 0)
463:   );
464: 
465:   const newLines = incoming
466:     .split('\n')
467:     .map(line => line.trim())
468:     .filter(line => line.length > 0 && !existingLines.has(line));
469: 
470:   if (newLines.length === 0) {
471:     return existing;
472:   }
473: 
474:   return existing + '\n\n<!-- Merged content -->\n' + newLines.join('\n');
475: }
476: 
477: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
478: // 7. CONFLICT OPERATION
479: 
480: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
481: /**
482:  * Resolve a conflict between highly similar memories (similarity 0.75-0.88).
483:  *
484:  * Preferred path (when caller provides a distinct new memory ID):
485:  * - Mark existing memory as deprecated (superseded)
486:  * - Create a 'supersedes' causal edge from new -> existing
487:  *
488:  * Legacy fallback (when no new ID is available):
489:  * - Update existing memory content/title in-place
490:  * - Skip edge creation (avoids self-referential edges)
491:  *
492:  * @param existingMemory - The existing memory being superseded
493:  * @param newMemory - The new memory replacing it
494:  * @param db - The database instance
495:  * @returns ConflictResult with supersede details
496:  */
497: export function executeConflict(
498:   existingMemory: SimilarMemory,
499:   newMemory: NewMemoryData,
500:   db: Database.Database
501: ): ConflictResult | ConflictAbortedResult {
502:   try {
503:     // Add causal 'supersedes' edge only when caller provides a distinct new ID.
504:     // Prevent self-referential supersedes edges (source == target).
505:     let edgeId: number | null = null;
506:     const baselineRow = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').get(existingMemory.id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
507:     if (!baselineRow || isArchivedRow(baselineRow)) {
508:       return buildConflictAbortResult(existingMemory, newMemory, 'conflict_stale_predecessor');
509:     }
510:     const predecessorSnapshot = capturePredecessorSnapshot(existingMemory, baselineRow);
511:     const hasDistinctNewId =
512:       typeof newMemory.id === 'number' &&
513:       Number.isFinite(newMemory.id) &&
514:       newMemory.id !== existingMemory.id;
515: 
516:     if (hasDistinctNewId) {
517:       const abortStatus = executeAtomicReconsolidationTxn(
518:         db,
519:         existingMemory,
520:         predecessorSnapshot,
521:         {
522:           mode: 'deprecate',
523:           apply: () => {
524:             const updateResult = db.prepare(`
525:               UPDATE memory_index
526:               SET importance_tier = 'deprecated',
527:                   updated_at = datetime('now')
528:               WHERE id = @id
529:                 AND importance_tier != 'deprecated'
530:                 AND ((content_hash = @contentHash) OR (content_hash IS NULL AND @contentHash IS NULL))
531:             `).run({
532:               id: existingMemory.id,
533:               contentHash: predecessorSnapshot.contentHash,
534:             }) as { changes: number };
535: 
536:             if (updateResult.changes === 0) {
537:               return false;
538:             }
539: 
540:             const sourceId = newMemory.id;
541:             const targetId = existingMemory.id;
542:             if (sourceId == null || targetId == null) {
543:               throw new Error('Reconsolidation source/target memory missing id — aborting');
544:             }
545:             edgeId = insertSupersedesEdge(db, sourceId, targetId);
546:             if (edgeId == null) {
547:               throw new Error(
548:                 `Failed to insert supersedes edge (${sourceId} -> ${targetId}) — aborting reconsolidation`
549:               );
550:             }
551:             return true;
552:           },
553:         },
554:       );
555:       if (abortStatus) {
556:         return buildConflictAbortResult(existingMemory, newMemory, abortStatus);
557:       }
558:     } else {
559:       // Atomic transaction: content + embedding + hash update together.
560:       const updatedHash = createHash('sha256').update(newMemory.content, 'utf-8').digest('hex');
561:       const abortStatus = executeAtomicReconsolidationTxn(
562:         db,
563:         existingMemory,
564:         predecessorSnapshot,
565:         {
566:           mode: 'content_update',
567:           apply: () => {
568:             const updateResult = db.prepare(`
569:               UPDATE memory_index
570:               SET content_text = ?,
571:                   title = ?,
572:                   content_hash = ?,
573:                   updated_at = datetime('now')
574:               WHERE id = ?
575:                 AND importance_tier != 'deprecated'
576:                 AND ((content_hash = ?) OR (content_hash IS NULL AND ? IS NULL))
577:             `).run(
578:               newMemory.content,
579:               newMemory.title,
580:               updatedHash,
581:               existingMemory.id,
582:               predecessorSnapshot.contentHash,
583:               predecessorSnapshot.contentHash,
584:             ) as { changes: number };
585:             if (updateResult.changes === 0) {
586:               return false;
587:             }
588: 
589:             if (newMemory.embedding) {
590:               const buffer = embeddingToBuffer(newMemory.embedding);
591:               db.prepare(
592:                 'UPDATE vec_memories SET embedding = ? WHERE rowid = ?'
593:               ).run(buffer, existingMemory.id);
594:             }
595:             return true;
596:           },
597:         },
598:       );
599:       if (abortStatus) {
600:         return buildConflictAbortResult(existingMemory, newMemory, abortStatus);
601:       }
602:     }
603: 
604:     return {
605:       action: 'conflict',
606:       existingMemoryId: existingMemory.id,
607:       newMemoryId: newMemory.id ?? existingMemory.id,
608:       causalEdgeId: edgeId,
609:       similarity: existingMemory.similarity,
610:     };
611:   } catch (err: unknown) {
612:     const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
613:     throw new Error(`Conflict operation failed: ${message}`);
614:   }
615: }
616: 
617: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
618: // 8. COMPLEMENT OPERATION
619: 
620: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
621: /**
622:  * Store a new memory unchanged (similarity < 0.75).
623:  *
624:  * The new memory is distinct enough from existing memories to be
625:  * stored as a separate entry.
626:  *
627:  * @param newMemory - The new memory to store
628:  * @param storeMemory - Callback to store the memory
629:  * @param topSimilarity - The highest similarity score found (null if no candidates)
630:  * @returns ComplementResult with storage details
631:  */
632: export function executeComplement(
633:   newMemory: NewMemoryData,
634:   storeMemory: StoreMemoryFn,
635:   topSimilarity: number | null
636: ): ComplementResult {
637:   const newMemoryId = storeMemory(newMemory);
638: 
639:   return {
640:     action: 'complement',
641:     newMemoryId,
642:     similarity: topSimilarity,
643:   };
644: }
645: 
646: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
647: // 9. RECONSOLIDATION ORCHESTRATOR
648: 
649: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
650: /** Options for the reconsolidation orchestrator */
651: export interface ReconsolidateOptions {
652:   findSimilar: FindSimilarFn;
653:   storeMemory: StoreMemoryFn;
654:   generateEmbedding?: GenerateEmbeddingFn | null;
655: }
656: 
657: /**
658:  * Orchestrate the full reconsolidation flow for a new memory.
659:  *
660:  * 1. Find top-3 most similar memories in the spec folder
661:  * 2. Determine action based on highest similarity score
662:  * 3. Execute the appropriate operation (merge/conflict/complement)
663:  *
664:  * When the feature flag is OFF, returns null (caller should use
665:  * normal store path).
666:  *
667:  * @param newMemory - The new memory to reconsolidate
668:  * @param db - The database instance
669:  * @param options - Callbacks for similarity search and storage
670:  * @returns ReconsolidationResult or null if feature is disabled
671:  */
672: export async function reconsolidate(
673:   newMemory: NewMemoryData,
674:   db: Database.Database,
675:   options: ReconsolidateOptions
676: ): Promise<ReconsolidationResult | null> {
677:   if (!isReconsolidationEnabled()) {
678:     return null;
679:   }
680: 
681:   const { findSimilar, storeMemory, generateEmbedding } = options;
682: 
683:   // Step 1: Find similar memories
684:   const similarMemories = findSimilarMemories(
685:     newMemory.embedding,
686:     newMemory.specFolder,
687:     findSimilar
688:   );
689: 
690:   // No existing memories: complement (new)
691:   if (similarMemories.length === 0) {
692:     // Do not persist in orchestrator complement path.
693:     // Caller owns canonical create flow (prevents duplicate writes).
694:     return {
695:       action: 'complement',
696:       newMemoryId: newMemory.id ?? 0,
697:       similarity: null,
698:     };
699:   }
700: 
701:   // Step 2: Get the most similar memory and determine action
702:   const topMatch = similarMemories[0];
703:   const action = determineAction(topMatch.similarity);
704: 
705:   // Step 3: Execute action
706:   switch (action) {
707:     case 'merge':
708:       return executeMerge(topMatch, newMemory, db, generateEmbedding);
709: 
710:     case 'conflict':
711:       {
712:         let conflictMemory = newMemory;
713: 
714:         // F04-002: Wrap store + conflict in single transaction for atomicity
715:         // live-save path: materialize memory + supersede edge together
716:         try {
717:           const conflictTx = db.transaction(() => {
718:             if (conflictMemory.id === undefined) {
719:               const storedId = storeMemory(newMemory);
720:               if (
721:                 typeof storedId === 'number' &&
722:                 Number.isFinite(storedId) &&
723:                 storedId > 0 &&
724:                 storedId !== topMatch.id
725:               ) {
726:                 conflictMemory = { ...newMemory, id: storedId };
727:               }
728:             }
729:             return executeConflict(topMatch, conflictMemory, db);
730:           });
731:           return conflictTx();
732:         } catch (conflictErr: unknown) {
733:           // If storeMemory succeeded but executeConflict failed, clean up the orphan
734:           // Memory so we don't leave dangling rows with no supersedes edge.
735:           if (conflictMemory.id !== undefined && conflictMemory.id !== newMemory.id) {
736:             // Graph cleanup: Use delete_memory_from_database (includes deleteAncillaryMemoryRows)
737:             // instead of raw DELETE to clean lineage, projections, and graph residue.
738:             try {
739:               const deleted = delete_memory_from_database(db, conflictMemory.id);
740:               if (deleted) {
741:                 try {
742:                   recordHistory(
743:                     conflictMemory.id!,
744:                     'DELETE',
745:                     null,
746:                     null,
747:                     'mcp:reconsolidation_cleanup',
748:                     conflictMemory.specFolder ?? null,
749:                   );
750:                 } catch (_histErr: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
751:               }
752:             } catch (_error: unknown) {
753:               // Best-effort cleanup
754:             }
755:             console.warn('[reconsolidation] cleaned up orphan memory', conflictMemory.id, 'after executeConflict failure');
756:           }
757:           throw conflictErr;
758:         }
759:       }
760: 
761:     case 'complement':
762:       // Complement is a routing decision only; caller persists once.
763:       return {
764:         action: 'complement',
765:         newMemoryId: newMemory.id ?? 0,
766:         similarity: topMatch.similarity,
767:       };
768: 
769:     default:
770:       // Exhaustive check
771:       return {
772:         action: 'complement',
773:         newMemoryId: newMemory.id ?? 0,
774:         similarity: topMatch.similarity,
775:       };
776:   }
777: }
778: 
779: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
780: // 10. HELPERS
781: 
782: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
783: /**
784:  * Convert an embedding array to a Buffer for SQLite storage.
785:  *
786:  * @param embedding - The embedding as Float32Array or number[]
787:  * @returns Buffer representation of the embedding
788:  */
789: function embeddingToBuffer(embedding: Float32Array | number[]): Buffer {
790:   if (embedding instanceof Float32Array) {
791:     return Buffer.from(embedding.buffer, embedding.byteOffset, embedding.byteLength);
792:   }
793:   return Buffer.from(new Float32Array(embedding).buffer);
794: }
795: 
796: function repairBm25Document(args: {
797:   previousMemoryId?: number;
798:   memoryId: number;
799:   documentText: string;
800: }): { success: boolean; error?: string } {
801:   if (!bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
802:     return { success: true };
803:   }
804: 
805:   try {
806:     const bm25 = bm25Index.getIndex();
807:     if (typeof args.previousMemoryId === 'number') {
808:       bm25.removeDocument(String(args.previousMemoryId));
809:     }
810:     bm25.removeDocument(String(args.memoryId));
811:     bm25.addDocument(String(args.memoryId), args.documentText);
812:     return { success: true };
813:   } catch (error: unknown) {
814:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
815:     console.warn('[reconsolidation] Immediate BM25 repair failed:', message);
816:     return {
817:       success: false,
818:       error: message,
819:     };
820:   }

210: function buildScopePrefix(row: {
211:   tenant_id?: unknown;
212:   user_id?: unknown;
213:   agent_id?: unknown;
214:   session_id?: unknown;

216:   const scopeTuple = [
217:     ['tenant', normalizeScopeValue(row.tenant_id)],
218:     ['user', normalizeScopeValue(row.user_id)],
219:     ['agent', normalizeScopeValue(row.agent_id)],
220:     ['session', normalizeScopeValue(row.session_id)],

223:   if (scopeTuple.length === 0) {
224:     return null;
225:   }
226: 
227:   const scopeHash = createHash('sha256')
228:     .update(JSON.stringify(scopeTuple), 'utf8')
229:     .digest('hex')
230:     .slice(0, 24);
231: 
232:   return `scope-sha256:${scopeHash}`;
233: }

243:   anchorId: string;

245:   const payload = JSON.stringify({

247:     specFolder: parts.specFolder,
248:     scopePrefix: parts.scopePrefix,
249:     canonicalPath: parts.canonicalPath,
250:     anchorId: parts.anchorId,
251:   });
252:   const digest = createHash('sha256')
253:     .update(payload, 'utf8')
254:     .digest('hex');
255:   return `logical-sha256:${digest}`;

258: export function buildMemoryLogicalKey(parts: {
259:   specFolder: string;
260:   filePath: string;
261:   canonicalFilePath?: string | null;
262:   anchorId?: string | null;
263:   tenant_id?: string | null;
264:   user_id?: string | null;
265:   agent_id?: string | null;
266:   session_id?: string | null;
267: }): string {
268:   const canonicalPath = typeof parts.canonicalFilePath === 'string' && parts.canonicalFilePath.trim().length > 0
269:     ? parts.canonicalFilePath.trim()
270:     : getCanonicalPathKey(parts.filePath);
271:   const anchorId = typeof parts.anchorId === 'string' && parts.anchorId.trim().length > 0
272:     ? parts.anchorId.trim()
273:     : '_';
274:   const scopePrefix = buildScopePrefix(parts);
275: 
276:   if (hasLogicalKeySeparatorCollision(parts.specFolder, canonicalPath, anchorId)) {
277:     return buildHashedLogicalKey({
278:       specFolder: parts.specFolder,
279:       scopePrefix,
280:       canonicalPath,
281:       anchorId,
282:     });
283:   }
284: 
285:   if (!scopePrefix) {
286:     return `${parts.specFolder}::${canonicalPath}::${anchorId}`;
287:   }
288:   return `${parts.specFolder}::${scopePrefix}::${canonicalPath}::${anchorId}`;

353: function isLogicalVersionConflict(error: unknown): boolean {
354:   const message = error instanceof Error
355:     ? error.message
356:     : typeof error === 'object' && error !== null && typeof Reflect.get(error, 'message') === 'string'
357:       ? String(Reflect.get(error, 'message'))
358:       : String(error);

364:     (code.includes('SQLITE_CONSTRAINT') || message.includes('SQLITE_CONSTRAINT') || message.includes('UNIQUE constraint failed'))
365:     && message.includes('memory_lineage.logical_key')
366:     && message.includes('memory_lineage.version_number')

426: function upsertActiveProjection(
427:   database: Database.Database,
428:   logicalKey: string,
429:   rootMemoryId: number,
430:   activeMemoryId: number,
431:   updatedAt: string,
432: ): void {
433:   // Evict any stale projection row that maps a *different* logical_key to the
434:   // same active_memory_id — prevents UNIQUE constraint violation on re-index
435:   // when the logical_key changes (e.g. anchor or path normalization drift).
436:   database.prepare(
437:     'DELETE FROM active_memory_projection WHERE active_memory_id = ? AND logical_key != ?',
438:   ).run(activeMemoryId, logicalKey);
439:   database.prepare(`
440:     INSERT INTO active_memory_projection (logical_key, root_memory_id, active_memory_id, updated_at)
441:     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
442:     ON CONFLICT(logical_key) DO UPDATE SET
443:       root_memory_id = excluded.root_memory_id,
444:       active_memory_id = excluded.active_memory_id,
445:       updated_at = excluded.updated_at
446:   `).run(logicalKey, rootMemoryId, activeMemoryId, updatedAt);
447: }
448: 
449: /**
450:  * Admit archived/cold (deprecated-tier) rows into `active_memory_projection` so the
451:  * vector (semantic) lane — which joins the projection — can reach them. Option A:
452:  * only rows whose logical key has NO active winner already in the projection, so the
453:  * one-active-per-logical-key UNIQUE invariant is preserved and superseded
454:  * dedup-losers are not surfaced. Picks the most recent embedded row per key.
455:  *
456:  * Uses `buildLogicalKey` (the same key builder the incremental upsert uses) — a
457:  * SQL key expression would not match the stored `logical_key` format. Idempotent:
458:  * keys already present are skipped, so re-running admits nothing new.
459:  *
460:  * Opt-in (default OFF) via SPECKIT_INCLUDE_ARCHIVED_VECTOR — a live projection
461:  * mutation whose correctness is confirmed against the running daemon.
462:  */
463: export function backfillColdOrphanProjection(
464:   database: Database.Database,
465: ): { scanned: number; admitted: number; skippedActiveKey: number } {
466:   if (!isArchivedVectorInclusionEnabled()) {
467:     return { scanned: 0, admitted: 0, skippedActiveKey: 0 };
468:   }
469:   let rows: MemoryIndexRow[];
470:   try {
471:     rows = database.prepare(`
472:       SELECT id, spec_folder, file_path, canonical_file_path, anchor_id,
473:              tenant_id, user_id, agent_id, session_id, updated_at
474:       FROM memory_index
475:       WHERE COALESCE(importance_tier, 'normal') = 'deprecated'
476:         AND embedding_status = 'success'
477:       ORDER BY updated_at DESC, id DESC
478:     `).all() as MemoryIndexRow[];
479:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
480:     // Legacy DBs without the columns/tables — nothing to backfill.
481:     return { scanned: 0, admitted: 0, skippedActiveKey: 0 };
482:   }
483: 
484:   const insert = database.prepare(`
485:     INSERT OR IGNORE INTO active_memory_projection (logical_key, root_memory_id, active_memory_id, updated_at)
486:     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
487:   `);
488:   const seen = new Set<string>();
489:   let admitted = 0;
490:   let skippedActiveKey = 0;
491: 
492:   const run = database.transaction(() => {
493:     for (const row of rows) {
494:       const logicalKey = buildLogicalKey(row);
495:       if (seen.has(logicalKey)) continue; // already admitted the best orphan for this key
496:       seen.add(logicalKey);
497:       if (getActiveProjection(database, logicalKey)) {
498:         // A non-deprecated winner already owns this key — option A leaves it alone.
499:         skippedActiveKey += 1;
500:         continue;
501:       }
502:       const id = Number(row.id);
503:       const updatedAt = typeof row.updated_at === 'string' ? row.updated_at : String(row.updated_at ?? '');
504:       const result = insert.run(logicalKey, id, id, updatedAt) as { changes: number };
505:       if (result.changes > 0) admitted += 1;
506:     }
507:   });
508:   run();
509: 
510:   return { scanned: rows.length, admitted, skippedActiveKey };
511: }
512: 
513: function bindHistory(database: Database.Database): void {
514:   if (typeof (database as Database.Database & { exec?: unknown }).exec === 'function') {
515:     initHistory(database);
516:   }
517: }
518: 
519: function markHistoricalPredecessor(database: Database.Database, memoryId: number, updatedAt: string): void {
520:   database.prepare(`
521:     UPDATE memory_index
522:     SET importance_tier = CASE
523:           WHEN importance_tier = 'constitutional' THEN importance_tier
524:           ELSE 'deprecated'
525:         END,
526:         updated_at = ?
527:     WHERE id = ?
528:   `).run(updatedAt, memoryId);
529: }

531: function insertAppendOnlyMemoryIndexRow(params: CreateAppendOnlyMemoryRecordParams): number {
532:   const { database, parsed, filePath, embedding, embeddingFailureReason } = params;
533:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
534:   const canonicalFilePath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
535:   const tenantId = normalizeScopeValue(params.tenantId);
536:   const userId = normalizeScopeValue(params.userId);
537:   const agentId = normalizeScopeValue(params.agentId);
538:   const sessionId = normalizeScopeValue(params.sessionId);
539:   const importanceWeight = calculateDocumentWeight(filePath, parsed.documentType);
540:   const specLevel = isSpecDocumentType(parsed.documentType)
541:     ? detectSpecLevelFromParsed(filePath)
542:     : null;
543:   const encodingIntent = isEncodingIntentEnabled()
544:     ? classifyEncodingIntent(parsed.content)
545:     : undefined;
546:   const embeddingStatus = embedding
547:     ? (sqlite_vec_available() ? 'success' : 'pending')
548:     : 'pending';
549: 
550:   const result = database.prepare(`
551:     INSERT INTO memory_index (
552:       spec_folder,
553:       file_path,
554:       canonical_file_path,
555:       anchor_id,
556:       title,
557:       trigger_phrases,
558:       importance_weight,
559:       created_at,
560:       updated_at,
561:       embedding_model,
562:       embedding_generated_at,
563:       embedding_status,
564:       failure_reason,
565:       encoding_intent,
566:       document_type,
567:       spec_level,
568:       content_text,
569:       quality_score,
570:       quality_flags,
571:       tenant_id,
572:       user_id,
573:       agent_id,
574:       session_id
575:     ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
576:   `).run(
577:     parsed.specFolder,
578:     filePath,
579:     canonicalFilePath,
580:     params.anchorId ?? null,
581:     parsed.title,
582:     JSON.stringify(parsed.triggerPhrases),
583:     importanceWeight,
584:     now,
585:     now,
586:     embeddingsProvider.getModelName(),
587:     embedding ? now : null,
588:     embeddingStatus,
589:     embeddingFailureReason,
590:     encodingIntent ?? 'document',
591:     parsed.documentType || 'memory',
592:     specLevel,
593:     parsed.content,
594:     parsed.qualityScore ?? 0,
595:     JSON.stringify(parsed.qualityFlags ?? []),
596:     tenantId,
597:     userId,
598:     agentId,
599:     sessionId,
600:   );
601: 
602:   const memoryId = Number(result.lastInsertRowid);
603:   applyPostInsertMetadata(database, memoryId, {
604:     content_hash: parsed.contentHash,
605:     context_type: parsed.contextType,
606:     importance_tier: parsed.importanceTier,
607:     memory_type: parsed.memoryType,
608:     type_inference_source: parsed.memoryTypeSource,
609:     encoding_intent: encodingIntent,
610:     document_type: parsed.documentType || 'memory',
611:     spec_level: specLevel,
612:     quality_score: parsed.qualityScore ?? 0,
613:     quality_flags: JSON.stringify(parsed.qualityFlags ?? []),
614:   });
615: 
616:   if (embedding && sqlite_vec_available()) {
617:     const expectedDim = get_embedding_dim();
618:     if (embedding.length !== expectedDim) {
619:       throw new Error(`Embedding must be ${expectedDim} dimensions, got ${embedding.length}`);
620:     }
621: 
622:     database.prepare(`
623:       INSERT INTO vec_memories (rowid, embedding)
624:       VALUES (?, ?)
625:     `).run(BigInt(memoryId), to_embedding_buffer(embedding));
626:   }
627: 
628:   if (bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
629:     try {
630:       bm25Index.getIndex().addDocument(String(memoryId), bm25Index.buildBm25DocumentText({
631:         title: parsed.title,
632:         content_text: parsed.content,
633:         trigger_phrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
634:         file_path: filePath,
635:       }));
636:     } catch (error: unknown) {
637:       logger.warn(`BM25 index update failed for memory ${memoryId}: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`);
638:     }
639:   }
640: 
641:   try {
642:     recordHistory(memoryId, 'ADD', null, parsed.title ?? filePath, params.actor ?? 'mcp:memory_save');
643:   } catch (error: unknown) {
644:     logger.warn(`History record failed for memory ${memoryId}: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`);
645:   }
646: 
647:   refresh_interference_scores_for_folder(database, parsed.specFolder);
648:   return memoryId;
649: }

651: // Shared early-return helper for lineage functions.
652: function getExistingLineageTransition(
653:   database: Database.Database,
654:   memoryId: number,
655: ): RecordedLineageTransition | null {
656:   const existing = getLineageRow(database, memoryId);
657:   if (existing) {
658:     return {
659:       logicalKey: existing.logical_key,
660:       versionNumber: existing.version_number,
661:       rootMemoryId: existing.root_memory_id,
662:       activeMemoryId: existing.memory_id,
663:       predecessorMemoryId: existing.predecessor_memory_id,
664:       transitionEvent: existing.transition_event,
665:     };
666:   }
667:   return null;
668: }
669: 
670: /**
671:  * Validates transition input combinations before persisting.
672:  * Rejects invalid event/predecessor pairings and backwards timestamps.
673:  */
674: function validateTransitionInput(
675:   transitionEvent: string,
676:   predecessorMemoryId: number | null,
677:   validFrom: string,
678:   predecessor: LineageRow | null,
679: ): void {
680:   if (transitionEvent === 'CREATE' && predecessorMemoryId != null) {
681:     throw new Error(`E_LINEAGE: CREATE transition must not specify a predecessor (got ${predecessorMemoryId})`);
682:   }
683:   if (transitionEvent === 'SUPERSEDE' && predecessorMemoryId == null) {
684:     throw new Error('E_LINEAGE: SUPERSEDE transition requires a predecessor');
685:   }
686:   // Compare timestamps as numeric epoch values, not raw strings,
687:   // to handle non-ISO strings and timezone-offset variants correctly.
688:   if (predecessor && new Date(validFrom).getTime() < new Date(predecessor.valid_from).getTime()) {
689:     throw new Error(
690:       `E_LINEAGE: valid_from (${validFrom}) is earlier than predecessor valid_from (${predecessor.valid_from})`,
691:     );
692:   }
693: }
694: 
695: /**
696:  * Seed lineage state from an existing memory row when no lineage entry exists yet.
697:  *
698:  * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
699:  * @param memoryId - Memory version to seed into lineage tables.
700:  * @param options - Optional actor, timestamps, and transition metadata.
701:  * @returns Seeded lineage state for the requested memory version.
702:  */
703: export function seedLineageFromCurrentState(
704:   database: Database.Database,
705:   memoryId: number,
706:   options: RecordLineageTransitionOptions = {},
707: ): RecordedLineageTransition {
708:   bindHistory(database);
709:   ensureLineageTables(database);
710: 
711:   const cached = getExistingLineageTransition(database, memoryId);
712:   if (cached) return cached;
713: 
714:   const row = getMemoryRow(database, memoryId);
715:   const logicalKey = buildLogicalKey(row);
716:   const actor = options.actor ?? 'system';
717:   const historyEvents = options.historyEvents ?? getSafeHistoryEvents(database, memoryId);
718:   const validFrom = options.validFrom
719:     ?? historyEvents[0]?.timestamp
720:     ?? normalizeTimestamp(row.created_at ?? row.updated_at);
721: 
722:   const seedTx = database.transaction(() => {
723:     database.prepare(`
724:       INSERT INTO memory_lineage (
725:         memory_id,
726:         logical_key,
727:         version_number,
728:         root_memory_id,
729:         predecessor_memory_id,
730:         superseded_by_memory_id,
731:         valid_from,
732:         valid_to,
733:         transition_event,
734:         actor,
735:         metadata
736:       ) VALUES (?, ?, 1, ?, NULL, NULL, ?, NULL, ?, ?, ?)
737:     `).run(
738:       memoryId,
739:       logicalKey,
740:       memoryId,
741:       validFrom,
742:       options.transitionEvent ?? 'BACKFILL',
743:       actor,
744:       buildMetadata(row, actor, historyEvents),
745:     );
746: 
747:     upsertActiveProjection(database, logicalKey, memoryId, memoryId, normalizeTimestamp(row.updated_at ?? validFrom));
748:   });
749: 
750:   seedTx();
751: 
752:   return {
753:     logicalKey,
754:     versionNumber: 1,
755:     rootMemoryId: memoryId,
756:     activeMemoryId: memoryId,
757:     predecessorMemoryId: null,
758:     transitionEvent: options.transitionEvent ?? 'BACKFILL',
759:   };
760: }
761: 
762: /**
763:  * Seed or append a lineage transition for a memory version.
764:  *
765:  * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
766:  * @param memoryId - Memory version being recorded.
767:  * @param options - Transition details such as predecessor and actor.
768:  * @returns Recorded lineage state for the requested memory version.
769:  */
770: export function recordLineageTransition(
771:   database: Database.Database,
772:   memoryId: number,
773:   options: RecordLineageTransitionOptions = {},
774: ): RecordedLineageTransition {
775:   bindHistory(database);
776:   ensureLineageTables(database);
777: 
778:   const cached = getExistingLineageTransition(database, memoryId);
779:   if (cached) return cached;
780: 
781:   for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= MAX_LINEAGE_VERSION_RETRIES; attempt += 1) {
782:     // Wrap predecessor UPDATE + lineage INSERT + projection UPSERT in a transaction.
783:     const recordTransitionTx = database.transaction(() => {
784:       const row = getMemoryRow(database, memoryId);
785:       const rowLogicalKey = buildLogicalKey(row);
786:       const actor = options.actor ?? 'system';
787:       const transitionEvent = options.transitionEvent ?? 'CREATE';
788:       const historyEvents = options.historyEvents ?? getSafeHistoryEvents(database, memoryId);
789:       const predecessorMemoryId = options.predecessorMemoryId ?? null;
790:       const validFrom = options.validFrom ?? normalizeTimestamp(row.updated_at ?? row.created_at);
791: 
792:       let logicalKey = rowLogicalKey;
793:       let rootMemoryId = memoryId;
794:       let versionNumber = 1;
795:       let predecessor: LineageRow | null = null;
796: 
797:       if (predecessorMemoryId != null) {
798:         predecessor = getLineageRow(database, predecessorMemoryId);
799:         if (predecessor) {
800:           if (predecessor.logical_key !== rowLogicalKey) {
801:             // The predecessor's lineage logical_key can go stale when its file is moved/renamed
802:             // (a spec-folder reorg updates memory_index.spec_folder/file_path in place but leaves
803:             // the older memory_lineage row's logical_key untouched). Re-check against the
804:             // predecessor's CURRENT row identity: if that matches, this is the same logical file
805:             // with a stale key — start a fresh chain under the current identity (the supersede
806:             // block below still deprecates the old predecessor). If it still differs, the memories
807:             // are genuinely distinct and the cross-identity link is rejected.
808:             const predecessorCurrentKey = buildLogicalKey(getMemoryRow(database, predecessorMemoryId));
809:             if (predecessorCurrentKey !== rowLogicalKey) {
810:               throw new Error(
811:                 `E_LINEAGE: predecessor ${predecessorMemoryId} logical identity ${predecessor.logical_key} ` +
812:                 `does not match memory ${memoryId} logical identity ${rowLogicalKey}`,
813:               );
814:             }
815:             logger.warn(
816:               `Lineage logical_key for predecessor ${predecessorMemoryId} is stale ` +
817:               `(${predecessor.logical_key} -> ${rowLogicalKey}); re-rooting chain under current identity`,
818:             );
819:             logicalKey = rowLogicalKey;
820:             rootMemoryId = memoryId;
821:             versionNumber = 1;
822:           } else {
823:             logicalKey = predecessor.logical_key;
824:             rootMemoryId = predecessor.root_memory_id;
825:             versionNumber = predecessor.version_number + 1;
826:           }
827:         } else {
828:           const seeded = seedLineageFromCurrentState(database, predecessorMemoryId, {
829:             actor,
830:             transitionEvent: 'BACKFILL',
831:           });
832:           if (seeded.logicalKey !== rowLogicalKey) {
833:             throw new Error(
834:               `E_LINEAGE: predecessor ${predecessorMemoryId} logical identity ${seeded.logicalKey} ` +
835:               `does not match memory ${memoryId} logical identity ${rowLogicalKey}`,
836:             );
837:           }
838:           logicalKey = seeded.logicalKey;
839:           rootMemoryId = seeded.rootMemoryId;
840:           versionNumber = seeded.versionNumber + 1;
841:           predecessor = getLineageRow(database, predecessorMemoryId);
842:         }
843:       }
844: 
845:       if (attempt > 0) {
846:         const latest = getLatestLineageRowForLogicalKey(database, logicalKey);
847:         if (latest) {
848:           rootMemoryId = latest.root_memory_id;
849:           versionNumber = latest.version_number + 1;
850:         }
851:       }
852: 
853:       validateTransitionInput(transitionEvent, predecessorMemoryId, validFrom, predecessor);
854: 
855:       if (predecessorMemoryId != null) {
856:         if (predecessor && predecessor.valid_to) {
857:           logger.warn(
858:             `Predecessor ${predecessorMemoryId} already superseded (valid_to: ${predecessor.valid_to}). ` +
859:             'COALESCE will preserve the existing valid_to.',
860:           );
861:         }
862:         database.prepare(`
863:           UPDATE memory_lineage
864:           SET valid_to = COALESCE(valid_to, ?),
865:               superseded_by_memory_id = COALESCE(superseded_by_memory_id, ?)
866:           WHERE memory_id = ?
867:         `).run(validFrom, memoryId, predecessorMemoryId);
868:         markHistoricalPredecessor(database, predecessorMemoryId, validFrom);
869:       }
870: 
871:       database.prepare(`
872:         INSERT INTO memory_lineage (
873:           memory_id,
874:           logical_key,
875:           version_number,
876:           root_memory_id,
877:           predecessor_memory_id,
878:           superseded_by_memory_id,
879:           valid_from,
880:           valid_to,
881:           transition_event,
882:           actor,
883:           metadata
884:         ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, NULL, ?, NULL, ?, ?, ?)
885:       `).run(
886:         memoryId,
887:         logicalKey,
888:         versionNumber,
889:         rootMemoryId,
890:         predecessorMemoryId,
891:         validFrom,
892:         transitionEvent,
893:         actor,
894:         buildMetadata(row, actor, historyEvents),
895:       );
896: 
897:       upsertActiveProjection(database, logicalKey, rootMemoryId, memoryId, validFrom);
898: 
899:       return {
900:         logicalKey,
901:         versionNumber,
902:         rootMemoryId,
903:         activeMemoryId: memoryId,
904:         predecessorMemoryId,
905:         transitionEvent,
906:       } as RecordedLineageTransition;
907:     });
908: 
909:     try {
910:       return recordTransitionTx();
911:     } catch (error: unknown) {
912:       const shouldRetry = isLogicalVersionConflict(error) && attempt < MAX_LINEAGE_VERSION_RETRIES;
913:       if (!shouldRetry) {
914:         throw error;
915:       }
916: 
917:       logger.warn(
918:         `Retrying lineage insert for memory ${memoryId} after logical_key/version_number conflict ` +
919:         `(attempt ${attempt + 1}/${MAX_LINEAGE_VERSION_RETRIES})`,
920:       );
921:     }
922:   }
923: 
924:   throw new Error(`E_LINEAGE: exhausted retries while recording lineage for memory ${memoryId}`);
925: }
926: 
927: /**
928:  * Create a new append-only memory row and wire it into lineage state.
929:  *
930:  * @param params - Parsed memory payload and append-only lineage metadata.
931:  * @returns Identifier of the newly inserted memory row.
932:  */
933: export function createAppendOnlyMemoryRecord(params: CreateAppendOnlyMemoryRecordParams): number {
934:   bindHistory(params.database);
935:   const appendTx = params.database.transaction(() => {
936:     const memoryId = insertAppendOnlyMemoryIndexRow(params);
937:     recordLineageTransition(params.database, memoryId, {
938:       actor: params.actor ?? 'mcp:memory_save',
939:       predecessorMemoryId: params.predecessorMemoryId,
940:       transitionEvent: 'SUPERSEDE',
941:     });
942:     return memoryId;
943:   });
944: 
945:   return appendTx();
946: }
947: 
948: function resolveLogicalKey(database: Database.Database, memoryId: number): string | null {
949:   const row = getLineageRow(database, memoryId);
950:   if (row) {
951:     return row.logical_key;
952:   }
953: 
954:   const memoryRow = getMemoryRow(database, memoryId);
955:   const projection = getActiveProjection(database, buildLogicalKey(memoryRow));
956:   return projection?.logical_key ?? buildLogicalKey(memoryRow);
957: }
958: 
959: /**
960:  * Return the full ordered lineage chain for the logical key behind a memory.
961:  *
962:  * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
963:  * @param memoryId - Memory identifier used to resolve the logical key.
964:  * @returns Ordered lineage snapshots from oldest to newest version.
965:  */
966: export function inspectLineageChain(database: Database.Database, memoryId: number): ResolvedLineageSnapshot[] {
967:   bindHistory(database);
968:   ensureLineageTables(database);
969:   const lineage = loadLineageRowsForMemory(database, memoryId);
970:   if (!lineage) {
971:     return [];
972:   }
973: 
974:   return lineage.rows.map((row) => resolveSnapshotFromLineageRow(database, row));
975: }
976: 
977: /**
978:  * Build a compact operator-facing summary for the lineage behind a memory.
979:  *
980:  * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
981:  * @param memoryId - Memory identifier used to resolve the logical key.
982:  * @returns Aggregated lineage summary when one exists.
983:  */
984: export function summarizeLineageInspection(
985:   database: Database.Database,
986:   memoryId: number,
987: ): LineageInspectionSummary | null {
988:   bindHistory(database);
989:   ensureLineageTables(database);
990:   const lineage = loadLineageRowsForMemory(database, memoryId);
991:   if (!lineage) {
992:     return null;
993:   }
994:   const { logicalKey, projection, rows } = lineage;
995: 
996:   if (rows.length === 0) {
997:     return null;
998:   }
999: 
1000:   const transitionCounts: Record<LineageTransitionEvent, number> = {
1001:     CREATE: 0,
1002:     UPDATE: 0,
1003:     SUPERSEDE: 0,
1004:     BACKFILL: 0,
1005:   };
1006:   const actors = new Set<string>();
1007:   let hasVersionGaps = false;
1008:   let activeRows = 0;
1009: 
1010:   for (let index = 0; index < rows.length; index += 1) {
1011:     const row = rows[index];
1012:     const expectedVersion = index + 1;
1013:     if (row.version_number !== expectedVersion) {
1014:       hasVersionGaps = true;
1015:     }
1016:     // Use version_number ordering for predecessor chain validation
1017:     // rather than assuming sequential array positions match predecessor IDs.
1018:     if (index > 0) {
1019:       const prevRow = rows[index - 1];
1020:       if (prevRow && row.predecessor_memory_id !== prevRow.memory_id && row.version_number === prevRow.version_number + 1) {
1021:         hasVersionGaps = true;
1022:       }
1023:     }
1024:     if (row.valid_to == null) {
1025:       activeRows += 1;
1026:     }
1027:     transitionCounts[row.transition_event] += 1;
1028:     if (row.actor.trim().length > 0) {
1029:       actors.add(row.actor);
1030:     }
1031:   }
1032: 
1033:   const activeVersion = projection
1034:     ? rows.find((row) => row.memory_id === projection.active_memory_id) ?? null
1035:     : rows.find((row) => row.valid_to == null) ?? null;
1036: 
1037:   return {
1038:     logicalKey,
1039:     rootMemoryId: rows[0].root_memory_id,
1040:     activeMemoryId: projection?.active_memory_id ?? activeVersion?.memory_id ?? null,
1041:     activeVersionNumber: activeVersion?.version_number ?? null,
1042:     totalVersions: rows.length,
1043:     versionNumbers: rows.map((row) => row.version_number),
1044:     historicalMemoryIds: rows.slice(0, -1).map((row) => row.memory_id),
1045:     firstValidFrom: rows[0].valid_from,
1046:     latestValidFrom: rows[rows.length - 1].valid_from,
1047:     actors: [...actors],
1048:     transitionCounts,
1049:     hasVersionGaps,
1050:     hasMultipleActiveVersions: activeRows > 1,
1051:   };
1052: }
1053: 
1054: /**
1055:  * Resolve the currently active lineage snapshot for a memory logical key.
1056:  *
1057:  * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
1058:  * @param memoryId - Memory identifier used to resolve the logical key.
1059:  * @returns Active lineage snapshot when one exists.
1060:  */
1061: export function resolveActiveLineageSnapshot(
1062:   database: Database.Database,
1063:   memoryId: number,
1064: ): ResolvedLineageSnapshot | null {
1065:   ensureLineageTables(database);
1066:   const lineage = loadLineageRowsForMemory(database, memoryId);
1067:   if (!lineage) {
1068:     return null;
1069:   }
1070:   const { projection, rows } = lineage;
1071:   if (rows.length === 0) {
1072:     return null;
1073:   }
1074: 
1075:   const row = projection
1076:     ? rows.find((candidate) => candidate.memory_id === projection.active_memory_id) ?? null
1077:     : rows.find((candidate) => candidate.valid_to == null) ?? null;
1078:   return row ? resolveSnapshotFromLineageRow(database, row) : null;
1079: }
1080: 
1081: /**
1082:  * Resolve the lineage snapshot visible at a specific point in time.
1083:  *
1084:  * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
1085:  * @param memoryId - Memory identifier used to resolve the logical key.
1086:  * @param asOf - Timestamp used for temporal resolution.
1087:  * @returns Snapshot active at the requested time when one exists.
1088:  */
1089: export function resolveLineageAsOf(
1090:   database: Database.Database,
1091:   memoryId: number,
1092:   asOf: string | Date,
1093: ): ResolvedLineageSnapshot | null {
1094:   ensureLineageTables(database);
1095:   const lineage = loadLineageRowsForMemory(database, memoryId);
1096:   if (!lineage) {
1097:     return null;
1098:   }
1099: 
1100:   const asOfTimestamp = typeof asOf === 'string' ? asOf : asOf.toISOString();
1101:   const row = [...lineage.rows].reverse().find((candidate) => (
1102:     candidate.valid_from <= asOfTimestamp
1103:     && (candidate.valid_to == null || candidate.valid_to > asOfTimestamp)
1104:   )) ?? null;
1105: 
1106:   return row ? resolveSnapshotFromLineageRow(database, row) : null;
1107: }
1108: 
1109: /**
1110:  * Validate lineage chains and active projections for structural drift.
1111:  *
1112:  * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
1113:  * @returns Integrity report describing missing predecessors and projection drift.
1114:  */
1115: export function validateLineageIntegrity(database: Database.Database): ValidateLineageIntegrityResult {
1116:   ensureLineageTables(database);
1117:   const issues: string[] = [];
1118:   const missingPredecessors: number[] = [];
1119:   const duplicateActiveLogicalKeys: string[] = [];
1120:   const projectionMismatches: string[] = [];
1121: 
1122:   const orphanPredecessors = database.prepare(`
1123:     SELECT logical_key, memory_id, predecessor_memory_id
1124:     FROM memory_lineage
1125:     WHERE predecessor_memory_id IS NOT NULL
1126:       AND predecessor_memory_id NOT IN (SELECT memory_id FROM memory_lineage)
1127:   `).all() as Array<{ logical_key: string; memory_id: number; predecessor_memory_id: number }>;
1128: 
1129:   for (const issue of orphanPredecessors) {
1130:     missingPredecessors.push(issue.memory_id);
1131:     issues.push(
1132:       `Lineage ${issue.logical_key} version ${issue.memory_id} references missing predecessor ${issue.predecessor_memory_id}`,
1133:     );
1134:   }
1135: 
1136:   const duplicateActive = database.prepare(`
1137:     SELECT logical_key, COUNT(*) AS total
1138:     FROM memory_lineage
1139:     WHERE valid_to IS NULL
1140:     GROUP BY logical_key
1141:     HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
1142:   `).all() as Array<{ logical_key: string; total: number }>;
1143: 
1144:   for (const issue of duplicateActive) {
1145:     duplicateActiveLogicalKeys.push(issue.logical_key);
1146:     issues.push(`Lineage ${issue.logical_key} has ${issue.total} active versions`);
1147:   }
1148: 
1149:   const projectionMismatch = database.prepare(`
1150:     SELECT p.logical_key, p.active_memory_id
1151:     FROM active_memory_projection p
1152:     LEFT JOIN memory_lineage l
1153:       ON l.logical_key = p.logical_key
1154:      AND l.memory_id = p.active_memory_id
1155:     WHERE l.memory_id IS NULL OR l.valid_to IS NOT NULL
1156:   `).all() as Array<{ logical_key: string; active_memory_id: number }>;
1157: 
1158:   for (const issue of projectionMismatch) {
1159:     projectionMismatches.push(issue.logical_key);
1160:     issues.push(`Active projection ${issue.logical_key} points to non-active memory ${issue.active_memory_id}`);
1161:   }
1162: 
1163:   const activeProjectionCount = (
1164:     database.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM active_memory_projection').get() as { total: number }
1165:   ).total;
1166:   const lineageRowCount = (
1167:     database.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM memory_lineage').get() as { total: number }
1168:   ).total;
1169: 
1170:   return {
1171:     valid: issues.length === 0,
1172:     issues,
1173:     activeProjectionCount,
1174:     lineageRowCount,
1175:     missingPredecessors,
1176:     duplicateActiveLogicalKeys,
1177:     projectionMismatches,
1178:   };
1179: }
1180: 
1181: /**
1182:  * Backfill lineage state from existing memory rows in append-only order.
1183:  *
1184:  * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
1185:  * @param options - Dry-run and actor settings for the backfill.
1186:  * @returns Backfill summary including scanned and seeded rows.
1187:  */
1188: export function backfillLineageState(
1189:   database: Database.Database,
1190:   options: { dryRun?: boolean; actor?: string } = {},
1191: ): BackfillLineageResult {
1192:   bindHistory(database);
1193:   ensureLineageTables(database);
1194:   const dryRun = options.dryRun === true;
1195:   const actor = options.actor ?? 'memory-lineage:backfill';
1196:   const rows = database.prepare(`
1197:     SELECT *
1198:     FROM memory_index
1199:     WHERE parent_id IS NULL
1200:     ORDER BY created_at ASC, id ASC
1201:   `).all() as MemoryIndexRow[];
1202: 
1203:   const groups = new Map<string, MemoryIndexRow[]>();
1204:   for (const row of rows) {
1205:     const logicalKey = buildLogicalKey(row);
1206:     const group = groups.get(logicalKey);
1207:     if (group) {
1208:       group.push(row);
1209:     } else {
1210:       groups.set(logicalKey, [row]);
1211:     }
1212:   }
1213: 
1214:   const logicalKeys = [...groups.keys()];
1215:   let seeded = 0;
1216:   let skipped = 0;
1217: 
1218:   for (const group of groups.values()) {
1219:     for (let index = 0; index < group.length; index += 1) {
1220:       const row = group[index];
1221:       const predecessor = index > 0 ? group[index - 1] : null;
1222:       const successor = index < group.length - 1 ? group[index + 1] : null;
1223:       const existing = getLineageRow(database, row.id);
1224:       // Align dry-run timestamp source with execution path.
1225:       const historyEventsForDryRun = getSafeHistoryEvents(database, row.id);
1226:       const expectedValidFrom = historyEventsForDryRun[0]?.timestamp
1227:         ?? normalizeTimestamp(row.created_at ?? row.updated_at);
1228:       const expectedValidTo = successor
1229:         ? normalizeTimestamp(successor.created_at ?? successor.updated_at)
1230:         : null;
1231:       const expectedVersion = index + 1;
1232:       const expectedRoot = group[0].id;
1233:       if (
1234:         !existing
1235:         || existing.version_number !== expectedVersion
1236:         || existing.root_memory_id !== expectedRoot
1237:         || existing.predecessor_memory_id !== (predecessor?.id ?? null)
1238:         || existing.superseded_by_memory_id !== (successor?.id ?? null)
1239:         || existing.valid_from !== expectedValidFrom
1240:         || existing.valid_to !== expectedValidTo
1241:       ) {
1242:         seeded += 1;
1243:       } else {
1244:         skipped += 1;
1245:       }
1246:     }
1247:   }
1248: 
1249:   if (dryRun) {
1250:     return {
1251:       dryRun: true,
1252:       scanned: rows.length,
1253:       seeded,
1254:       skipped,
1255:       logicalKeys,
1256:       totalGroups: groups.size,
1257:     };
1258:   }
1259: 
1260:   const execute = database.transaction(() => {
1261:     for (const [logicalKey, group] of groups.entries()) {
1262:       for (let index = 0; index < group.length; index += 1) {
1263:         const row = group[index];
1264:         const predecessor = index > 0 ? group[index - 1] : null;
1265:         const successor = index < group.length - 1 ? group[index + 1] : null;
1266:         const historyEvents = getSafeHistoryEvents(database, row.id);
1267:         const validFrom = historyEvents[0]?.timestamp
1268:           ?? normalizeTimestamp(row.created_at ?? row.updated_at);
1269:         const validTo = successor
1270:           ? normalizeTimestamp(successor.created_at ?? successor.updated_at)
1271:           : null;
1272: 
1273:         database.prepare(`
1274:           INSERT INTO memory_lineage (
1275:             memory_id,
1276:             logical_key,
1277:             version_number,
1278:             root_memory_id,
1279:             predecessor_memory_id,
1280:             superseded_by_memory_id,
1281:             valid_from,
1282:             valid_to,
1283:             transition_event,
1284:             actor,
1285:             metadata
1286:           ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'BACKFILL', ?, ?)
1287:           ON CONFLICT(memory_id) DO UPDATE SET
1288:             logical_key = excluded.logical_key,
1289:             version_number = excluded.version_number,
1290:             root_memory_id = excluded.root_memory_id,
1291:             predecessor_memory_id = excluded.predecessor_memory_id,
1292:             superseded_by_memory_id = excluded.superseded_by_memory_id,
1293:             valid_from = excluded.valid_from,
1294:             valid_to = excluded.valid_to,
1295:             transition_event = excluded.transition_event,
1296:             actor = excluded.actor,
1297:             metadata = excluded.metadata
1298:         `).run(
1299:           row.id,
1300:           logicalKey,
1301:           index + 1,
1302:           group[0].id,
1303:           predecessor?.id ?? null,
1304:           successor?.id ?? null,
1305:           validFrom,
1306:           validTo,
1307:           actor,
1308:           buildMetadata(row, actor, historyEvents),
1309:         );
1310: 
1311:         if (predecessor) {
1312:           markHistoricalPredecessor(database, predecessor.id, validFrom);
1313:         }
1314:       }
1315: 
1316:       const latest = group[group.length - 1];
1317:       upsertActiveProjection(
1318:         database,
1319:         logicalKey,
1320:         group[0].id,
1321:         latest.id,
1322:         normalizeTimestamp(latest.updated_at ?? latest.created_at),
1323:       );
1324:     }
1325:   });
1326: 
1327:   execute();
1328: 
1329:   return {
1330:     dryRun: false,
1331:     scanned: rows.length,
1332:     seeded,
1333:     skipped,
1334:     logicalKeys,
1335:     totalGroups: groups.size,
1336:   };
1337: }
1338: 
1339: /**
1340:  * Resolve the active projection row for the lineage that owns a memory.
1341:  *
1342:  * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
1343:  * @param memoryId - Memory identifier used to resolve the logical key.
1344:  * @returns Active projection row when one exists.
1345:  */
1346: export function getActiveProjectionRow(
1347:   database: Database.Database,
1348:   memoryId: number,
1349: ): ActiveProjectionRow | null {
1350:   const lineage = loadLineageRowsForMemory(database, memoryId);
1351:   if (!lineage) {
1352:     return null;
1353:   }
1354:   return lineage.projection;
1355: }
1356: 
1357: /**
1358:  * Resolve the latest lineage row for the logical key behind a memory.
1359:  *
1360:  * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
1361:  * @param memoryId - Memory identifier used to resolve the logical key.
1362:  * @returns Latest lineage row when one exists.
1363:  */
1364: export function getLatestLineageForMemory(database: Database.Database, memoryId: number): MemoryLineageRow | null {
1365:   const lineage = loadLineageRowsForMemory(database, memoryId);
1366:   if (!lineage || lineage.rows.length === 0) {
1367:     return null;
1368:   }
1369:   return lineage.rows[lineage.rows.length - 1] ?? null;
1370: }
1371: 
1372: /**
1373:  * Compatibility wrapper used by roadmap tests and save flows for lineage writes.
1374:  *
1375:  * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
1376:  * @param params - Memory identifier and transition metadata to record.
1377:  * @returns Recorded lineage transition for the requested memory version.
1378:  */
1379: export function recordLineageVersion(
1380:   database: Database.Database,
1381:   params: {
1382:     memoryId: number;
1383:     actor?: string;
1384:     predecessorMemoryId?: number | null;
1385:     effectiveAt?: string;
1386:     transitionEvent?: LineageTransitionEvent;
1387:   },
1388: ): RecordedLineageTransition {
1389:   if (typeof (database as Database.Database & { exec?: unknown }).exec !== 'function') {
1390:     // Mock path returns memoryId as root — predecessor's root is unavailable
1391:     // without DB access, and using predecessor ID itself is incorrect.
1392:     return {
1393:       logicalKey: `mock:${params.memoryId}`,
1394:       versionNumber: 1,
1395:       rootMemoryId: params.memoryId,
1396:       activeMemoryId: params.memoryId,
1397:       predecessorMemoryId: params.predecessorMemoryId ?? null,
1398:       transitionEvent: params.transitionEvent ?? 'CREATE',
1399:     };
1400:   }
1401: 
1402:   return recordLineageTransition(database, params.memoryId, {
1403:     actor: params.actor,
1404:     predecessorMemoryId: params.predecessorMemoryId,
1405:     transitionEvent: params.transitionEvent,
1406:     validFrom: params.effectiveAt,
1407:   });
1408: }
1409: 
1410: /**
1411:  * Retire a predecessor row before its same-key successor is inserted, preserving
1412:  * lineage and history.
1413:  *
1414:  * Seeds the predecessor's lineage from its still-active state (idempotent — a no-op
1415:  * when lineage already exists), then marks the row `deprecated` so it leaves the
1416:  * active-row uniqueness guard. The row, its lineage chain, and its audit history are
1417:  * all retained; only the active-tier classification changes. Seeding before the tier
1418:  * change keeps the seed snapshot anchored to the predecessor's active identity.
1419:  *
1420:  * Call inside the caller's write transaction, immediately before inserting the new
1421:  * version, so the new active row never collides with a still-active predecessor.
1422:  *
1423:  * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage and index state.
1424:  * @param predecessorMemoryId - Memory id being superseded by a same-key successor.
1425:  */
1426: export interface RetiredPredecessorCarry {
1427:   /** Importance tier a human/manual writer set on the retired predecessor. */
1428:   importanceTier: string;
1429:   /** Source kind to re-stamp so the carried tier keeps its manual protection. */
1430:   sourceKind: string;
1431: }
1432: 
1433: export function retirePredecessorForActiveReindex(
1434:   database: Database.Database,
1435:   predecessorMemoryId: number,
1436: ): RetiredPredecessorCarry | null {
1437:   const predecessor = database
1438:     .prepare('SELECT importance_tier, source_kind FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?')
1439:     .get(predecessorMemoryId) as { importance_tier: string | null; source_kind: string | null } | undefined;
1440:   if (!predecessor) {
1441:     return null;
1442:   }
1443:   // Constitutional rows are exempt from the active-row guard and must keep their
1444:   // always-surface tier — deprecating one would declassify it. Leave it active and
1445:   // let the lineage transition supersede it without a tier change.
1446:   if (predecessor.importance_tier === 'constitutional') {
1447:     return null;
1448:   }
1449: 
1450:   // A manual/human tier decision must survive a reindex. The predecessor still
1451:   // has to be deprecated to free the active-row uniqueness slot for the incoming
1452:   // successor — skipping the retire would collide on the active unique index —
1453:   // so instead we surface the manual tier to the caller, which re-applies it to
1454:   // the successor. The human's classification carries forward rather than being
1455:   // silently reset to the reindexed default.
1456:   const carry: RetiredPredecessorCarry | null =
1457:     isManualSourceKind(predecessor.source_kind) && predecessor.importance_tier
1458:       ? { importanceTier: predecessor.importance_tier, sourceKind: 'human' }
1459:       : null;
1460: 
1461:   seedLineageFromCurrentState(database, predecessorMemoryId, { transitionEvent: 'BACKFILL' });
1462:   database
1463:     .prepare("UPDATE memory_index SET importance_tier = 'deprecated', updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?")
1464:     .run(normalizeTimestamp(null), predecessorMemoryId);
1465: 
1466:   return carry;
1467: }

1470:  * Compatibility wrapper that resolves the active snapshot for a memory target.
1471:  *
1472:  * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
1473:  * @param target - Memory identifier used to resolve the active snapshot.

1475:  */
1476: export function getActiveMemoryProjection(
1477:   database: Database.Database,
1478:   target: { memoryId: number },
1479: ): ResolvedLineageSnapshot | null {
1480:   return resolveActiveLineageSnapshot(database, target.memoryId);
1481: }
