42: function batchedInQuery<T>(db: Database.Database, sql: string, ids: (number | string)[], batchSize = 500): T[] {
43:   const results: T[] = [];
44:   for (let i = 0; i < ids.length; i += batchSize) {
45:     const batch = ids.slice(i, i + batchSize);
46:     const placeholders = batch.map(() => '?').join(', ');
47:     const batchSql = sql.replace('__PLACEHOLDERS__', placeholders);
48:     results.push(...(db.prepare(batchSql).all(...batch) as T[]));
49:   }
50:   return results;
51: }

53: function dedupeRowsById<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(rows: T[]): T[] {
54:   const results: T[] = [];
55:   const seenIds = new Set<number | string>();
56: 
57:   for (const row of rows) {
58:     const id = row.id;
59:     if (typeof id === 'number' || typeof id === 'string') {
60:       if (seenIds.has(id)) {
61:         continue;
62:       }
63:       seenIds.add(id);
64:     }
65:     results.push(row);
66:   }
67: 
68:   return results;
69: }

71: function getSnapshotColumnsFromRows(rows: Array<Record<string, unknown>>): string[] {

74:   for (const row of rows) {
75:     for (const key of Object.keys(row ?? {})) {
76:       if (typeof key === 'string' && key.length > 0) {
77:         columns.add(key);
78:       }
79:     }
80:   }
81: 
82:   return Array.from(columns);

186: const MUTATION_LEDGER_TRIGGER_SQL = `
187:   CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS prevent_ledger_update BEFORE UPDATE ON mutation_ledger
188:   BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'mutation_ledger is append-only'); END;
189:   CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS prevent_ledger_delete BEFORE DELETE ON mutation_ledger
190:   BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'mutation_ledger is append-only'); END
191: `;

229: type CheckpointCreateErrorCode =

231:   | 'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_SQLITE_LOCKED'
232:   | 'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_DUPLICATE_NAME'
233:   | 'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_INVALID_NAME'

239:   | 'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_DOWNGRADE_UNSAFE'
240:   | 'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_EMBEDDER_MISMATCH'
241:   | 'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_FILE_MISSING'
242:   | 'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_PATH_UNRESOLVED'
243:   | 'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_JOURNAL_FAILED'

246: class CheckpointCreateError extends Error {
247:   public readonly code: CheckpointCreateErrorCode;
248:   public readonly originalCode: string | null;
249:   public readonly details: Record<string, unknown>;
250: 
251:   constructor(
252:     code: CheckpointCreateErrorCode,
253:     message: string,
254:     details: Record<string, unknown> = {},
255:     cause?: unknown,
256:   ) {
257:     super(message, { cause });
258:     Object.setPrototypeOf(this, CheckpointCreateError.prototype);
259:     this.name = 'CheckpointCreateError';
260:     this.code = code;
261:     this.originalCode = typeof details.originalCode === 'string' ? details.originalCode : null;
262:     this.details = details;
263:   }
264: }

266: class CheckpointRestoreError extends Error {
267:   public readonly code: CheckpointRestoreErrorCode;
268: 
269:   constructor(code: CheckpointRestoreErrorCode, message: string, cause?: unknown) {
270:     super(message, { cause });
271:     Object.setPrototypeOf(this, CheckpointRestoreError.prototype);
272:     this.name = 'CheckpointRestoreError';
273:     this.code = code;
274:   }
275: }

405: // Validate the decompressed checkpoint snapshot before
406: // restore. Previously the gzipped blob was JSON.parsed and cast directly
407: // to CheckpointSnapshot — malformed memory_index rows propagated through
408: // `getMemoryIds()` and the INSERT loops. This schema validates the
409: // outer envelope and the array shapes; individual row shapes are still
410: // permissive (sqlite columns evolve over time) but each row must be a
411: // non-null object so downstream column extraction doesn't NPE.
412: const CheckpointTableSnapshotSchema = z.object({
413:   columns: z.array(z.string()),
414:   rows: z.array(z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())),
415: });
416: 
417: const CheckpointVectorRowSchema = z.object({
418:   rowid: z.number(),
419:   embedding: z.unknown(), // Buffer or null — preserved as-is
420: });
421: 
422: const CheckpointSnapshotSchema = z.object({
423:   manifest: z.object({
424:     snapshot: z.array(z.string()),
425:     rebuild: z.array(z.string()),
426:     skip: z.array(z.string()),
427:   }).optional(),
428:   tables: z.record(z.string(), CheckpointTableSnapshotSchema).optional(),
429:   memories: z.array(z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())),
430:   workingMemory: z.array(z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())),
431:   vectors: z.array(CheckpointVectorRowSchema).optional(),
432:   causalEdges: z.array(z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())).optional(),
433:   timestamp: z.string(),
434: }).passthrough();
435: 
436: interface RestoreBarrierStatus {
437:   code: string;
438:   message: string;
439: }
440: 
441: interface RestoreBarrierHooks {
442:   afterAcquire?: (() => void) | null;
443:   beforeRelease?: (() => void) | null;
444: }

446: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
447:    3. MODULE STATE
448: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
449: 
450: let db: Database.Database | null = null;
451: let restoreInProgress = false;
452: let restoreBarrierHooks: RestoreBarrierHooks = {};
453: 
454: const RESTORE_IN_PROGRESS_ERROR_CODE = 'E_RESTORE_IN_PROGRESS';
455: const RESTORE_IN_PROGRESS_ERROR_MESSAGE = 'Checkpoint restore maintenance is in progress. Retry after the restore lifecycle completes.';
456: 
457: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
458:    4. INITIALIZATION
459: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
460: 
461: function init(database: Database.Database): void {
462:   db = database;
463: }
464: 
465: function getDatabase(): Database.Database {
466:   if (!db) throw new Error('Database not initialized. The checkpoints module requires the MCP server to be running. Restart the MCP server and retry.');
467:   return db;
468: }
469: 
470: function getErrorCode(error: unknown): string | null {
471:   if (typeof error === 'object' && error !== null && 'code' in error) {
472:     const code = (error as { code?: unknown }).code;
473:     return typeof code === 'string' ? code : null;
474:   }
475:   return null;
476: }
477: 
478: function isCheckpointBusyError(error: unknown): boolean {
479:   const code = getErrorCode(error);
480:   const message = toErrorMessage(error);
481:   return code === 'SQLITE_BUSY'
482:     || code === 'SQLITE_LOCKED'
483:     || /\bSQLITE_BUSY\b|\bSQLITE_LOCKED\b|database is locked/i.test(message);
484: }
485: 
486: function classifyCheckpointCreateError(
487:   error: unknown,
488:   context: Record<string, unknown> = {},
489: ): CheckpointCreateError {
490:   if (error instanceof CheckpointCreateError) {
491:     return error;
492:   }
493: 
494:   const originalCode = getErrorCode(error);
495:   const message = toErrorMessage(error);
496:   const details = {
497:     ...context,
498:     ...(originalCode ? { originalCode } : {}),
499:     originalMessage: message,
500:   };
501: 
502:   if (originalCode === 'SQLITE_BUSY' || /\bSQLITE_BUSY\b|database is locked/i.test(message)) {
503:     return new CheckpointCreateError(
504:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_SQLITE_BUSY',
505:       'Checkpoint creation failed because the SQLite database was busy. Retry after current writes finish.',
506:       details,
507:       error,
508:     );
509:   }
510: 
511:   if (originalCode === 'SQLITE_LOCKED' || /\bSQLITE_LOCKED\b/i.test(message)) {
512:     return new CheckpointCreateError(
513:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_SQLITE_LOCKED',
514:       'Checkpoint creation failed because the SQLite database was locked by another operation.',
515:       details,
516:       error,
517:     );
518:   }
519: 
520:   if (/UNIQUE constraint failed: checkpoints\.name|constraint failed/i.test(message)) {
521:     return new CheckpointCreateError(
522:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_DUPLICATE_NAME',
523:       'Checkpoint creation failed because a checkpoint with this name already exists.',
524:       details,
525:       error,
526:     );
527:   }
528: 
529:   if (
530:     originalCode === 'EACCES'
531:     || originalCode === 'EPERM'
532:     || originalCode === 'SQLITE_READONLY'
533:     || /permission denied|readonly|read-only/i.test(message)
534:   ) {
535:     return new CheckpointCreateError(
536:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_PERMISSION_DENIED',
537:       'Checkpoint creation failed because the database path is not writable.',
538:       details,
539:       error,
540:     );
541:   }
542: 
543:   return new CheckpointCreateError(
544:     'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_FAILED',
545:     `Checkpoint creation failed: ${message}`,
546:     details,
547:     error,
548:   );
549: }
550: 
551: function checkpointRetryDelayMs(): number {
552:   return CHECKPOINT_CREATE_RETRY_MIN_DELAY_MS
553:     + Math.floor(Math.random() * (CHECKPOINT_CREATE_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_MS - CHECKPOINT_CREATE_RETRY_MIN_DELAY_MS + 1));
554: }
555: 
556: function isRestoreInProgress(): boolean {
557:   return restoreInProgress;
558: }
559: 
560: function getRestoreBarrierStatus(): RestoreBarrierStatus | null {
561:   if (!restoreInProgress) {
562:     return null;
563:   }
564: 
565:   return {
566:     code: RESTORE_IN_PROGRESS_ERROR_CODE,
567:     message: RESTORE_IN_PROGRESS_ERROR_MESSAGE,
568:   };
569: }
570: 
571: function setRestoreBarrierHooks(hooks: RestoreBarrierHooks | null): void {
572:   restoreBarrierHooks = hooks ?? {};
573: }
574: 
575: function acquireRestoreBarrier(): void {
576:   restoreInProgress = true;
577:   restoreBarrierHooks.afterAcquire?.();
578: }
579: 
580: function releaseRestoreBarrier(): void {
581:   try {
582:     restoreBarrierHooks.beforeRelease?.();
583:   } finally {
584:     restoreInProgress = false;
585:   }
586: }
587: 
588: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
589:    5. HELPERS
590: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
591: 
592: function getGitBranch(): string | null {
593:   const candidates = [
594:     process.env.GIT_BRANCH,
595:     process.env.BRANCH_NAME,
596:     process.env.CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME,
597:     process.env.VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_REF,
598:   ];
599: 
600:   for (const candidate of candidates) {
601:     if (typeof candidate === 'string' && candidate.trim().length > 0) {
602:       return candidate.trim();
603:     }
604:   }
605: 
606:   return null;
607: }
608: 
609: function quoteSqlString(value: string): string {
610:   return `'${value.replace(/'/g, "''")}'`;
611: }
612: 
613: function getDatabaseList(database: Database.Database): DatabaseListRow[] {
614:   return database.prepare('PRAGMA database_list').all() as DatabaseListRow[];
615: }
616: 
617: function sanitizeCheckpointName(name: string): string {
618:   const sanitized = name.trim();
619:   if (
620:     sanitized.length === 0
621:     || sanitized.length > 128
622:     || sanitized.includes('/')
623:     || sanitized.includes('\\')
624:     || sanitized.includes('..')
625:     || sanitized.includes('\0')
626:   ) {
627:     throw new CheckpointCreateError(
628:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_INVALID_NAME',
629:       'Checkpoint creation failed because the checkpoint name is invalid.',
630:       { name },
631:     );
632:   }
633:   return sanitized;
634: }
635: 
636: function resolveCheckpointDir(database: Database.Database, name: string): string {
637:   const sanitizedName = sanitizeCheckpointName(name);
638:   const mainRow = getDatabaseList(database).find((row) => row.name === 'main');
639:   const mainFile = mainRow?.file;
640:   if (!mainFile || mainFile === ':memory:') {
641:     throw new CheckpointCreateError(
642:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_FAILED',
643:       'Checkpoint creation failed because the main database file path could not be resolved.',
644:       { name: sanitizedName },
645:     );
646:   }
647:   return path.join(path.dirname(mainFile), 'checkpoints', sanitizedName);
648: }
649: 
650: function getActiveVectorShardPath(database: Database.Database): string | null {
651:   const activeVec = getDatabaseList(database).find((row) => row.name === 'active_vec');
652:   return activeVec?.file && activeVec.file.length > 0 ? activeVec.file : null;
653: }
654: 
655: function deriveEmbedderSlug(shardPath: string | null): string | null {
656:   if (!shardPath) {
657:     return null;
658:   }
659:   const match = /^context-vectors__(.+)\.sqlite$/.exec(path.basename(shardPath));
660:   return match?.[1] ?? null;
661: }
662: 
663: function sweepStaleCheckpointTmpDirs(checkpointsDir: string): void {
664:   if (!fs.existsSync(checkpointsDir)) {
665:     return;
666:   }
667:   for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(checkpointsDir, { withFileTypes: true })) {
668:     if (entry.isDirectory() && entry.name.includes('.tmp-')) {
669:       fs.rmSync(path.join(checkpointsDir, entry.name), { recursive: true, force: true });
670:     }
671:   }
672: }
673: 
674: function resolvePathSafe(targetPath: string): string {
675:   try {
676:     return fs.realpathSync(targetPath);
677:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
678:     return path.resolve(targetPath);
679:   }
680: }
681: 
682: function sweepOrphanCheckpointSnapshotDirs(database: Database.Database, checkpointsDir: string): void {
683:   // Checkpoint pruning removes the catalog row inside the create transaction but deletes
684:   // the snapshot dir AFTER commit. A crash in that gap leaves a snapshot dir with no
685:   // catalog row — never swept by the .tmp-* sweep (it has a published name) and never
686:   // restorable. Reconcile on-disk v2 snapshot dirs against the live catalog and remove
687:   // any that no catalog row references. Best-effort: never throw out of the create path.
688:   try {
689:     if (!fs.existsSync(checkpointsDir) || !tableExists(database, 'checkpoints')) {
690:       return;
691:     }
692: 
693:     const checkpointColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'checkpoints'));
694:     if (!checkpointColumns.has('snapshot_path')) {
695:       return;
696:     }
697:     const referenced = new Set<string>();
698:     const rows = database.prepare(
699:       "SELECT snapshot_path FROM checkpoints WHERE snapshot_path IS NOT NULL AND snapshot_path <> ''",
700:     ).all() as Array<{ snapshot_path?: string | null }>;
701:     for (const row of rows) {
702:       if (typeof row.snapshot_path === 'string' && row.snapshot_path.length > 0) {
703:         referenced.add(resolvePathSafe(row.snapshot_path));
704:       }
705:     }
706: 
707:     for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(checkpointsDir, { withFileTypes: true })) {
708:       if (!entry.isDirectory() || entry.name.includes('.tmp-')) {
709:         continue;
710:       }
711:       const entryPath = path.join(checkpointsDir, entry.name);
712:       if (referenced.has(resolvePathSafe(entryPath))) {
713:         continue;
714:       }
715:       try {
716:         fs.rmSync(entryPath, { recursive: true, force: true });
717:         console.warn(`[checkpoints] Swept orphan checkpoint snapshot dir with no catalog row: ${entry.name}`);
718:       } catch (_removeError: unknown) {
719:         // Best-effort: a single un-removable dir must not block checkpoint creation.
720:       }
721:     }
722:   } catch (error: unknown) {
723:     console.warn(`[checkpoints] Orphan checkpoint snapshot sweep skipped (non-fatal): ${toErrorMessage(error)}`);
724:   }
725: }
726: 
727: function readSchemaVersion(database: Database.Database): number | null {
728:   try {
729:     const row = database.prepare('SELECT version FROM schema_version WHERE id = 1').get() as { version?: number } | undefined;
730:     return typeof row?.version === 'number' ? row.version : null;
731:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
732:     return null;
733:   }
734: }
735: 
736: function countTableRows(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): number {
737:   if (!tableExists(database, tableName)) {
738:     return 0;
739:   }
740:   const row = database.prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM ${tableName}`).get() as { count?: number } | undefined;
741:   return typeof row?.count === 'number' ? row.count : 0;
742: }
743: 
744: function countActiveVectorRows(database: Database.Database): number {
745:   try {
746:     const row = database.prepare(`
747:       SELECT 1 AS found
748:       FROM active_vec.sqlite_master
749:       WHERE type IN ('table', 'view') AND name = 'vec_memories'
750:       LIMIT 1
751:     `).get() as { found?: number } | undefined;
752:     if (row?.found !== 1) {
753:       return 0;
754:     }
755:     const countRow = database.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM active_vec.vec_memories').get() as { count?: number } | undefined;
756:     return typeof countRow?.count === 'number' ? countRow.count : 0;
757:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
758:     return 0;
759:   }
760: }
761: 
762: function readCheckpointV2Manifest(snapshotPath: string | null): CheckpointV2Manifest | null {
763:   if (!snapshotPath) {
764:     return null;
765:   }
766:   try {
767:     const raw = fs.readFileSync(path.join(snapshotPath, 'manifest.json'), 'utf-8');
768:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as Partial<CheckpointV2Manifest>;
769:     return parsed.formatVersion === 2 ? parsed as CheckpointV2Manifest : null;
770:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
771:     return null;
772:   }
773: }
774: 
775: function loadCheckpointV2Manifest(snapshotPath: string | null): CheckpointV2Manifest {
776:   if (!snapshotPath) {
777:     throw new CheckpointRestoreError(
778:       'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_MANIFEST_INVALID',
779:       'Checkpoint v2 restore failed because snapshot_path is missing.',
780:     );
781:   }
782: 
783:   const manifestPath = path.join(snapshotPath, 'manifest.json');
784:   try {
785:     const raw = fs.readFileSync(manifestPath, 'utf-8');
786:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as Partial<CheckpointV2Manifest>;
787:     if (
788:       parsed.formatVersion !== 2
789:       || typeof parsed.includeEmbeddings !== 'boolean'
790:       || !Array.isArray(parsed.mainTables)
791:       || !Array.isArray(parsed.vecTables)
792:       || typeof parsed.schemaVersion !== 'number'
793:       || typeof parsed.memoryCount !== 'number'
794:       || typeof parsed.vectorCount !== 'number'
795:     ) {
796:       throw new Error('manifest shape is invalid');
797:     }
798:     return parsed as CheckpointV2Manifest;
799:   } catch (error: unknown) {
800:     throw new CheckpointRestoreError(
801:       'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_MANIFEST_INVALID',
802:       `Checkpoint v2 restore failed because manifest.json could not be read: ${toErrorMessage(error)}`,
803:       error,
804:     );
805:   }
806: }
807: 
808: function resolveMainDatabasePath(database: Database.Database): string {
809:   const mainRow = getDatabaseList(database).find((row) => row.name === 'main');
810:   const mainFile = mainRow?.file;
811:   if (!mainFile || mainFile === ':memory:') {
812:     throw new CheckpointRestoreError(
813:       'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_PATH_UNRESOLVED',
814:       'Checkpoint v2 restore failed because the live main database path could not be resolved.',
815:     );
816:   }
817:   return mainFile;
818: }
819: 
820: function removeSqliteSidecars(databasePath: string): void {
821:   for (const sidecarPath of [`${databasePath}-wal`, `${databasePath}-shm`]) {
822:     fs.rmSync(sidecarPath, { force: true });
823:   }
824: }

826: function getRestoreJournalPath(liveMainPath: string): string {
827:   return path.join(path.dirname(liveMainPath), 'checkpoints', RESTORE_JOURNAL_NAME);
828: }

831:   return path.join(path.dirname(liveMainPath), 'checkpoints', NEEDS_REBUILD_SENTINEL_NAME);
832: }
833: 
834: function fsyncFileIfPossible(filePath: string): void {
835:   let fd: number | null = null;
836:   try {
837:     fd = fs.openSync(filePath, 'r');
838:     fs.fsyncSync(fd);
839:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
840:     // Best-effort durability; the next boot still treats any visible journal as authoritative.
841:   } finally {
842:     if (fd !== null) {
843:       try { fs.closeSync(fd); } catch (_error: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
844:     }
845:   }
846: }
847: 
848: function fsyncDirectoryIfPossible(dirPath: string): void {
849:   let fd: number | null = null;
850:   try {
851:     fd = fs.openSync(dirPath, 'r');
852:     fs.fsyncSync(fd);
853:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
854:     // Directory fsync is unavailable on some platforms/filesystems.
855:   } finally {
856:     if (fd !== null) {
857:       try { fs.closeSync(fd); } catch (_error: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
858:     }
859:   }
860: }

862: function writeRestoreJournal(journalPath: string, journal: RestoreJournalFile): void {
863:   const journalDir = path.dirname(journalPath);
864:   const tempJournalPath = `${journalPath}.tmp`;
865:   fs.mkdirSync(journalDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
866:   fs.writeFileSync(tempJournalPath, `${JSON.stringify(journal, null, 2)}\n`, { mode: 0o600 });
867:   fsyncFileIfPossible(tempJournalPath);
868:   fs.renameSync(tempJournalPath, journalPath);
869:   fsyncDirectoryIfPossible(journalDir);
870: }
871: 
872: function clearRestoreJournal(journalPath: string): void {
873:   fs.rmSync(journalPath, { force: true });
874:   fsyncDirectoryIfPossible(path.dirname(journalPath));
875: }

877: function writeNeedsRebuildSentinelAtPath(

880: ): void {
881:   const sentinelDir = path.dirname(sentinelPath);
882:   const tempSentinelPath = `${sentinelPath}.tmp`;
883:   const sentinel: NeedsRebuildSentinelFile = {
884:     formatVersion: 1,
885:     createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
886:     source: options.source,
887:     reason: options.reason,
888:     ...(options.summary ? { rebuildSummary: options.summary } : {}),
889:   };
890: 
891:   fs.mkdirSync(sentinelDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
892:   fs.writeFileSync(tempSentinelPath, `${JSON.stringify(sentinel, null, 2)}\n`, { mode: 0o600 });
893:   fsyncFileIfPossible(tempSentinelPath);
894:   fs.renameSync(tempSentinelPath, sentinelPath);
895:   fsyncDirectoryIfPossible(sentinelDir);
896: }

898: function clearNeedsRebuildSentinelAtPath(sentinelPath: string): boolean {

957: function readCheckpointCatalogRows(database: Database.Database): Array<Record<string, unknown>> {
958:   if (!tableExists(database, 'checkpoints')) {
959:     return [];
960:   }
961:   return database.prepare('SELECT * FROM checkpoints ORDER BY id ASC').all() as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
962: }
963: 
964: function mergeCheckpointCatalogRows(database: Database.Database, rows: Array<Record<string, unknown>>): void {
965:   if (rows.length === 0 || !tableExists(database, 'checkpoints')) {
966:     return;
967:   }
968: 
969:   const targetColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'checkpoints'));
970:   for (const row of rows) {
971:     const name = row.name;
972:     if (typeof name !== 'string' || name.length === 0) {
973:       continue;
974:     }
975:     const existingByName = database.prepare('SELECT id FROM checkpoints WHERE name = ?').get(name) as { id?: number } | undefined;
976:     if (existingByName?.id) {
977:       continue;
978:     }
979: 
980:     let columns = Object.keys(row).filter((column) => targetColumns.has(column));
981:     const id = row.id;
982:     if (typeof id === 'number') {
983:       const existingById = database.prepare('SELECT name FROM checkpoints WHERE id = ?').get(id) as { name?: string } | undefined;
984:       if (existingById?.name && existingById.name !== name) {
985:         columns = columns.filter((column) => column !== 'id');
986:       }
987:     }
988:     if (columns.length === 0) {
989:       continue;
990:     }
991: 
992:     const placeholders = columns.map(() => '?').join(', ');
993:     database.prepare(`
994:       INSERT OR IGNORE INTO checkpoints (${columns.join(', ')})
995:       VALUES (${placeholders})
996:     `).run(...columns.map((column) => row[column]));
997:   }
998: }
999: 
1000: function pruneCheckpointCatalogRowsMissingSnapshots(database: Database.Database): void {
1001:   // The restored snapshot-main DB carries the full checkpoints table as of the
1002:   // snapshot, including v2 rows whose snapshot dir was deleted after the snapshot
1003:   // was taken. Those rows would resurface in listCheckpoints with no restorable
1004:   // dir, so drop any v2 row whose snapshot_path no longer exists on disk.
1005:   // Best-effort: never throw out of the restore path.
1006:   try {
1007:     if (!tableExists(database, 'checkpoints')) {
1008:       return;
1009:     }
1010:     const checkpointColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'checkpoints'));
1011:     if (!checkpointColumns.has('snapshot_path') || !checkpointColumns.has('snapshot_format')) {
1012:       return;
1013:     }
1014:     const rows = database.prepare(
1015:       "SELECT id, snapshot_path FROM checkpoints WHERE snapshot_format = 'v2' AND snapshot_path IS NOT NULL AND snapshot_path <> ''",
1016:     ).all() as Array<{ id: number; snapshot_path: string }>;
1017:     for (const row of rows) {
1018:       if (!fs.existsSync(row.snapshot_path)) {
1019:         database.prepare('DELETE FROM checkpoints WHERE id = ?').run(row.id);
1020:         console.warn(`[checkpoints] Dropped restored catalog row with missing snapshot dir: ${row.snapshot_path}`);
1021:       }
1022:     }
1023:   } catch (error: unknown) {
1024:     console.warn(`[checkpoints] Post-restore catalog reconciliation skipped (non-fatal): ${toErrorMessage(error)}`);
1025:   }
1026: }
1027: 
1028: function statPathBytes(targetPath: string): number {
1029:   try {
1030:     const stats = fs.statSync(targetPath);
1031:     if (stats.isFile()) {
1032:       return stats.size;
1033:     }
1034:     if (!stats.isDirectory()) {
1035:       return 0;
1036:     }
1037:     return fs.readdirSync(targetPath).reduce((total, entry) => total + statPathBytes(path.join(targetPath, entry)), 0);
1038:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1039:     return 0;
1040:   }
1041: }
1042: 
1043: function getCheckpointV2SnapshotSize(snapshotPath: string | null): number {
1044:   const manifest = readCheckpointV2Manifest(snapshotPath);
1045:   if (manifest) {
1046:     return manifest.mainBytes + manifest.vecBytes;
1047:   }
1048:   return snapshotPath ? statPathBytes(snapshotPath) : 0;
1049: }
1050: 
1051: function removeCheckpointSnapshotDirs(snapshotPaths: string[]): void {
1052:   for (const snapshotPath of snapshotPaths) {
1053:     try {
1054:       fs.rmSync(snapshotPath, { recursive: true, force: true });
1055:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
1056:       // Best-effort cleanup; the checkpoint row is already gone.
1057:     }
1058:   }
1059: }
1060: 
1061: function collectPrunedCheckpointSnapshotPaths(database: Database.Database, pruneCount: number): string[] {
1062:   if (pruneCount <= 0) {
1063:     return [];
1064:   }
1065:   const checkpointColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'checkpoints'));
1066:   const snapshotPathSelect = checkpointColumns.has('snapshot_path') ? 'snapshot_path' : 'NULL AS snapshot_path';
1067:   const snapshotFormatSelect = checkpointColumns.has('snapshot_format') ? 'snapshot_format' : "'v1' AS snapshot_format";
1068:   const rows = database.prepare(`
1069:     SELECT id, ${snapshotPathSelect}, ${snapshotFormatSelect}
1070:     FROM checkpoints
1071:     ORDER BY created_at ASC, id ASC
1072:     LIMIT ?
1073:   `).all(pruneCount) as Array<{ id: number; snapshot_path?: string | null; snapshot_format?: string | null }>;
1074: 
1075:   if (rows.length === 0) {
1076:     return [];
1077:   }
1078:   const ids = rows.map((row) => row.id);
1079:   const placeholders = ids.map(() => '?').join(', ');
1080:   database.prepare(`DELETE FROM checkpoints WHERE id IN (${placeholders})`).run(...ids);
1081:   return rows
1082:     .filter((row) => row.snapshot_format === 'v2' && typeof row.snapshot_path === 'string' && row.snapshot_path.length > 0)
1083:     .map((row) => row.snapshot_path as string);
1084: }
1085: 
1086: function enforceMaxCheckpointsInTransaction(database: Database.Database): string[] {
1087:   const checkpointCount = (database.prepare(
1088:     'SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM checkpoints'
1089:   ) as Database.Statement).get() as { count: number };
1090: 
1091:   if (checkpointCount.count <= MAX_CHECKPOINTS) {
1092:     return [];
1093:   }
1094: 
1095:   return collectPrunedCheckpointSnapshotPaths(database, checkpointCount.count - MAX_CHECKPOINTS);
1096: }
1097: 
1098: function supportsCheckpointV2(database: Database.Database): boolean {
1099:   const checkpointColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'checkpoints'));
1100:   return checkpointColumns.has('snapshot_format') && checkpointColumns.has('snapshot_path');
1101: }

1103: function hasMainVectorPayloadTables(database: Database.Database): boolean {
1104:   try {
1105:     // Gate v2 selection on vec_memories ONLY — the actual vector payload that the
1106:     // shard-attach slimming removes from main. vec_metadata is a tiny key/value
1107:     // config table that the same slimming intentionally RETAINS in main as a
1108:     // dimension fallback, so its presence does NOT mean vector payload lives in
1109:     // main. Including it here made full-DB create always observe "payload in main"
1110:     // on a sharded runtime and silently fall back to the v1 whole-snapshot path
1111:     // that overflows V8's single-string ceiling — the exact failure v2 prevents.
1112:     const row = database.prepare(`
1113:       SELECT 1 AS found
1114:       FROM sqlite_master
1115:       WHERE type IN ('table', 'view')
1116:         AND name = 'vec_memories'
1117:       LIMIT 1
1118:     `).get() as { found?: number } | undefined;
1119:     return row?.found === 1;
1120:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1121:     return false;
1122:   }
1123: }
1124: 
1125: function tableExists(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): boolean {
1126:   try {
1127:     if (tableName === 'vec_memories') {
1128:       const mainOrTempRow = database.prepare(`
1129:         SELECT 1 AS found
1130:         FROM temp.sqlite_master
1131:         WHERE type IN ('table','view') AND name = 'vec_memories'
1132:         UNION ALL
1133:         SELECT 1 AS found
1134:         FROM sqlite_master
1135:         WHERE type IN ('table','view') AND name = 'vec_memories'
1136:         LIMIT 1
1137:       `).get() as { found?: number } | undefined;
1138:       if (mainOrTempRow?.found === 1) {
1139:         return true;
1140:       }
1141: 
1142:       const hasActiveVectorShard = (database.prepare('PRAGMA database_list').all() as Array<{ name?: string }>)
1143:         .some((entry) => entry.name === 'active_vec');
1144:       if (!hasActiveVectorShard) {
1145:         return false;
1146:       }
1147: 
1148:       const vectorRow = database.prepare(`
1149:         SELECT 1 AS found
1150:         FROM active_vec.sqlite_master
1151:         WHERE type IN ('table','view') AND name = 'vec_memories'
1152:         LIMIT 1
1153:       `).get() as { found?: number } | undefined;
1154:       return vectorRow?.found === 1;
1155:     }
1156: 
1157:     const row = database.prepare(
1158:       "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type IN ('table','view') AND name = ?"
1159:     ).get(tableName) as { name?: string } | undefined;
1160:     return !!row?.name;
1161:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1162:     return false;
1163:   }
1164: }
1165: 
1166: // Static allowlist for dynamic table name interpolation
1167: const ALLOWED_TABLE_NAMES = new Set([
1168:   // Core tables
1169:   'memory_index', 'memory_fts', 'vec_memories', 'vec_metadata', 'causal_edges',
1170:   'memory_conflicts', 'memory_corrections', 'memory_lineage', 'memory_history',
1171:   'memory_summaries', 'mutation_ledger',
1172:   // Working memory & session
1173:   'working_memory', 'session_state', 'session_sent_memories', 'session_learning',
1174:   // Graph & signals
1175:   'weight_history', 'adaptive_signal_events', 'negative_feedback_events',
1176:   'active_memory_projection', 'auto_entities', 'community_assignments', 'degree_snapshots',
1177:   // Feedback & learning
1178:   'learned_feedback_audit', 'learned_trigger_scores', 'learned_trigger_feedback',
1179:   // Governance
1180:   'governance_audit',
1181:   // Checkpoints & system
1182:   'checkpoints', 'checkpoint_items', 'deletion_log', 'consumption_log',
1183:   'eval_shadow_comparisons',
1184: ]);
1185: 
1186: function validateTableName(tableName: string): void {
1187:   if (!ALLOWED_TABLE_NAMES.has(tableName)) {
1188:     throw new Error(`[checkpoints] Table name "${tableName}" not in allowlist`);
1189:   }
1190: }

1196:       .map((column) => column.name)
1197:       .filter((name) => typeof name === 'string' && name.length > 0);
1198:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1199:     return [];
1200:   }

1204:   database: Database.Database,

1211: function toBuffer(value: unknown): Buffer | null {
1212:   if (Buffer.isBuffer(value)) return value;
1213:   if (value && typeof value === 'object') {
1214:     const maybeSerialized = value as { type?: unknown; data?: unknown };
1215:     if (
1216:       maybeSerialized.type === 'Buffer' &&
1217:       Array.isArray(maybeSerialized.data) &&
1218:       maybeSerialized.data.every((entry) => typeof entry === 'number')
1219:     ) {
1220:       return Buffer.from(maybeSerialized.data);
1221:     }
1222:   }
1223:   return null;
1224: }
1225: 
1226: function deserializeSnapshotValue(value: unknown): unknown {
1227:   if (value === undefined) {
1228:     return null;
1229:   }
1230:   return toBuffer(value) ?? value;
1231: }
1232: 
1233: function getMemoryIds(memories: Array<Record<string, unknown>>): number[] {
1234:   const ids = new Set<number>();
1235:   for (const memory of memories) {
1236:     if (!memory || typeof memory !== 'object') {
1237:       continue;
1238:     }
1239:     const rawId = memory.id;
1240:     if (typeof rawId === 'number' && Number.isSafeInteger(rawId)) {
1241:       ids.add(rawId);
1242:       continue;
1243:     }
1244:     if (typeof rawId === 'string' && /^\d+$/.test(rawId)) {
1245:       ids.add(Number.parseInt(rawId, 10));
1246:     }
1247:   }
1248:   return Array.from(ids);
1249: }
1250: 
1251: function parseCheckpointMetadata(value: unknown): Record<string, unknown> {
1252:   if (!value) {
1253:     return {};
1254:   }
1255:   if (typeof value === 'string') {
1256:     try {
1257:       const parsed = JSON.parse(value) as unknown;
1258:       return parsed && typeof parsed === 'object' && !Array.isArray(parsed)
1259:         ? parsed as Record<string, unknown>
1260:         : {};
1261:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
1262:       return {};
1263:     }
1264:   }
1265:   return typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value)
1266:     ? value as Record<string, unknown>
1267:     : {};
1268: }
1269: 
1270: function checkpointMetadataMatchesScope(rawMetadata: unknown, scope: ScopeContext): boolean {
1271:   const normalizedScope = normalizeScopeContext(scope);
1272:   if (!hasScopeConstraints(normalizedScope)) {
1273:     return true;
1274:   }
1275: 
1276:   const metadata = parseCheckpointMetadata(rawMetadata);
1277:   return (
1278:     (normalizedScope.tenantId === undefined || metadata.tenantId === normalizedScope.tenantId)
1279:     && (normalizedScope.userId === undefined || metadata.userId === normalizedScope.userId)
1280:     && (normalizedScope.agentId === undefined || metadata.agentId === normalizedScope.agentId)
1281:   );
1282: }
1283: 
1284: function hasDirectScopeColumns(columns: ReadonlySet<string>): boolean {
1285:   return (
1286:     columns.has('tenant_id')
1287:     || columns.has('user_id')
1288:     || columns.has('agent_id')
1289:     || columns.has('session_id')
1290:   );
1291: }
1292: 
1293: function getScopeFilterContext(
1294:   _database: Database.Database,
1295:   scope: ScopeContext = {},
1296: ): {
1297:   normalizedScope: ScopeContext;
1298:   predicate: ((row: Record<string, unknown>) => boolean) | null;
1299: } {
1300:   const normalizedScope = normalizeScopeContext(scope);
1301:   if (!hasScopeConstraints(normalizedScope)) {
1302:     return {
1303:       normalizedScope,
1304:       predicate: null,
1305:     };
1306:   }
1307: 
1308:   return {
1309:     normalizedScope,
1310:     predicate: createScopeFilterPredicate<Record<string, unknown>>(normalizedScope),
1311:   };
1312: }

1314: function getScopedMemories(
1315:   database: Database.Database,
1316:   specFolder: string | null,
1317:   scope: ScopeContext = {},
1318: ): {
1319:   memories: Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
1320:   memoryIds: number[];
1321:   normalizedScope: ScopeContext;
1322: } {
1323:   const { normalizedScope, predicate } = getScopeFilterContext(database, scope);
1324:   const baseMemories = specFolder
1325:     ? database.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index WHERE spec_folder = ?').all(specFolder) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>
1326:     : database.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index').all() as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
1327:   const memories = predicate ? baseMemories.filter((row) => predicate(row)) : baseMemories;
1328: 
1329:   return {
1330:     memories,
1331:     memoryIds: getMemoryIds(memories),
1332:     normalizedScope,
1333:   };
1334: }

1336: function getCurrentScopedMemoryIds(
1337:   database: Database.Database,
1338:   specFolder: string | null,
1339:   scope: ScopeContext = {},
1340: ): number[] {
1341:   return getScopedMemories(database, specFolder, scope).memoryIds;

1344: function buildRestoreScopeDeleteWhere(
1345:   columns: ReadonlySet<string>,
1346:   checkpointSpecFolder: string | null,
1347:   scope: ScopeContext = {},
1348: ): {
1349:   clauses: string[];
1350:   params: string[];
1351: } {
1352:   const normalizedScope = normalizeScopeContext(scope);
1353:   const clauses: string[] = [];
1354:   const params: string[] = [];
1355: 
1356:   if (checkpointSpecFolder && columns.has('spec_folder')) {
1357:     clauses.push('spec_folder = ?');
1358:     params.push(checkpointSpecFolder);
1359:   }
1360:   if (normalizedScope.tenantId && columns.has('tenant_id')) {
1361:     clauses.push('tenant_id = ?');
1362:     params.push(normalizedScope.tenantId);
1363:   }
1364:   if (normalizedScope.userId && columns.has('user_id')) {
1365:     clauses.push('user_id = ?');
1366:     params.push(normalizedScope.userId);
1367:   }
1368:   if (normalizedScope.agentId && columns.has('agent_id')) {
1369:     clauses.push('agent_id = ?');
1370:     params.push(normalizedScope.agentId);
1371:   }
1372: 
1373:   return { clauses, params };
1374: }

1376: function getEdgeIds(edges: Array<Record<string, unknown>>): number[] {
1377:   const ids = new Set<number>();
1378:   for (const edge of edges) {
1379:     const rawId = edge?.id;
1380:     if (typeof rawId === 'number' && Number.isSafeInteger(rawId)) {
1381:       ids.add(rawId);
1382:       continue;
1383:     }
1384:     if (typeof rawId === 'string' && /^\d+$/.test(rawId)) {
1385:       ids.add(Number.parseInt(rawId, 10));
1386:     }
1387:   }
1388:   return Array.from(ids);
1389: }

1391: function deleteRowsByIds(
1392:   database: Database.Database,
1393:   tableName: string,
1394:   columnName: string,
1395:   ids: number[],
1396: ): void {
1397:   if (ids.length === 0) {
1398:     return;
1399:   }
1400: 
1401:   const sql = `DELETE FROM ${tableName} WHERE ${columnName} IN (__PLACEHOLDERS__)`;
1402:   for (let i = 0; i < ids.length; i += 500) {
1403:     const batch = ids.slice(i, i + 500);
1404:     const placeholders = batch.map(() => '?').join(', ');
1405:     database.prepare(sql.replace('__PLACEHOLDERS__', placeholders)).run(...batch);
1406:   }
1407: }

1409: function snapshotCausalEdgesForMemoryIds(
1410:   database: Database.Database,
1411:   memoryIds: number[],
1412: ): Array<Record<string, unknown>> {
1413:   if (memoryIds.length === 0 || !tableExists(database, 'causal_edges')) {
1414:     return [];
1415:   }
1416: 
1417:   try {
1418:     const edgeIds = memoryIds.map((id) => String(id));
1419:     return dedupeRowsById([
1420:       ...batchedInQuery<Record<string, unknown>>(
1421:         database,
1422:         `
